### S6 ‚Äî Relation R: `work_category_material_categories` bridge + seed (spec 227) ‚Äî ‚úÖ COMPLETE (2026-06-30)

Migration `20260813033000_spec227_work_material_relation.sql`. The additive M2M bridge on the
GLOBAL library (defect **D5**) connecting the WORK axis (`work_categories`, S5) to the MATERIAL
axis (`catalog_categories`, spec 221): `id` uuid PK, `work_category_id` ‚Üí `work_categories(id)`
**ON DELETE CASCADE**, `category_id` ‚Üí `catalog_categories(id)` **ON DELETE CASCADE**, nullable
`kind_filter catalog_item_kind` (NULL = no kind filter), `created_by`, `created_at`. RLS = enable

- revoke anon/authenticated + grant SELECT to authenticated + `using (true)` policy; **no direct
  write grant** (RPC-sole-writer). Writes via two null-safe SECURITY DEFINER RPCs
  `add_/remove_work_category_material_category(p_work_category_id, p_category_id, p_kind_filter
default null)`. Read resolver `src/lib/catalog/scoped-categories.ts` ‚Üí
  `resolveScopedCategories(supabase, workCategoryId)` returns `[{categoryId, kindFilter}]`, EMPTY for
  an unmapped work-category (the pickers' show-all fallback). This is the **last schema unit** before
  the code-only scoped pickers (S7/S8).

**Decisions made:**

- **Seed grain = W-TOP** (`work_categories.code` length 3, W01‚ÄìW09), NOT the W0101 subsections. The
  BuildAll BOQ items aren't loaded into `catalog_items` (different grain, ADR 0066 ¬ß10.6), so a
  per-item material-category join is unavailable; the honest, reproducible signal is "which of the
  13 managed `catalog_categories` does a TOP work-category's BOQ items fall under". Pinned in the
  migration comment. Source = `boq_work_axis.csv` (S0 scratchpad), cross-read against LIVE
  `catalog_categories`, parsed programmatically (RFC4180 CSV parser; no hand-transcription of Thai).
- **19 pairs across 8 of 9 top categories**, looked up by stable `code` (no hard-coded uuids),
  `on conflict do nothing` (idempotent). W01‚Üí{01,03}; W02‚Üí{01,08}; W03‚Üí{02,04,05,07,08}; W04‚Üí{02,12};
  W05‚Üí{06}; W06‚Üí{01,08}; W08‚Üí{01,02,06,08}; W09‚Üí{01}.
- **W07 ý∏õýπâý∏≤ý∏¢ (signage) INTENTIONALLY UNSEEDED** ‚Äî vinyl/stainless sign boxes fit no material
  category among the 13 ‚Äî so it is the clean **empty-Relation-R anchor** the resolver/picker
  show-all fallback is tested against (ADR 0066 ¬ß10.1 adoption-cliff caveat).
- **RPC role gate = `pm/super/procurement/director` (4 roles, INCLUDES procurement)** ‚Äî per spec 227
  ¬ßRPC posture / ADR 0066 D8's catalog-side list. This differs from S5's 3-role work-library gate:
  Relation R is firm-wide MATERIAL curation, so procurement is admitted (pinned by a `lives_ok`
  procurement-add assert).
- **NULL-`kind_filter` uniqueness via a PARTIAL INDEX PAIR**, not `coalesce(kind_filter::text,‚Ä¶)`:
  an enum‚Üítext cast is only STABLE, not IMMUTABLE, so it is rejected in an index expression
  (`42P17`). Pair: typed-kind rows unique on `(work_category_id, category_id, kind_filter)
WHERE kind_filter IS NOT NULL`; NULL-kind rows unique on `(work_category_id, category_id)
WHERE kind_filter IS NULL`. Together: a NULL-kind and a typed-kind row coexist; two NULL-kind (or
  two identical typed) rows collide (`23505`). All seed rows carry NULL `kind_filter` (no v1 kind
  narrowing). The remove RPC matches with the same `coalesce((kind_filter)::text,'')` in its WHERE
  (runtime-stable is fine; only index expressions require immutability).
- **Resolver = DIRECT lookup** (work_category_id ‚Üí its rows). A WP reconciled to a SUBSECTION
  (5-char code) resolves empty ‚Üí picker shows-all. Subsection-grain rows / prefix-climbing are a
  future unit (out of scope; CLAUDE.md scope discipline).

pgTAP `241-spec227-work-material-relation.test.sql` (52 asserts: table+RLS+grant+no-write,
FK validity + ON DELETE CASCADE (both), nullable+typed `kind_filter`, seed count/span/W07-empty/
anchors, resolver mapped-vs-empty contract, uniqueness incl. NULL-kind coexistence + dup, 2√ó DEFINER
+anon-revoke posture, behaviour + 23505/22023/42501 + procurement-admit + null-safe + visitor deny).
Vitest `catalog-scoped-categories.test.ts` (4: mapped rows, kind_filter preserved, empty array for
unmapped, empty for null data). Full pgTAP suite + `lint`¬∑`typecheck`¬∑`vitest` green.

**Open questions (S6):** seed is W-TOP grain only ‚Äî subsection-reconciled WPs resolve empty (show-all)
until a later unit adds subsection rows or prefix-climbing. `kind_filter` is unused in v1 (all NULL);
it exists for a future tool/equipment-narrowing relation (the UC2 picker, spec 229 + the equipment
family). The Relation-R **management UI** (link/unlink screen) is out of scope (RPCs exist; later
unit). Equipment-category side of Relation R (ADR 0066 D5 mentions `{material-cat, equipment-cat}`)
is deferred ‚Äî this unit ships the material side only, per spec 227's scope.

### S7 ‚Äî UC1: scoped supply-plan item picker (spec 228) ‚Äî ‚úÖ COMPLETE (2026-06-30)

**First CODE-ONLY unit** ‚Äî no migration, no schema lane. The supply-plan grid's item picker now
surfaces the materials a row's WP work-category actually buys (Relation R), **without ever hiding the
rest** (ADR 0066 **D8** / ¬ß10.1 ‚Äî the scope reorders/pre-filters, never gates).

**Helper API (the tested core):** `src/lib/catalog/scoped-picker.ts` ‚Üí
`scopeCatalogItems(items, membershipsByItem, scopedCategoryIds)` returns
`{ scoped, entries: [{ item, inScope }], inScopeCount }`. In-scope items (canonical‚à™secondary ‚à©
scope ‚âÝ ‚àÖ) come **first** (stable order), flagged; the rest follow, still present. **Show-all
fallback:** an empty/undefined `scopedCategoryIds` ‚Üí the full catalog in original order, `scoped:
false`, all `inScope: false`. A scope that matches **no** item ‚Üí `scoped: true` but `inScopeCount: 0`
and every item present (never an empty picker). Reuses the S4 SSOT `itemCategoryIds` (no re-rolled
union).

**How a row resolves its scope (server-side, page loader):** row WP ‚Üí `work_packages.category_id` ‚Üí
`project_categories.work_category_id` (spec 226 reconcile) ‚Üí the **S6 resolver**
`resolveScopedCategories(supabase, workCategoryId)` ‚Üí distinct material `category_id`s. Resolved once
**per DISTINCT work-category** (not per WP ‚Äî no N+1), fanned onto every WP into a
`wpScopedCategories: Record<wpId, string[]>`. Item memberships come from the **S4 loader**
`loadCatalogItemMemberships` (canonical + secondary). Both pass through `SupplyPlanManager` ‚Üí the
picker; the manager builds the membership Map **once** for all rows.

**Component:** `CatalogItemPicker` ‚Üí renamed **`ScopedCatalogItemPicker`** (spec 228 AC1; the PR +
self-purchase forms import the new name unchanged ‚Äî they pass no scope ‚Üí full catalog, behaviour
identical; PR-side scoping is **S8**). New optional props `scopedCategoryIds` + `membershipsByItem`.
When a scope is active it pre-filters to the in-scope items, flags each with a **"ý∏ïý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏±ý∏öý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô"** ‚úì, and
always shows a **"ýπÅý∏™ý∏îý∏áý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏´ý∏°ý∏î"** escape that reveals the full catalog (toggles to "ýπÄý∏âý∏ûý∏≤ý∏∞ý∏óý∏µýπàý∏ïý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏±ý∏öý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô").
A whole-project row (no WP), an uncategorised WP, or an empty Relation R ‚Üí no escape, full catalog.

**Tests (TDD, +14 ‚Üí vitest 2063):** `catalog-scoped-picker.test.ts` (6 ‚Äî fallback, reorder/flag,
multi-category, secondary-membership, no-match-shows-all); `catalog-item-picker.test.tsx` (+5 scope
cases incl. the mandated empty-scope‚Üífull-catalog + escape-reveals-hidden); `supply-plan-manager.test.tsx`
(+3 wiring ‚Äî WP‚Üíscope, whole-project, unmapped-WP). `lint`¬∑`typecheck`¬∑`vitest` all green; no `db:test`
(no schema).

**Reused / not re-rolled:** S6 `resolveScopedCategories`, S4 `loadCatalogItemMemberships` /
`membershipsByItem` / `itemCategoryIds`. **Deferred (NOT built here, per scope discipline):** the
UC2 WP-detail PR + stock-issue pickers + WorkCategoryBadge (**S8 / spec 229**); read-side
spend-by-category / worklist filter / catalog badges (**S9 / spec 230**); `kind_filter` narrowing
(tools-vs-materials ‚Äî the supply-plan item shape carries no `kind`, so S7 scopes by category only;
lands with the equipment-family pickers in S8/S9).

**Open questions (S7):** subsection-reconciled WPs still resolve empty (the S6 W-TOP seed grain) ‚Üí
show-all, which is correct-but-broad until a later unit adds subsection rows / prefix-climbing. The
‚úì "ý∏ïý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏±ý∏öý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô" string + "ýπÅý∏™ý∏îý∏áý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏´ý∏°ý∏î"/"ýπÄý∏âý∏ûý∏≤ý∏∞ý∏óý∏µýπàý∏ïý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏±ý∏öý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô" escape labels are used once each (inline
Thai, no `labels.ts` SSOT needed yet ‚Äî the SSOT rule triggers at 2+ call sites; S8 will reuse the
picker and may promote them).

---

### S8 ‚Äî UC2: scoped WP-detail pickers + WorkCategoryBadge (spec 229) ‚Äî ‚úÖ COMPLETE (2026-06-30)

**Second CODE-ONLY unit** ‚Äî no migration, no schema lane. The WP-detail screen now (a) shows the WP's
bound **ý∏´ý∏°ý∏ßý∏îý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô** as a header badge, (b) scopes the **ý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ (PR) item picker** to the WP's
work-category, and (c) scopes the **ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å (issue-stock) on-hand `<select>`** the same way ‚Äî both with the
same D8 **never-hide** discipline (the scope reorders/pre-filters, an escape/fallback always reaches the
full set).

**The call sites (all in `‚Ä¶/work-packages/[workPackageId]/page.tsx`, one loader):**

- **PR picker** ‚Äî `PurchaseRequestForm` gained optional `scopedCategoryIds` + `membershipsByItem`,
  threaded straight to the already-shipped `ScopedCatalogItemPicker` (S7). **Category-only** scope
  (spec 229 AC2 ‚Äî no `kind` on the catalog-item shape), reusing the S7 `scopeCatalogItems` helper
  untouched: in-scope first + ‚úì "ý∏ïý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏±ý∏öý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô", **"ýπÅý∏™ý∏îý∏áý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏´ý∏°ý∏î"** escape, empty scope ‚Üí full catalog.
- **ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å picker** ‚Äî the spec's **caveat**: this is a native `<select>` over an `onHand WpStockRow[]`
  list (NOT the catalog bottom-sheet), so it is NOT swapped. `WpStockRow` gained `categoryId` + `kind`;
  a **new kind-aware helper** `scopeStockRows(rows, membershipsByItem, relation)` (+ predicate
  `itemInRelationScope`) in `scoped-picker.ts` partitions the on-hand list against Relation R's
  **`(category_id, kind_filter)`** rows. When a scope matches, the select renders **two `<optgroup>`s**
  ‚Äî `ý∏ïý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏±ý∏öý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô` (in-scope) then `ý∏ßý∏±ý∏™ý∏îý∏∏ý∏≠ý∏∑ýπàý∏ôýπÉý∏ôý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á` (rest) ‚Äî so the relevant stock surfaces first while
  **every item stays selectable**. Empty/unmapped relation ‚Üí the unchanged flat list.
- **Badge** ‚Äî new reusable `WorkCategoryBadge` (`components/features/work-packages/work-category-badge.tsx`),
  in the header under the WP name. Shows `ý∏´ý∏°ý∏ßý∏îý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô ¬∑ <name>` or the muted **"ý∏¢ý∏±ý∏áýπÑý∏°ýπàý∏£ý∏∞ý∏öý∏∏ý∏´ý∏°ý∏ßý∏îý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô"** nudge.

**How the WP resolves its work-category (server, page loader):** `wp.category_id` ‚Üí
`project_categories (name, work_category_id)` (the spec-226 reconcile, **membership-readable** so it
works for every WP_DETAIL_ROLES viewer, incl. site_admin/procurement) ‚Üí name = the badge; the
`work_category_id` ‚Üí **S6 resolver** `resolveScopedCategories` ‚Üí Relation R rows. Resolved once for the
single WP (no N+1). Item memberships from the **S4 loader** `loadCatalogItemMemberships` ‚Üí
`membershipsByItem` map, shared by both pickers. The on-hand read gained `category_id, kind` on the
embedded `catalog_items`.

**kind_filter (S8's new wrinkle vs S7):** applies **only** on the ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å side (spec 229 AC3). A Relation R
row `(category, kindFilter)` matches an on-hand item when the category is in the item's
canonical‚à™secondary set **and** `kindFilter` is null (any kind) or equals the item's `kind` ‚Äî so a
`kind_filter='tool'` relation surfaces tools and not the materials in the same category, while still
never hiding them.

**SSOT promotion ([[ui-term-consistency-ssot]]):** the ‚úì relevance string **"ý∏ïý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏±ý∏öý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô"** now hits 2
call sites (PR picker + the ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å optgroup) ‚Üí promoted to `WORK_CATEGORY_MATCH_LABEL` in `labels.ts`; the
uncategorised nudge **"ý∏¢ý∏±ý∏áýπÑý∏°ýπàý∏£ý∏∞ý∏öý∏∏ý∏´ý∏°ý∏ßý∏îý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô"** likewise (badge + `WpCategoryControl`) ‚Üí
`WORK_CATEGORY_UNSET_LABEL`. The PR picker's "ýπÅý∏™ý∏îý∏áý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏´ý∏°ý∏î"/"ýπÄý∏âý∏ûý∏≤ý∏∞ý∏óý∏µýπàý∏ïý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏±ý∏öý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô" stay inline (1 site).

**Tests (TDD, failing-first; +15 ‚Üí vitest 2099):** `catalog-scoped-picker.test.ts` (+`scopeStockRows`
/`itemInRelationScope` ‚Äî empty fallback, category-only, **kind tool-vs-material separation**,
secondary-membership, no-match-shows-all); `work-category-badge.test.tsx` (name + nudge);
`wp-issue-stock.test.tsx` (+optgroup-surfaces-first-never-hides, kind separation, flat-when-unmapped);
`purchase-request-form-catalog.test.tsx` (+scope surfaces-first + ýπÅý∏™ý∏îý∏áý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏´ý∏°ý∏î escape + empty‚Üífull).
`lint`¬∑`typecheck`¬∑`vitest` all green; no `db:test` (no schema).

**Reused / not re-rolled:** S7 `ScopedCatalogItemPicker` + `scopeCatalogItems`; S6 `resolveScopedCategories`;
S4 `loadCatalogItemMemberships`/`membershipsByItem`/`itemCategoryIds`. **Deferred (NOT built, scope
discipline):** read-side spend-by-category / worklist filter / catalog badges (**S9 / spec 230**); the
self-purchase section is left unscoped (not named by spec 229); Relation-management UI.

**Open questions (S8):** same S6 W-TOP seed-grain limitation as S7 ‚Äî a subsection-reconciled WP resolves
empty ‚Üí show-all (correct-but-broad) until a later unit adds subsection rows / prefix-climbing. The ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å
scope reorders via `<optgroup>` (the native-select analog of the bottom-sheet's pre-filter+escape) rather
than removing rows ‚Äî the spec's "filter" intent realised as never-hide partitioning per D8 / ¬ß10.1.

### S9 ‚Äî Read-side wins: spend-by-ý∏´ý∏°ý∏ßý∏îý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô card + procurement filter + catalog badge (spec 230) ‚Äî ‚úÖ COMPLETE (2026-06-30)

**Third CODE-ONLY unit** ‚Äî no migration, no schema lane, no danger-path. Three DISJOINT read surfaces, each
read-only + show-all-safe (opt-in facets, never hide rows by default per ADR 0066 ¬ß10.1 / D8). Resolved the
S0 open question (3): **the dashboard uses the WORK-category (ý∏´ý∏°ý∏ßý∏îý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô) lens; the procurement grid + catalog
use the MATERIAL category** ‚Äî coherent with each surface's axis (the dashboard reads money per WP‚Üíwork-cat;
procurement/catalog deal in the catalog item's material category). Shipped as **one combined PR** (disjoint
files, all green together, one review/CI cycle; the diff is self-evidently 3 units).

**(i) Dashboard spend-by-ý∏´ý∏°ý∏ßý∏îý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô card (`/dashboard`).** New pure helper `spendByWorkCategory(atoms, nameById,
unsetLabel)` in `src/lib/dashboard/spend.ts` partitions the SAME ýπÉý∏äýπâý∏àý∏£ý∏¥ý∏áý∏£ý∏ßý∏° the cards already compute ‚Äî each
WP-level spend atom (labor + WP materials + ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å ‚àí returns, the existing per-project building blocks, reused
**per-WP**) is tagged with its work-category; the project store pool (no WP) + uncategorised WPs fold into a
single unset bucket. **Œ£ rows ‚â° the existing portfolio total ‚Üí a true partition, no double-count** (a return
is a NEGATIVE atom, mirroring `wp_profit`'s netting ‚Äî same discipline as [[prc-ops-dashboard-spend-model]]).
The page loader (`src/app/dashboard/page.tsx`) resolves `wp.category_id ‚Üí project_categories.work_category_id
‚Üí work_categories.name_th` (global name so categories aggregate across projects), and reads `work_package_id`
on the issues/returns admin queries to tag those atoms. Card shown only once ‚â•1 WP carries a work-category
(pre-adoption every baht would sit in the unset bucket ‚Äî no signal). Money stays the `ink` hue (never `done`).

**(ii) Procurement grid material-category facet + badge (`procurement-grid.tsx`).** New pure helper pair
`src/lib/purchasing/category-facet.ts` ‚Äî `buildCategoryFacets(records, unsetLabel)` (chip counts over the
FULL set so they don't shift as the user filters; unset bucket last) + `recordMatchesCategory(record,
selected)` (ALL = show-all default). The grid grew an opt-in `RadioChip` row (`ý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏´ý∏°ý∏î (N)` + a chip per
present category + `ýπÑý∏°ýπàý∏£ý∏∞ý∏öý∏∏ý∏´ý∏°ý∏ßý∏îý∏´ý∏°ý∏πýπà`) that narrows ROWS only (`visibleGroups`, empty bands dropped; drawer
prev/next follows the visible order) and a per-row category badge. The PR resolves its material category in
the page loader (`src/app/requests/page.tsx`, procurement-only): `purchase_requests.catalog_item_id ‚Üí
catalog_items.category_id ‚Üí catalog_categories.name` (a targeted read ‚Äî `catalog_item_id` is NOT in the shared
`PR_LIST_COLUMNS` SSOT, so fetched separately rather than widen it for the detail page that doesn't need it).

**(iii) Catalog row material-category badge (`catalog-list.tsx`).** Each item row carries a muted category
chip, name resolved from the existing `categories` prop (sourced from `loadCatalogCategories` ‚Äî reuse, no
re-roll), null/unresolved ‚Üí no badge (never a raw uuid). The existing section-header test was tightened to
target the heading role (the category name now also appears as a row badge, so a bare `getByText` would be
ambiguous).

**No reverse Relation-R lookup was needed.** The orchestration flagged a possible material-cat‚Üíwork-cat
reverse helper; spec 230 resolves D2/D3 by the catalog item's OWN managed material category (the populated,
useful axis for procurement ‚Äî PRs are store-bound since spec 208, so a work-cat would mostly be absent), so
no new reverse helper was built. (Logged here per scope discipline ‚Äî the spec, not the orchestration, is the
contract.)

**Reused / not re-rolled:** the spend model + its tested primitives (`sumMaterials`/`sumStoreIssues`/
`sumStoreReturns`/`sumStorePool`/`aggregateLaborCost`); `loadCatalogCategories`/`categoryNameById` (S4/221
SSOT); `WORK_CATEGORY_UNSET_LABEL` (S8 labels SSOT); `RadioChip`. **NOT reused:** the S8 `WorkCategoryBadge`
‚Äî it prefixes `ý∏´ý∏°ý∏ßý∏îý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô` (a WORK category), wrong for a MATERIAL-category chip on catalog/procurement rows.

**Tests (TDD, failing-first; +19 ‚Üí vitest 2129):** `dashboard-spend.test.ts` (+`spendByWorkCategory`: the
partition-sums-to-total invariant, return-netting, unknown-id folds to unset, unset-sorts-last, zero-drop);
`category-facet.test.ts` (full-dataset counts, unset omitted/last, name tie-break, match predicate);
`procurement-grid-category.test.tsx` (per-row badge, facet chips, show-all default, filter-rows-not-counts,
unset bucket); `catalog-list.test.tsx` (+per-row badge inside a row). `lint`¬∑`typecheck`¬∑`vitest` green; no
`db:test` (no schema). **Adversarial review (cavecrew-reviewer) fixed one finding pre-ship:** the facet
builder could surface a raw-uuid chip for a deactivated category (id resolves, name doesn't ‚Äî active-only
`loadCatalogCategories`); now folds id-without-name into the unset bucket (helper + the page loader keep
`category_id`/`category_name` consistent so the chip count and the row filter agree).

**Open questions (S9):** the D3 catalog badge is somewhat redundant with the existing per-category section
headers (rows are already grouped under a category heading) ‚Äî implemented per the spec's explicit DoD, kept
visually muted; a future cleanup could drop the section headers in favour of the row badge, or vice-versa.
The dashboard card's per-WP atom re-aggregation assumes labor supersession never crosses WPs (true today ‚Äî
a labor log belongs to one WP); if that ever changes, the per-WP labor sum could diverge from the project sum.

### S10-U1 ‚Äî BOQ estimate core: `boq_template` + `boq_line` + 6 RPCs (spec 236) ‚Äî üîî HELD PR (2026-06-30)

First build sub-unit of the S10 estimate/template/bid epic (the 231 decomposition: U1 schema core ‚Üí U2 UI ‚Üí
U3/U4 assemblies ‚Üí U5 bid/freeze ‚Üí U6 wp_templates). Operator greenlit D1/D2/D3. Migration
`20260813040000_spec236_boq_template_line.sql` (applied to the shared DB; main‚ÜîDB sync moves to `040000`
once the held PR merges). Two new enums `boq_line_status` (draft/frozen/superseded) + `boq_variation_type`
(standard/added/omitted/provisional_sum) ‚Äî created inline (new `CREATE TYPE`, not an `ALTER TYPE ADD VALUE`).
`boq_template` (firm-wide reusable header, D3 ‚Äî stable unique `code`, deactivate-not-delete via `is_active`)

- `boq_line` with **rates ON THE LINE** (`material_rate` + `labor_rate`, D6). RLS + grant-select-to-
  authenticated + `using(true)`, no write/delete grant; 6 null-safe SECURITY DEFINER RPCs
  (create/update/set_active template + add/update/remove line), role = pm/super/procurement/director.

**Decisions made (operator-locked):**

- **D1 ‚Äî `boq_line.catalog_item_id` NULLABLE + required free-text `description`.** BOQ lines mostly aren't
  catalog items (ADR ¬ß10.6); the catalog link is optional enrichment, the human label mandatory. pgTAP pins
  `col_is_null(catalog_item_id)` + `col_not_null(description)` + a free-text add returning a NULL item.
- **D2 ‚Äî status enums + `exclusivity_group`** as above; new lines default `line_status=draft` /
  `variation_type=standard` (behaviourally pinned). The draft‚Üífrozen transition + bids are **S10-U5** (the
  `line_status` column exists; nothing in U1 drives it past draft).
- **D3 ‚Äî `boq_template` firm-wide reusable.** Instantiation-per-project via a clone is a later unit.
- **THREE-GRAIN INVARIANT pgTAP-pinned (D6 headline):** `boq_line` HAS material_rate/labor_rate;
  `catalog_items` has NEITHER (price-free); `supply_plan_lines` has NEITHER + keeps `qty` (qty-only).
- **Role gate = pm/super/procurement/director** (the catalog/material-side set per ADR D8 ‚Äî estimating is
  procurement-adjacent, matching Relation R / spec 227); null-safe (`v_role IS NULL OR NOT IN (...)` ‚Üí 42501).
  FK existence pre-checked in the RPCs so a bad catalog/work/template id is a friendly `22023`, not a raw
  `23503`. Errcodes 42501/22023/23505.

**Verification:** pgTAP `245-spec236-boq-core.test.sql` (99 asserts: structure + RLS/grant, the 3-grain
invariant, all 6 RPCs' DEFINER+anon-revoke posture, behaviour incl. 23505/22023/42501 + procurement-admit +
null-safe deny). Full pgTAP suite **207 files / 3777 / 0 fails**. `lint`¬∑`typecheck`¬∑`vitest 2129` green
(no vitest delta ‚Äî pure schema). Adversarial review (cavecrew-reviewer) = no issues.

**Open questions (S10-U1):** (1) `boq_line` rates are readable by all authenticated (grant-select posture per
AC5 / spec 221 U2); if rate data should be money-read-scoped, that's a deliberate later refinement (bids, U5,
will need this thinking). (2) `qty > 0` enforced (omissions modelled via `variation_type=omitted`, not negative
qty). (3) no clone/instantiate RPC yet (D3 instantiation is U2/U6). (4) `sort_order` exists but no reorder RPC
(authoring UI concern, U2).

### S10-U2 ‚Äî BOQ estimate authoring UI (spec 237) ‚Äî ‚úÖ SHIPPED (2026-06-30)

Second sub-unit of the S10 epic: the authoring surface over the U1 RPCs. **CODE-ONLY** (no migration ‚Äî the
`boq_template`/`boq_line` tables + 6 RPCs already exist on this branch from U1), so ‚úÖ AUTO-MERGE posture.

- **Templates list + create** ‚Äî `/catalog/boq-templates` (a `/catalog` drill, `DetailHeader` back ‚Üí /catalog,
  `BottomTabBar`), gated `requireRole(BACK_OFFICE_ROLES)`. Active templates first, inactive muted; each row
  links to its detail + shows its line count + grand total. `AddBoqTemplate` sheet ‚Üí `create_boq_template`.
- **Template detail** ‚Äî `[templateId]/page.tsx` loads the template + lines + picker data; `notFound()` when the
  template is null. `BoqTemplateDetail` renders the header (code ¬∑ name ¬∑ active state) with rename
  (`update_boq_template`) + activate/deactivate (`set_boq_template_active`), the priced line table
  (description ¬∑ catalog-item-or-free-text ¬∑ work-cat ¬∑ qty√óunit ¬∑ material/labor rate ¬∑ computed **line
  total** ¬∑ variation/standard/exclusivity when set), and a **template grand total** (Œ£).
- **Add / edit / remove line** ‚Äî `BoqLineForm` (one form for both add `add_boq_line` and edit `update_boq_line`,
  prefilled) reuses the **S1** `catalog_units` picker (+ `UNIT_OTHER_VALUE` free-text escape), the **S7**
  `ScopedCatalogItemPicker` passed **NO scope ‚Üí the full catalog** (D1: a line need not be a catalog item),
  and an optional **S5** global `work_categories` `<select>`. Remove is confirm-gated (`remove_boq_line`).
- **Money** routes through `src/lib/format.ts` (`baht`) + a new pure `src/lib/boq/totals.ts`
  (`lineTotal` = `qty√ó(material+labor)`, `templateTotal` = Œ£, both `round2`). Terms in `labels.ts` (the
  `// Spec 237` block). The `/catalog` page links to the new screen next to ý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ý∏´ý∏°ý∏ßý∏îý∏´ý∏°ý∏πýπà.

**`'use client'` justification:** ONLY the three interactive forms (`add-boq-template`, `boq-line-form`,
`boq-template-detail`) ‚Äî each owns sheet/confirm open state, field inputs, a `useTransition` pending state, and
an inline post-submit error (transient client-only state). The two pages + the three `src/lib/boq/*` modules
are Server-side.

**Verification:** `lint`¬∑`typecheck` green; `vitest` green. New tests: `boq-totals` (6), `boq-actions` (15:
role-reject + RPC-arg-shape incl. the omit-empty-optional spread), `boq-line-form` (5: renders the S1/S5/S7
pickers + submits the typed payload). `nav-back-affordance` STATIC_DETAIL gains `catalog/boq-templates`
(anti-drift registry). No migration ‚Üí `db:test` unaffected.

**Open questions (S10-U2):** (1) the line picker is UNSCOPED (full catalog) per the spec ‚Äî scoping it by the
line's work-category via Relation R (S6) is logged as a nice-to-have that couples item-pick to work-cat-pick
ordering (deferred). (2) No line reorder control (the `sort_order` column exists; deferred per the spec). (3)
The spec's AC1 names `requireActionRole` for the server action; this unit uses `requireRole(BACK_OFFICE_ROLES)`
to match the sibling `src/app/catalog/actions.ts` (createCatalogItem) pattern exactly ‚Äî same role set, same
defense-in-depth posture, friendlier `requireRole` redirect on a non-curator. The DEFINER RPC gates again.

### S10-U3 ‚Äî Assemblies: `kind=assembly` + BOM + explode (spec 238) ‚Äî üîî HELD PR (2026-06-30)

Decision **D5 = explode COMPUTED-ON-READ** (resolver, no persisted explosion rows). Two migrations:
`20260813041000` (`alter type catalog_item_kind add value 'assembly'` ‚Äî its OWN migration: `ALTER TYPE ADD
VALUE` can't share a txn with code that uses the new label) + `20260813042000` (the BOM table + RPCs +
explode). Applied to the shared DB; main‚ÜîDB sync moves to `042000` once the held PR merges.

`catalog_assembly_components(id, assembly_id ‚Üí catalog_items ON DELETE CASCADE, component_item_id ‚Üí
catalog_items ON DELETE RESTRICT, qty_per numeric(14,4) >0, waste_factor numeric(6,4) >=0, created_by,
created_at)` + `cac_no_self_ref` CHECK (`assembly_id <> component_item_id`) + unique `(assembly_id,
component_item_id)` + both FK indexes. RLS + grant-select-to-authenticated + `using(true)`, no write/delete
grant. 3 null-safe SECURITY DEFINER RPCs `add/update/remove_assembly_component` (pm/super/procurement/director)
‚Äî `add_` rejects a non-assembly parent / self-ref / unknown assembly¬∑component / qty<=0 / negative waste with
`22023`, dup with native `23505`. **`explode_assembly(p_assembly_id, p_qty default 1)`** = a `stable` SQL
function, **SECURITY INVOKER** (reads via the caller's RLS), `grant execute to authenticated` + revoke
public/anon; returns `(component_item_id, qty_per, waste_factor, effective_qty)` with `effective_qty =
round(qty_per*(1+waste_factor)*p_qty, 4)`; no BOM ‚Üí zero rows.

**Decisions made:** D5 computed-on-read; explode is **single-level** (direct components) for v1; the
`assembly` enum label add necessitated an `assembly` entry in `ITEM_KIND_OPTION_LABEL` (`ý∏äý∏∏ý∏îý∏õý∏£ý∏∞ý∏Åý∏≠ý∏ö`) ‚Äî caught
by typecheck (`Record<catalog_item_kind, string>`). Role set = pm/super/procurement/director (catalog/material
side, ADR 0066 D8). Component FK is RESTRICT (an item used in a BOM can't be hard-deleted out from under it);
assembly FK is CASCADE.

**Verification:** pgTAP `246-spec238-boq-assemblies.test.sql` (45 asserts: enum label, table/RLS/grant/no-delete,
FKs, the 3 RPCs' DEFINER+anon-revoke posture + behaviour [22023/23505/42501, non-assembly-parent, self-ref,
explode arithmetic 3*1.1*2=6.6], procurement-admit, null-safe deny). Full pgTAP suite **208/3822/0** (one libuv
timer flake on the first run ‚Äî re-ran clean). `lint`¬∑`typecheck`¬∑`vitest 2157` green. Adversarial review
(cavecrew-reviewer) = no issues.

**Open questions (S10-U3):** (1) **nested explosion** (a component that is itself an assembly) is NOT handled ‚Äî
v1 explode is single-level; recursive expansion + cycle handling is a future unit. (2) explode is
computed-on-read (D5); persisting exploded rows into a boq/supply grain is deferred. (3) the BOM editor UI +
explode view is **S10-U4**. (4) `explode_assembly` reads via RLS (INVOKER) but the table policy is `using(true)`
firm-wide, so no per-project scoping (estimate library is firm-wide, matching the rest of the chain).

---

## Spec 221 ‚Äî Managed category taxonomy (enum ‚Üí table) + product-code derivation (2026-06-29)

Status: **U1 + U2 + U3 + U3b + U3c + U4 COMPLETE (2026-06-29).** The catalog item form + browse filter
run on `category_id` (the managed table); the item **product code is now composed from the taxonomy**
(category code + subcategory code + a typed sequence), not free-typed. `DROP TYPE` = deferred optional
cleanup ‚Äî **spec 221 functional units are done.** Spec: `221-managed-category-taxonomy.md`. Follows spec 219.

- **U4 ‚Äî product-code auto-derive on the item form (this unit, code-only).** `src/lib/catalog/validate.ts`
  gains `composeProductCode(catCode, subCode, tail)` + `productCodeTailLength(...)` (single-sourced). The
  form (`catalog-item-form.tsx`) derives the code **prefix** from the chosen category (+ subcategory)
  `.code` and shows it read-only; the user types only the **sequence tail** (4 digits with no subcategory,
  2 with one); `productCode = prefix + tail` (still the stored 6-digit string, spec 214; empty tail = no
  code). On edit the tail is seeded from the existing code. **Adversarial 4-lens review (workflow) found +
  fixed two real issues:** (1) **data-integrity** ‚Äî recomposing on every save would silently rewrite a
  _divergent_ legacy code (stored prefix ‚âÝ category, which spec 214 permitted) even on a name-only edit;
  fixed with a `codeEdited` gate that **preserves the stored code until the user actually edits the
  sequence/taxonomy**, then recomposes. (2) **a11y** ‚Äî the derived-prefix badge is `aria-hidden`, so the
  field is linked to a hint (`aria-describedby="ci-code-hint"`) that **names the derived prefix** so a
  screen-reader user perceives the full code. TDD: `catalog-validate` (compose/tail-length),
  `catalog-product-code-derive` (prefix display, with/without subcategory, empty=optional, incomplete
  blocks submit, category-change resets, edit-seed, **divergent-preserve + recompose-on-edit + a11y
  link**); updated the add/edit form tests to the composed code. lint¬∑typecheck¬∑vitest **1963** green.
  **Open question (not a bug):** for a divergent legacy item the badge+tail visually show the _would-be_
  recomposed code while the preserved stored code differs until the user edits the tail ‚Äî accepted cosmetic
  edge (a real fix is a data audit of pre-scheme codes, out of scope for a code-only form unit). The
  subcategory-change tail reset is intentional + uncommented to the user (review nit, left as-is).

- **U3c ‚Äî item form + browse filter switched to `category_id` (this unit, code-only; held: db:types ‚Üí
  worker/).** `catalog-list.tsx` groups/filters by `category_id` with names from a `categories` prop (not
  the enum + `ITEM_CATEGORY_LABEL`); `catalog-item-form.tsx` picks the category from the managed
  `categories` (by id), cascading the subcategory by `categoryId`; `/catalog` loads `catalog_categories`
  - each item's `category_id` and threads `categories` through add/list/edit. `createCatalogItem` /
    `updateCatalogItem` take `categoryId` + look up `legacy_category` to write the enum through (keeps the
    13 displaying in the not-yet-switched adjacent readers; NULL for a user-category). `CatalogItem.category`
    ‚Üí `categoryId`; `CatalogSubcategoryOption.category` ‚Üí `categoryId`. Rewrote the 4 catalog test files to
    `categoryId` + a `categories` prop. lint¬∑typecheck¬∑vitest **1949** green. **Remaining (not blocking):**
    the adjacent readers (store / supply-plan / work-packages / review / picker) still read the vestigial
    enum (cast) ‚Äî a user-category item shows blank category there until they switch too (a cleanup unit).

- **U3b ‚Äî RPC `p_category` made OPTIONAL (this unit; SCHEMA, held PR).** Migration `20260813021000`:
  DROP+CREATE `create/update_catalog_item` + `create_catalog_subcategory` with `default`s added to the
  leading params (so `p_category` can be omitted) ‚Äî the param **types are unchanged**, so every
  `::regprocedure` pin + positional test call stays valid (no test churn). Bodies made null-safe
  (`coalesce(p_base_item,'')` / `p_unit`) so omitting a genuinely-required field still raises the friendly
  22023, not a 23502. Test-first pgTAP `223-spec221u3b` (p_category omitted ‚Üí item/subcategory created
  under a user-category by category_id; base_item omitted ‚Üí 22023). **db:test 192/192 (3334 asserts)**; no
  src/db:types change (deferred to U3c) ‚Üí CI green except the migration guard. Adversarial-review of the
  near-identical U2 bodies already done; this delta is defaults + coalesce only.

- **U3 ‚Äî taxonomy manage screen + main-category CRUD (this unit, code-only; held: db:types ‚Üí worker/).**
  `/catalog/subcategories` ‚Üí the **taxonomy manager** (`ý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ý∏´ý∏°ý∏ßý∏îý∏´ý∏°ý∏πýπà`): a MAIN-category section
  (`AddCategory`/`EditCategory`; code editable = recode, name, order, deactivate; wraps the U1
  `create/update_catalog_category` RPCs via new server actions) above the subcategory section.
  Regenerated `db:types` (category nullable + `p_category_id`); the ~8 nullable-`category` read sites
  (catalog/store/supply-plan/work-packages/review) got a safe non-null narrowing (data non-null until
  the item form switches). Term SSOT `CATALOG_CATEGORY_LABEL`/`MANAGE_TAXONOMY_LABEL`. Test-first
  `add-category` + `edit-category`. lint¬∑typecheck¬∑vitest **1949** green. **U3b deferred** (item
  form/filter switch to category_id; the `create_catalog_subcategory` p_category-optional RPC fix so a
  brand-new user-category can hold subcategories/items) ‚Äî noted in the spec.

- **U2 ‚Äî category_id source of truth (enum vestigial) (this unit, NOT break-glass).** Migration
  `20260813020000` (additive + backward-compatible): enum `category` columns ‚Üí nullable; subcategory
  identity + the item‚Üîsubcategory match move onto `category_id` (`unique(category_id, code)` +
  `unique(id, category_id)` + composite FK `catalog_items(subcategory_id, category_id)`); sync trigger
  ‚Üí only-fill-when-null; **DROP+CREATE** `create/update_catalog_item` + `create_catalog_subcategory`
  to add a trailing `p_category_id` (uuid) ‚Äî old enum-only calls still resolve (trailing default), so
  the live app keeps working; category_id is the source of truth (explicit wins, else derived); enum
  written through (NULL for a user-category); subcategory guard now on `category_id`. **Adversarially
  verified by a 5-lens workflow** before applying (DDL/ordering, backward-compat, trigger/data,
  RPC/grant, test-pins ‚Äî all clean; the migration is sound). Test-first: new pgTAP `222-spec221u2`
  (signatures + DEFINER + anon-revoke, explicit-category_id-wins, enum-only-resolves, sync no-overwrite,
  constraints, the category_id guard) + bumped `120/121` signature pins to 9-arg. **db:types + the
  consumer null/category_id switch = U3** (intentional defer ‚Äî keeps U2 a clean schema unit; no src
  change ‚Üí CI green). U3 carry-forwards (the demote-on-old-edit risk + the enum-keyed reads) noted in
  the spec. **Also fixed a latent bug from #165:** the spec220‚Üí221 renumber `sed 's/220/221/g'`
  corrupted `22023`‚Üí`22123` in `221-catalog-categories.test.sql` (shipped uncaught because the
  post-renumber db:test was skipped) ‚Äî restored to `22023`. db:test 191/191; lint¬∑typecheck green.

> ‚öÝÔ∏è **Spec-number note:** this work began as spec 220 and **migration `20260813018000`,
> collided with a concurrent session (G63 role-admin, also spec 220 / `018000`), and was
> renumbered to **spec 221** by operator decision. G63 kept spec 220 + renumbered its
> migration to `019000`. My `018000` was already applied + green, so it stays; the migration
> **file keeps the `spec220u1` name\*\* (matches the recorded DB version name). See LANES.md.

- **U1 ‚Äî additive foundation (this unit).** Migration `20260813018000` (additive, no drops):
  - `catalog_categories (id, code 2-digit unique, name, sort_order, is_active, + transient
`legacy_category` enum map)` + RLS (select to authenticated, RPC writes); **seed the 13**
    enum values (codes 01‚Äì13, names from `ITEM_CATEGORY_LABEL`).
  - `category_id uuid` (nullable FK) on `catalog_items` + `catalog_subcategories` + **backfill**
    from `legacy_category`; a `BEFORE INSERT/UPDATE` **sync trigger** keeps `category_id`
    current from the enum while the app still writes `category` (no drift pre-cutover).
  - `create_catalog_category` / `update_catalog_category` RPCs (back-office gate; code editable ‚Äî
    items key on `category_id`, not the code). pgTAP `221-catalog-categories` (18 asserts:
    structure, RLS/grants, seed=13, backfill, sync trigger, create/dup/bad-code/update). **`db:test`
    189/189 ¬∑ 3294 asserts ¬∑ 0 fails**; `db:types` regenerated. Held PR (migrations/).
- **U2 ‚Äî CUTOVER ‚öÝÔ∏è BREAK-GLASS (operator-gated, irreversible).** Drop the enum + old columns,
  rebuild the composite FK on `category_id`, swap the catalog RPCs + ~8 code files to
  `category_id` + table-sourced names. Needs `pg_dump` floor + preview rehearsal + operator go.
- **U3 ‚Äî unified taxonomy tree manager (code-only).** Main + sub in one screen.
- **U4 ‚Äî product-code auto-derive (code-only).** Form composes digits 1-4 from the taxonomy.

## Spec 219 ‚Äî Catalog subcategory taxonomy + 2-level filter (2026-06-28)

Status: **SHIPPED ‚Äî U1 (schema) + U2 (manage UI) + U3 (drill filter) all COMPLETE.**
Spec: `219-catalog-subcategory-taxonomy.md`. Operator asked to redesign the
ý∏óý∏∞ýπÄý∏öý∏µý∏¢ý∏ôý∏ßý∏±ý∏™ý∏îý∏∏ (`/catalog`) filter "uxui pro max" because the register has categories +
subcategories; chose to **model the subcategory as a real taxonomy table** (over a
labels-map / code-only shim), then chose to **build it now** (schema lane was free).

- **U1 ‚Äî schema + CRUD RPCs (this unit).** Migration `20260813015000`:
  - `public.catalog_subcategories (id, category item_category, code text 2-digit,
name, sort_order, is_active, created_at)`; unique `(category, code)` + unique
    `(id, category)`; RLS on, `select` to authenticated, no write policy (reference
    data ‚Äî written via RPC, the `catalog_items` posture). Seed one anchor:
    `(steel_fixing, '01', 'ý∏ßý∏±ý∏™ý∏îý∏∏ýπÇý∏Ñý∏£ý∏áý∏™ý∏£ýπâý∏≤ý∏á')`.
  - `catalog_items.subcategory_id uuid` nullable + **composite FK**
    `(subcategory_id, category) ‚Üí catalog_subcategories(id, category)` so an item's
    category must equal its subcategory's (nulls skip the check ‚Äî uncoded items OK).
  - RPCs (definer, back-office gate, revoke public/anon, grant authenticated):
    `create_catalog_subcategory` / `update_catalog_subcategory`; **DROP+CREATE**
    `create_catalog_item` (+`p_subcategory_id`, now 8-arg) and `update_catalog_item`
    (+`p_subcategory_id`, now 9-arg) with a category-match guard (‚Üí 22023). `product_code`
    stays the free spec-214 string.
  - **Test-first** `219-catalog-subcategory.test.sql` (21 asserts: structure, composite
    FK, RLS/grants, seed, both RPCs + the item-RPC category-match guard). Updated the
    signature **pins** in `120-create-catalog-item` / `121-edit-catalog-item` to the new
    arity (the DROP+CREATE moved them). `pnpm db:test` 188/188 files ¬∑ 3276 asserts ¬∑ 0
    fails; lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ vitest 1927 all green. `db:types` regenerated.
  - Note: branched off `origin/main` (which lacks the held `014000`); migration is
    `015000`, pushed to the shared DB (only `015000` applied ‚Äî `013000`/`014000` already
    present). Transient main-gap until #159 (`014000`) merges, per the operator's go.

- **U2 ‚Äî manage UI + cascading picker (this unit, code-only).**
  - New route `/catalog/subcategories` (a `/catalog` drill, `DetailHeader` back ‚Üí /catalog,
    `BACK_OFFICE_ROLES`): active subcategories grouped by main category; add / edit /
    deactivate. New `AddSubcategory` + `EditSubcategory` (`Subcategory` type) components.
  - `catalog/actions.ts` gains `createCatalogSubcategory` / `updateCatalogSubcategory`
    (wrap the U1 RPCs; map 23505 ‚Üí "ý∏£ý∏´ý∏±ý∏™ý∏´ý∏°ý∏ßý∏îý∏¢ýπàý∏≠ý∏¢ý∏ôý∏µýπâý∏ñý∏πý∏ÅýπÉý∏äýπâýπÅý∏•ýπâý∏ß", 42501, 22023). The item
    actions now thread `subcategoryId` ‚Üí `p_subcategory_id` (omit-when-empty spread so
    `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` is happy); 22023 disambiguated (subcategory vs unknown id).
  - `CatalogItemForm` gains a **cascading subcategory `<select>`** ‚Äî offered only for the
    chosen category's subcategories, reset on category change, optional (`‚Äî ýπÑý∏°ýπàý∏£ý∏∞ý∏öý∏∏ ‚Äî`).
    Threaded `subcategories` through page ‚Üí `AddCatalogItem` / `CatalogList` ‚Üí `EditCatalogItem`.
    `/catalog` loads `catalog_subcategories` + each item's `subcategory_id`, and links to the
    manage screen (`MANAGE_SUBCATEGORIES_LABEL`). Term SSOT `CATALOG_SUBCATEGORY_LABEL = "ý∏´ý∏°ý∏ßý∏îý∏¢ýπàý∏≠ý∏¢"`.
  - **Test-first**: `add-subcategory` / `edit-subcategory` tests + cascade test on
    `add-catalog-item` (scoped options) + `subcategoryId` added to the existing add/edit
    call-shape assertions + `nav-back-affordance` route registered. lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑
    vitest **1935** green. Code-only ‚Äî auto-merges on green CI.
- **U3 ‚Äî the 2-level drill filter (this unit, code-only, the original ask).** Rewrote
  `catalog-list.tsx` from the flat 13-chip cloud to a drill: a horizontal-scroll ý∏´ý∏°ý∏ßý∏îý∏´ý∏•ý∏±ý∏Å
  strip (single-select, counts) ‚Üí on category select a drill-in ý∏´ý∏°ý∏ßý∏îý∏¢ýπàý∏≠ý∏¢ strip reading the
  real `catalog_subcategories` names (counts) + a `ý∏¢ý∏±ý∏áýπÑý∏°ýπàý∏°ý∏µý∏´ý∏°ý∏ßý∏îý∏¢ýπàý∏≠ý∏¢` bucket for uncoded items
  ‚Üí a breadcrumb (`ý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏´ý∏°ý∏î ‚Ä∫ category ‚Ä∫ subcategory`, crumbs pop a level) ‚Üí results grouped by
  subcategory (vs by category at the top level). Search **overrides** the drill (flat,
  category-grouped). No schema, no new data ‚Äî the page already feeds `subcategories` +
  `subcategory_id` (U2). Test-first: 6 drill tests added to `catalog-list.test.tsx` (strip
  reveal, sub filter, uncoded bucket, breadcrumb reset); the existing 13 still pass (kept the
  RadioChip strips + grouped sections; the sub "all" chip is `ý∏óý∏∏ý∏Åý∏´ý∏°ý∏ßý∏îý∏¢ýπàý∏≠ý∏¢` to avoid a radio-name
  clash, breadcrumb reset is a `button`). lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ vitest **1940** green.

## Spec 218 ‚Äî SA rework clarity ("ý∏ïýπâý∏≠ý∏áýπÅý∏ÅýπâýπÑý∏Ç") (2026-06-28)

Status: **U1‚ÄìU4 in one code-only PR** (in-app clarity); **U5 notifications = separate
held PR** (danger-path). Spec: `218-sa-rework-worklist.md`. Operator: "there are WPs
that require rework but the SA workflow is unclear ‚Äî make it clear on SA pages (color,
notification)." Three "needs my fix" cases: defect-reopened (status=rework), PM ýπÉý∏´ýπâýπÅý∏ÅýπâýπÑý∏Ç
(needs_revision) ‚Äî incl. **"request for more photos"** ‚Äî and PM ýπÑý∏°ýπàý∏≠ý∏ôý∏∏ý∏°ý∏±ý∏ïý∏¥ (rejected).
Cases 2‚Äì3 stay `pending_approval` (the SA home excluded them, no notification ‚Üí broken
loop). Scope (operator): cover all three; cases 2‚Äì3 **display-only** (no status change ‚Äî
that's a separate decision, with FB2/#149).

- **U1 ‚Äî classifier.** Pure `buildSaActionList` (`src/lib/sa/action-list.ts`): rework |
  revision | rejected, ordered rejected‚Üírework‚Üírevision, rest = the everyday list.
- **U2 ‚Äî /sa "ý∏ïýπâý∏≠ý∏áýπÅý∏ÅýπâýπÑý∏Ç" section** pinned above ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ôý∏Çý∏≠ý∏áý∏âý∏±ý∏ô. The home now keeps
  `pending_approval` and surfaces those whose **latest decision** is negative
  (`getLatestDecisionsForWorkPackages`) + the reopen reason/source/round for rework.
  Color = severity (amber=fix, red=rejected), one-tap CTA to the photo capture.
- **U3 ‚Äî WP-detail CTAs.** The rework banner + the needs_revision/rejected attention
  card each get a Camera CTA (ý∏ñýπàý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏£ý∏πý∏õý∏´ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áýπÅý∏ÅýπâýπÑý∏Ç / ý∏ñýπàý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏£ý∏πý∏õýπÄý∏ûý∏¥ýπàý∏° ‚Üí #wp-photos), !readOnly.
- **U4 ‚Äî tab badge.** `SaActionBadge` on the SA's ý∏´ý∏ôýπâý∏≤ý∏´ý∏•ý∏±ý∏Å tab (count = rework +
  bounced), visible app-wide (mirrors PendingApprovalsBadge).

‚öÝÔ∏è Overlaps #149 (FB2 explicit-submit) on the WP-detail page.tsx ‚Äî additive CTAs vs
their submit button, neutral copy; resolve any trivial conflict at merge.
U5 (notifications on reopen + needs_revision/rejected via notification_outbox) follows.

## Spec 217 ‚Äî Rework source (ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏àý∏Ýý∏≤ý∏¢ýπÉý∏ô vs ý∏•ý∏πý∏Åý∏Ñýπâý∏≤ýπÅý∏àýπâý∏á) (2026-06-28)

Status: **U1‚ÄìU3 in one held PR** (migration + definer RPC ‚Üí danger-path; one-tap).
Spec: `217-rework-source.md`. Operator: two rework types ‚Äî internal call (our QA/SA) vs
client call (the client). Scope (operator-chosen): **record + display only**. Labels
(operator-chosen): internal ‚Üí ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏àý∏Ýý∏≤ý∏¢ýπÉý∏ô, client ‚Üí ý∏•ý∏πý∏Åý∏Ñýπâý∏≤ýπÅý∏àýπâý∏á.

- **U1 ‚Äî schema.** New enum `public.rework_source {internal, client}`. Migration
  `20260813009000`: DROP the 2-arg `reopen_work_package_for_defect`, CREATE 3-arg with
  `p_source default 'internal'` (from the LIVE 216 body ‚Äî project_director gate +
  rework_round increment kept), stamping `source` into the audit payload; re-REVOKE
  public/anon + GRANT authenticated. Default keeps the still-2-arg app call working
  until U2. pgTAP 75 += signature + default-internal + explicit-client (keyed on
  `round`, not time ‚Äî `now()` is fixed within a pgTAP txn so both reopen rows tie).
- **U2 ‚Äî write.** `REWORK_SOURCE_LABEL` (SSOT); `reportDefect` action takes + validates
  `source`, passes `p_source`; report-defect form gains a required ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏àý∏Ýý∏≤ý∏¢ýπÉý∏ô/ý∏•ý∏πý∏Åý∏Ñýπâý∏≤ýπÅý∏àýπâý∏á
  toggle (default internal).
- **U3 ‚Äî read + display.** `reworkSourcesFromAuditRows` (pure); `load-detail` returns
  `reworkSources` + `defectSource`; review page reads the same. Per-round heading ‚Üí
  "ý∏´ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áýπÅý∏ÅýπâýπÑý∏Ç ‚Äî ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏ö N ¬∑ <source>" (`afterFixRoundHeading` gains an optional source label;
  `reworkSourceLabel` helper); the rework banner shows "ý∏óý∏µýπàý∏°ý∏≤: <source>". Legacy reopens
  (no source) omit the label.

Open: behaviour per source (notify / warranty / SLA) ‚Äî out of scope (operator).

---

## Spec 216 ‚Äî Multi-rework rounds (2026-06-28)

Status: **U1 shipped as a held PR** (additive migration + RPC ‚Üí danger-path; one-tap merge).
Spec: `216-multi-rework-rounds.md`. Operator asked the system to support more than one
rework. The defect loop (spec 144) already repeats, but every round's after_fix (ý∏´ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áýπÅý∏ÅýπâýπÑý∏Ç,
spec 215) photos collided in one bucket and only the latest reason showed. Operator chose
(design question) **model A ‚Äî round counter** over a defect-cycle table or photo-only tagging.

- **U1 (this PR) ‚Äî schema + reopen increment.** Migration `20260813008000`: `rework_round`
  smallint (default 0) on `work_packages` + `photo_logs`; `reopen_work_package_for_defect`
  CREATE-OR-REPLACE'd from its LIVE body (project_director gate preserved) to increment the
  WP counter and stamp `round` into the audit payload. Applied to the shared DB; db:types
  regen'd. pgTAP 75 += round-1 / audit-round / second-reopen / round-2 (187/187, 0 fails).
  Test fixtures that build full `PhotoLogRow` literals gained `rework_round: 0`.
- **U2 (this PR) ‚Äî write side.** `addPhoto` selects `wp.rework_round` and stamps
  after_fix inserts with it (other phases ‚Üí 0) via the pure `photoReworkRoundFor`
  helper; `removePhoto` reads the target's round and `buildTombstoneRow` carries it so
  a removal stays in the same cycle. Unit-tested (photo-write-helpers). No schema change
  ‚Äî rides on U1's columns, so it folds into the same held PR (#145).
- **U3 (this PR) ‚Äî read-side helpers.** Pure + unit-tested: `groupAfterFixByRound`
  (after_fix rows ‚Üí per-round groups, ascending) and `reworkReasonsFromAuditRows`
  (the `wp_reopened_for_defect` audit rows ‚Üí round‚Üíreason map; audit_log SELECT is
  `using(true)` for authenticated). No page wiring yet ‚Äî U4 consumes these.
- **U4 (this PR) ‚Äî UI + #144 gate folded in.** PhotoCaptureZone gains `showAfterFix`
  (gate) + `currentReworkRound` ‚Äî the ý∏´ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áýπÅý∏ÅýπâýπÑý∏Ç tile surfaces only inside a rework
  cycle and is labelled "ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏ö N ¬∑ ‚Ä¶". Both galleries (review + read-only project) render
  the lifecycle phases, then one PhaseGallery per rework round
  (`groupAfterFixByRound`), headed "ý∏´ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áýπÅý∏ÅýπâýπÑý∏Ç ‚Äî ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏ö N" with that round's defect reason
  (`PhaseGallery` gains a `note` prop). `load-detail` returns `reworkReasons` (round ‚Üí
  reason from the reopen audit rows, one read); the review page reads the same. The
  "ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏ö N" tag + per-round heading are SSOT helpers (`reworkRoundTag` /
  `afterFixRoundHeading`; legacy round-0 shows the plain label). This supersedes PR
  #144 (its show-only-for-reworked gate is reimplemented here), so #144 is closed.

Open: per-round approval attribution; after-fix in the PDF report (both deferred).

---

## Spec 215 ‚Äî After-fix photos (ý∏´ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áýπÅý∏ÅýπâýπÑý∏Ç) (2026-06-28)

Status: **shipped as one held PR** (additive enum-add ‚Üí danger-path; one-tap merge). Spec: `215-after-fix-photos.md`. From feedback `0fa23307` (project_director, WP detail): capture completion photos when a WP's rework is done, distinct from the original ýπÅý∏•ýπâý∏ßýπÄý∏™ý∏£ýπáý∏à work photos. **Operator chose (via design question) the explicit new-phase option** over auto-distinguish / discoverability-only.

**Schema** (mig `20260813006000`, db:push'd + db:types'd, schema lane claimed‚Üíreleased): `alter type photo_phase add value 'after_fix' after 'after'` (own migration, the enum-add convention). **Totality updates** (same held PR ‚Äî depend on the regenerated enum): `PHOTO_PHASE_LABEL.after_fix = "ý∏´ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áýπÅý∏ÅýπâýπÑý∏Ç"`; `PHASES` += after_fix bucket; `CurrentPhotosByPhase`/`selectCurrentPhotosByPhase` += after_fix (load-detail adopts the canonical type + spreads it into `allPhotos`; both page count literals add the key); `PHOTO_PHASES` validation += after_fix; `shouldTransitionToPendingApproval` fires on after **or** after_fix (closes the rework loop); capture tile grid ‚Üí 2√ó2 with after_fix lock-free + never auto-"current"; pgTAP `09` enum pin ‚Üí four values. **after_fix is deliberately NOT in `PHASE_ORDER`** (the 3-step progress bar) ‚Äî it's a rework addendum, so a never-reworked WP's progress is unchanged. Test-first/after: phase-progress (ignores after_fix), current-photos (groups after_fix), photo-write-helpers (after_fix transition), photos-phases (4 buckets), capture tile renders + tappable (`phase-uploader-after-fix.test.tsx`). Lint + typecheck + full suite green.

**Deferred:** auto-pre-select the ý∏´ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áýπÅý∏ÅýπâýπÑý∏Ç tile when the WP is in rework (zero-tap); after-fix photos in the PDF report.

## Spec 214 ‚Äî Product code (ý∏£ý∏´ý∏±ý∏™ý∏™ý∏¥ý∏ôý∏Ñýπâý∏≤) (2026-06-28)

Status: **shipped as one held PR** (additive migration ‚Üí danger-path; one-tap merge). Spec: `214-product-code.md`. From feedback `dfd70375` (procurement): a structured 6-digit code per catalog item (main 2 + sub 2 + sequence 2), prefix-searchable. Operator already has a scheme (procurement owns it).

**Design (v1):** the code is a FREE 6-digit string (`^[0-9]{6}$`), not a modelled main/sub taxonomy ‚Äî the existing `item_category` is a flat 13-value enum, and the company taxonomy is procurement's business decision, not a schema lock. Manual assignment, no backfill (existing items stay NULL).

**Schema** (mig `20260813005000`, db:push'd + db:types'd, schema lane claimed): `catalog_items.product_code` (nullable) + format CHECK + partial-unique index (unique when set). DROP+CREATE `create_catalog_item`/`update_catalog_item` to add `p_product_code text default null` (default keeps existing named calls valid; full-replace on update ‚Äî the form always sends the field). pgTAP `214` (column/nullable/index + RPC stores code + bad format‚Üí22023 + dup‚Üí23505 + null allowed) all green; updated the signature pins in `120`/`121` (the "grep all pins" discipline).

**UI** (code-only, same held PR ‚Äî depends on the regenerated types): `src/lib/catalog/validate.ts` (`isValidProductCode`); `catalog-item-form.tsx` gains a 6-digit `ý∏£ý∏´ý∏±ý∏™ý∏™ý∏¥ý∏ôý∏Ñýπâý∏≤` field (segment hint + inline validity, digit-only input); `createCatalogItem`/`updateCatalogItem` thread `productCode` + disambiguate the 23505 (identity vs code); `catalog-list.tsx` shows the code as a mono chip AND gains a search box matching name/spec/code (type `0101` ‚Üí prefix filter) + a no-match state; `/catalog` page selects `product_code`. New `PRODUCT_CODE_LABEL`. Test-first-ish: `catalog-validate.test.ts` (5) + catalog-list display/search/no-match + add/edit flow updated to carry the code. Lint + typecheck + **1866** unit tests green.

**Deferred:** threading the code into the PR-creation picker (`PurchaseRequestCatalogItem` + loaders) ‚Äî a follow-up that avoids touching the PR flow in this PR. Modelled main/sub taxonomy + auto-derived/auto-sequence codes = a later spec.

**Pre-existing pgTAP failures (NOT from this change):** `104-settlement` errored on a transient `524` API timeout; `200-store-inventory-reconciliation` fails its table-wide GL-1500-vs-subledger tie (have 459,539 / want 443,139 = the documented ý∏ø16k unposted-equipment drift, [[spec208-store-centric-procurement]]). My branch touches no GL/on-hand/settlement files.

---

## Spec 213 ‚Äî Material logs (ý∏õý∏£ý∏∞ý∏ßý∏±ý∏ïý∏¥ý∏ßý∏±ý∏™ý∏îý∏∏), U1 (2026-06-28)

Status: **U1+U2+U3 shipped** (spec + assembly lib + page + drill-in, code-only, NO schema). Spec: `213-material-logs.md`. From feedback `15151fb3` (project_director, ý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á): wants each material's history + current status "like order tracking." Operator clarified: **"no need to be a dashboard, item-specific logs."**

**U2 (route + view):** `src/app/projects/[projectId]/store/items/[catalogItemId]/page.tsx` (`requireRole(WP_DETAIL_ROLES)`; project_id-pinned reads so a cross-project id or hidden project 404s; an item with no on-hand row AND no movements 404s). Six parallel RLS-scoped reads (project, catalog_item, on-hand, + the five movement tables with WP/supplier joins) ‚Üí `buildMaterialLog` ‚Üí `DetailHeader` (back to ý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á via `safeBackHref(from, storeHref)`) + a cost-side status card (on-hand qty + `ý∏ïýπâý∏ôý∏óý∏∏ý∏ôýπÄý∏âý∏•ý∏µýπàý∏¢` + total value, mirroring what the console already shows) + `MaterialLogView`. New presentational `material-log-view.tsx`: a newest-first timeline, per row an icon + reused Thai kind label (`STORE_RECEIVE_LABEL`/`STORE_ISSUE_LABEL`/`STOCK_COUNT_LABEL`/`STORE_RETURN_TO_STORE_LABEL`/`STORE_FIX_WRONG_ENTRY_LABEL`) + signed qty + cost (baht) + WP + count detail + note + running balance; empty state otherwise. New labels `MATERIAL_LOG_LABEL`/`STOCK_COUNT_LABEL` + nav SSOT `storeItemHref`. Test-first `material-log-view.test.tsx` (6).

**U3 (drill-in):** the store console on-hand row's identity+qty is now a `<Link>` to `storeItemHref(...)` (with `withBackFrom` so back returns to ý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á); the `ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏àý∏ôý∏±ý∏ö` action stays beside it, outside the anchor. Test-first: added a link assertion to `store-manager.test.tsx`.

Verify limit: the page is behind LINE auth + a project-with-stock, so not exercised in a live browser; the assembly lib + the view component are fully unit-tested (13 tests across the two files) and the row-link is asserted. Lint + typecheck + full suite green. **SPEC 213 COMPLETE (U1+U2+U3).**

**U1 (this unit):** the pure assembly `src/lib/store/material-log.ts` ‚Äî `buildMaterialLog(sources)` unions the five movement sources (receipts / issues / counts / returns / reversals) for one (project, item) into a newest-first `MaterialLogEntry[]` with a signed on-hand delta per kind (receipt/return `+`, issue `‚àí`, count `= variance`, reversal flips by which FK it undoes), the cost-side figure only (line cost / variance value / reversal value-delta ‚Äî never sell/margin, per the spec money rule), the WP for issue/return, and a running balance (ascending cumulative sum) whose newest row equals current on-hand. Stable tie-break (at ‚Üí createdAt ‚Üí id). No I/O ‚Äî the page (U2) does the RLS-scoped reads and maps rows into the inputs. Test-first `tests/unit/material-log.test.ts` (7: ordering, signed deltas incl. receipt-vs-issue reversal, balance invariant lands on 52, cost-side figures, tie-break, empty). Lint + typecheck green.

## Spec 211 ‚Äî Procurement terminology & level clarity, U1‚ÄìU7 + U10a/b/c/d + U6b + U11a + U11b (2026-06-27)

Status: **shipping U1** (code-only, NO schema). Spec: `211-procurement-terminology-clarity.md`. Evidence: `docs/procurement-uxui-audit-2026-06.md` (multi-agent UX audit, 85 surfaces / 91 verified findings).

Operator report: admins can't intuitively distinguish a PO (ýπÉý∏öý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠) from its PO-items. The headline cause (audit `po-vs-items-01`, confirmed **high**, independently rediscovered √ó9): a PO has no status column ‚Äî its roll-up is derived from member PRs ‚Äî so `PURCHASE_ORDER_STATUS_LABEL` and `PURCHASE_REQUEST_STATUS_LABEL` shared the byte-identical strings `ý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ýπÅý∏•ýπâý∏ß` (PR purchased / PO ordered) and `ý∏Åý∏≥ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏™ýπàý∏á` (PR on_route / PO in_transit). On the PO detail the order pill and a line pill then read the same word.

**U1 (this unit):** split the PO roll-up vocabulary in `src/lib/i18n/labels.ts` ‚Äî `ordered: ý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ýπÅý∏•ýπâý∏ß ‚Üí ý∏≠ý∏≠ý∏ÅýπÉý∏öý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ýπÅý∏•ýπâý∏ß`, `in_transit: ý∏Åý∏≥ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏™ýπàý∏á ‚Üí ý∏Åý∏≥ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏™ýπàý∏áý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áýπÉý∏ö` (PR map untouched; `open`/`partially_received`/`received` already order-scoped). Test-first: added a cross-map invariant in `tests/unit/i18n-labels.test.ts` ‚Äî the PR-status and PO-status maps must share NO string value (regression guard), plus pins for the two new strings. Display-only (logic keys on enums), so all PO surfaces (detail header, grid PO header row, phone PO card, delivery page) inherit it with no other code change. Verify limitation: not exercised in a live browser (PO detail needs LINE auth + seeded PO data); the unit test asserts the exact rendered strings the surfaces consume.

**U2 (PR #?? ‚Äî shipping):** visually type the IDs. New `src/lib/purchasing/format-id.ts` SSOT (`formatPoNumber`/`formatPrNumber`, zero-pad 4) routed through all 16 PR/PO render sites ‚Äî fixes the bare-`PR-7`-vs-`PR-0007` drift (the grid/basket/create-sheet showed bare numbers, everything else 4-padded). New server-safe `<PoNumberTag>` (a `bg-sunk` chip with the Package icon) marks every PO number as an order, visually distinct from the plain-mono PR number ‚Äî applied on the worklist grid PO header + phone PO card, the PO detail header, the delivery detail eyebrow, and the PR‚ÜíPO parent link. Notifications keep their own copy of the formatter (`compose-notification.ts` is danger-path ‚Üí U8). Test-first `tests/unit/format-id.test.ts`; no existing test broke (purchase-request-card already rendered `PR-0007`). Code-only.

**U3 (PR #?? ‚Äî shipping):** de-overload `ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£` on the worklist grid (`procurement-grid.tsx` only). The word labelled the item column, the bundle hint, the selection count AND the drawer doc count ‚Äî colliding with its line-count meaning on the same screen. Renamed: column header `ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£`‚Üí`ý∏™ý∏¥ýπàý∏áý∏óý∏µýπàý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠`; bundle hint `ýπÄý∏•ý∏∑ý∏≠ý∏Åý∏´ý∏•ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£‚Ä¶`‚Üí`ýπÄý∏•ý∏∑ý∏≠ý∏Åý∏´ý∏•ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠‚Ä¶`; selection `ýπÄý∏•ý∏∑ý∏≠ý∏Å {n} ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£`‚Üí`ýπÄý∏•ý∏∑ý∏≠ý∏Å {n} ý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠`; drawer doc fact `{n} ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£`‚Üí`{n} ýπÑý∏üý∏•ýπå`. The legitimate PO line-count (`{n} ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£`) is KEPT. Test-first `procurement-grid-terminology.test.tsx` (header rename; the bundle-hint case was dropped ‚Äî rendering the bundle path mounts the create-PO sheet's `useRouter`, which the grid-health test also avoids). Accounting register `ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£`‚Üí`ýπÉý∏öý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠` moved to U9. Code-only.

**U5 (PR #?? ‚Äî shipping):** PO membership visible in EVERY worklist band, not just the `in_transit` PO group. The page now fetches `po_number` for every PO any row belongs to (`poNumberById`, all bands), bakes it onto `ProcurementGridRecord.po_number`, and passes `poNumber` to `PurchaseRequestCard`. The desktop grid renders a `<PoNumberTag>` chip per row outside `in_transit` (which keeps its PoHeaderRow group); the phone card renders the chip whenever the request belongs to a PO. Required-field add to `ProcurementGridRecord` ‚Üí updated all 4 constructors (page, grid-health test, terminology test, phone-basket test, grid-preview page). Test-first: extended `purchase-request-card.test.tsx` (chip shows with poNumber, absent without). Code-only.

**U6 (PR #?? ‚Äî shipping):** make the level legible in nav. The nav landmark named the whole purchasing area `ý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠` (the PR term) in every role's tab set, and a **PO** detail's back-chip read `ý∏Åý∏•ý∏±ý∏öýπÑý∏õý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠`. Renamed `ý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠` ‚Üí neutral `ý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠` in both nav sources (`hub-nav.tsx` desktop strip √ó3 sets + `bottom-tab-bar.tsx` phone bar √ó3 sets) + the worklist page title/kicker; back-chips on the PO detail and PR detail ‚Üí `ý∏Åý∏•ý∏±ý∏öýπÑý∏õý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠`. The PR-concept `ý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠` stays for the request list/empty-state/create buttons. Test-first: flipped the existing nav assertions in `hub-nav.test.tsx` + `bottom-tab-bar.test.tsx` to expect `ý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠` (7 red ‚Üí green). Code-only. **Deferred ‚Üí U6b:** breadcrumb + `?from=` PR‚ÜíPO back-href.

**U4 (PR #?? ‚Äî shipping, operator-directed reframe):** status icons app-wide. Operator asked to "use icons to distinguish statuses, consistent with detail pages" ‚Äî better than restyling the PO pill colour (which would regress the spec-20 sun-readable solid fills). New SSOT `src/lib/status-icons.ts` (icon maps + accessors for all 7 StatusPill domains, `Record<Enum, LucideIcon>` = type-enforced totality) + an optional `icon` prop on `StatusPill` (backward-compatible). A one-agent-per-file **workflow** added `icon={domainIcon(sameArg)}` to all **28** call sites across **18** files, so the glyph renders identically on the worklist and every detail page/card/tracker. Test-first `status-icons.test.ts` (totality). Verified: tsc + eslint clean (run direct ‚Äî corepack flaked to pnpm 11.9.0 mid-run, recovered), 1796/1796 tests. Code-only. Feedback-status pills use a separate mechanism ‚Üí follow-up.

**U7 (PR #?? ‚Äî shipping):** one band-label SSOT on the procurement worklist. The `to_order` band read three ways ‚Äî chip `ý∏≠ý∏ôý∏∏ý∏°ý∏±ý∏ïý∏¥ýπÅý∏•ýπâý∏ß`, KPI tile `ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠`, pipeline header `ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠`. New `PROCUREMENT_BAND_LABEL` in `procurement-pipeline.ts`, consumed by the band headers + `worklist-status-chips.ts` + `worklist-kpis.ts`; the chip now uses the canonical `ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠` (in_transit/overdue already agreed, now SSOT-sourced). Test-first: flipped the chip assertion + added `band-label-ssot.test.ts` (the three sources must agree). The SITE band engine (`request-bands.ts`) is a separate role-view, NOT force-unified (cross-engine reconciliation is its own change). Code-only.

**U10a (PR #?? ‚Äî shipping):** delivery-installment term. A bare `ý∏áý∏ßý∏îý∏óý∏µýπà N` collided with `ý∏áý∏ßý∏îý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô` (the billing/work milestone on deliverables + schedule gantt). New `deliveryOrdinalLabel(n)` SSOT in `po-deliveries.ts` ‚Üí `ý∏áý∏ßý∏îý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏™ýπàý∏áý∏óý∏µýπà N`, used in the deliveries section, the per-`ý∏áý∏ßý∏î` tracker, and the delivery detail h1. The billing `ý∏áý∏ßý∏îý∏óý∏µýπà` (deliverables/gantt) is deliberately left untouched ‚Äî that's the disambiguation. Test-first `delivery-ordinal-label.test.ts`. Code-only. U10 split into sub-units (U10b ETA term, U10c store noun `ý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á`, U10d supplier-ref/stepper/dispatch/create-PO) ‚Äî done incrementally.

**U10b (PR #?? ‚Äî shipping):** ETA term SSOT. Expected-arrival read three ways ‚Äî grid English `ETA`, drawer `ý∏Ñý∏≤ý∏îý∏ßýπàý∏≤ý∏àý∏∞ýπÑý∏îýπâý∏£ý∏±ý∏ö`, PO card `ý∏Åý∏≥ý∏´ý∏ôý∏îý∏£ý∏±ý∏öý∏Çý∏≠ý∏á`. New `ETA_LABEL = "ý∏Åý∏≥ý∏´ý∏ôý∏îý∏£ý∏±ý∏öý∏Çý∏≠ý∏á"` in labels.ts ‚Üí grid header/cell + drawer Fact + PO card. Form inputs (create-PO sheet, purchase-record-form) keep `ý∏Ñý∏≤ý∏îý∏ßýπàý∏≤ý∏àý∏∞ýπÑý∏îýπâý∏£ý∏±ý∏öý∏Çý∏≠ý∏á` (set-the-date prompt, separate from display). Test-first (grid header drops English ETA). Code-only.

**U10c (PR #?? ‚Äî shipping):** store noun. Retired `ý∏™ýπÇý∏ïý∏£ýπå` (loanword duplicate) ‚Üí SSOT `ý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á` across labels + store actions + PR form + store-count-manager + store-manager + wp-issue-stock + validate-purchase-request (13 sites). `ý∏™ý∏ïýπäý∏≠ý∏Å` (=quantity) kept (ý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á=place, ý∏™ý∏ïýπäý∏≠ý∏Å=quantity). Tests updated in lockstep. Code-only.

**U10d (PR #?? ‚Äî shipping, BATCHED):** the small terminology leftovers in one PR (operator "Batch"). `CREATE_PO_LABEL="ý∏™ý∏£ýπâý∏≤ý∏áýπÉý∏öý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠"` SSOT (dropped English "PO" from bundle bar/drawer/sheet/button/price-comparison); supplier-ref field `ýπÄý∏•ý∏Çý∏óý∏µýπàýπÉý∏öý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠`‚Üí`ýπÄý∏•ý∏Çý∏≠ýπâý∏≤ý∏áý∏≠ý∏¥ý∏áý∏úý∏πýπâý∏Çý∏≤ý∏¢` (create-PO sheet + record-purchase form + validator); PR-stepper aria `ý∏™ý∏ñý∏≤ý∏ôý∏∞ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠`‚Üí`ý∏™ý∏ñý∏≤ý∏ôý∏∞ý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠`; dispatch button `ý∏öý∏±ý∏ôý∏óý∏∂ý∏Åý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏™ýπàý∏á`‚Üí`ý∏öý∏±ý∏ôý∏óý∏∂ý∏Åý∏ßýπàý∏≤ý∏Åý∏≥ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏™ýπàý∏á`; `ý∏™ý∏£ýπâý∏≤ý∏á PR ýπÅý∏•ýπâý∏ß`‚Üí`ý∏™ý∏£ýπâý∏≤ý∏áý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ýπÅý∏•ýπâý∏ß`. Lockstep test updates. One-agent-per-file workflow. Code-only.

**U6b (PR #?? ‚Äî shipping):** the two pieces deferred out of U6, both safe nav polish. **(1) PO-detail breadcrumb** ‚Äî the PO detail dropped you onto a supplier name with only a bare number chip for level context. New server-safe `<PoBreadcrumb>` (`src/components/features/purchasing/po-breadcrumb.tsx`, parallel to `PoNumberTag`) renders `ý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ ‚Ä∫ ýπÉý∏öý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ [PO-####]` in the DetailHeader nameplate: the `ý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠` area crumb links back to `/requests` (the U6 landmark), the order crumb is terminal (`aria-current="page"`, the typed U2 chip), replacing the bare `<PoNumberTag>` eyebrow. **(2) `?from=` PR‚ÜíPO back-href** ‚Äî a PR reached from its PO now returns to the PO, not the worklist: the PO detail wraps each member-PR link in `withBackFrom(.../requests/{id}, /requests/orders/{poId})`, and the PR detail reads `?from` ‚Üí `safeBackHref(from, "/requests")` for its back chip (icon-only, label stays `ý∏Åý∏•ý∏±ý∏öýπÑý∏õý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠`; other entry points keep the `/requests` fallback). Built on the already-tested `back-href.ts` primitives (same mechanism as WP/feedback detail). Test-first `po-breadcrumb.test.tsx` (area crumb links to /requests; ýπÉý∏öý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ + PO-0012 shown; only the area crumb is a link). Code-only.

**U11a (PR #?? ‚Äî shipping; operator-steered 2026-06-27):** self-purchase consolidation. Operator steer: "PR is PR, self purchase is self purchase ‚Ä¶ consolidate in 1 place" (+ self-purchase needs item-image + docs uploads). The two self-purchase actions sat in DIFFERENT WP tabs ‚Äî `ý∏öý∏±ý∏ôý∏óý∏∂ý∏Åý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ý∏´ý∏ôýπâý∏≤ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô` (`SitePurchaseForm`, off-catalog + VAT invoice) in `ý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠`, and `ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ýπÄý∏áý∏¥ý∏ôý∏™ý∏î ýπÉý∏äýπâý∏óý∏µýπàý∏áý∏≤ý∏ôý∏ôý∏µýπâýπÄý∏•ý∏¢` (`SitePurchaseUseNow`, catalogued cash + buy/ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å) in `ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Åý∏Çý∏≠ý∏á`. New server-safe `<SelfPurchaseSection>` (`src/components/features/purchasing/self-purchase-section.tsx`) groups BOTH under one **`ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ýπÄý∏≠ý∏á`** heading with an invoice-vs-cash hint, rendered in the `ý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠` tab below the PR form; the cash buy is removed from the `ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Åý∏Çý∏≠ý∏á` tab (now the pure `WpIssueStock` withdraw). PR (`ý∏™ý∏£ýπâý∏≤ý∏áý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠`) stays its own distinct affordance ‚Äî "PR is PR". Both self-purchase RPC gates exclude procurement ‚Üí kept the `!readOnly` gate. Test-first `self-purchase-section.test.tsx` (both paths under one heading). 1807/1807. Code-only, no schema.

**U11b (PR #?? ‚Äî HELD, danger-path RLS; operator-merge):** a self-purchase now carries an **item photo** in addition to the receipt/invoice docs. **Correction to the U11a-era "code-only" estimate:** the `reference` attachment INSERT RLS arm only admitted status `requested`, so a `site_purchased` self-purchase couldn't attach an item photo. Additive migration `20260813003900` widens the `reference` arm to also admit `site_purchased` (DROP+CREATE in place, reconstructed verbatim from the live spec-182-U4 policy ‚Äî only the reference arm changed; storage policy already admitted `site_purchased`). New `addReferenceAttachment` action (mirrors `addInvoiceAttachment`, purpose `reference`, `site_purchased`-gated) + thin `ItemPhotoUploader` (reuses `InvoiceUploader`, label `ýπÅý∏ôý∏öý∏£ý∏πý∏õý∏™ý∏¥ý∏ôý∏Ñýπâý∏≤`), shown beside the docs uploader in `SitePurchaseForm`'s success state. Only `#2` has a PR to attach to (`#3` use-now writes straight to stock movements, no PR ‚Üí item images there = separate feature). Test-first `site-purchase-form-photos.test.tsx` + pgTAP `33-site-purchase.test.sql` E.1/E.2. **RLS = danger-path ‚Üí PR held for operator one-tap merge; migration `db:push`'d to the shared DB ahead of merge (additive, safe).**

**Cross-engine band reconcile (PR #?? ‚Äî shipping):** `terminology-05`/`worklist-hub-16`, critic gap X2. The site worklist (`request-bands.ts`) and procurement pipeline (`procurement-pipeline.ts`) are two band engines over one table. Confirmed a user only ever sees ONE engine (`isProcurement` gate in `requests/page.tsx`) ‚Üí no same-screen contradiction (that was U7); the real risk was DRIFT of the shared band terms (`ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏≠ý∏ôý∏∏ý∏°ý∏±ý∏ïý∏¥`, `ý∏Åý∏≥ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏™ýπàý∏á`, the `ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠` core were duplicated literals). New `src/lib/purchasing/worklist-band-vocab.ts` `WORKLIST_BAND_TERM` SSOT; both engines source the shared terms from it (procurement verbatim; site `to_order` = `ý∏≠ý∏ôý∏∏ý∏°ý∏±ý∏ïý∏¥ýπÅý∏•ýπâý∏ß ` + the shared core). The deliberate per-view differences are PRESERVED (site terminal `ýπÄý∏™ý∏£ýπáý∏àýπÅý∏•ýπâý∏ß`/`closed` vs procurement `ýπÑý∏îýπâý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÅý∏•ýπâý∏ß`). Behaviour-preserving (every rendered string byte-identical). Test-first `worklist-band-vocab.test.ts`. Code-only.

**U9a (PR #?? ‚Äî HELD, danger-path accounting; operator-merge):** carry the PO-vs-items clarity into the accounting surface. (1) voucher PO reference `#3` ‚Üí `formatPoNumber()` = `PO-0003` (reuses the U2 SSOT; `accounting/purchases/[id]/page.tsx`). (2) register count `{n} ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£` ‚Üí `{n} ýπÉý∏öý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠` via new tested `purchaseRegisterCountLabel()` in `purchases-view.ts` (accounting-ap-02, moved from U3). Test-first `purchases-view.test.ts`. Display-only, no schema; `src/app/accounting/**` = danger-path ‚Üí held. **Deferred ‚Üí U9b:** the live PO link (blocked ‚Äî `accounting` role isn't in `PURCHASING_ROLES` so the PO detail route is a dead link for it; needs an access-policy decision + `load-voucher` to expose the PO id) and the register grouping-by-PO with subtotal (bigger restructure).

**U8 (PR #?? ‚Äî HELD, danger-path notifications; operator-merge):** PR notifications name their parent PO (critic gap X1). `compose-notification.ts` pushed raw PR status words with no PO context; now `ý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ PR-0012 ¬∑ ýπÉý∏öý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ PO-0003: ý∏Åý∏≥ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏™ýπàý∏á`. Code-only, no migration: the PO is resolved at compose time in `drain/route.ts` (the outbox row already carries `purchase_request_id` ‚Üí batched 2-hop PR‚ÜíPO‚Üí`po_number` ‚Üí new `ComposeContext.poNumber`, mirrors the `wpCode` enrichment). Composer drops its duplicated local `formatPrNumber` for the U2 SSOT via a new `prRef()` helper. Absent PO ‚Üí unchanged. Test-first `compose-notification.test.ts`. `src/lib/notifications/**` + `src/app/api/notifications/**` = danger-path ‚Üí held.

**U11c-A (PR #?? ‚Äî HELD, danger-path money migration; operator-merge):** VAT-invoiced buy-&-use-now. Operator approved the bounded U11c design (subagent posting-map showed the ONLY real missing combo is catalogued+VAT+use-now; free-text+use-now is structurally impossible AND not real ‚Äî `record_site_purchase` already books free-text to the WP). `site_purchase_use_now` gains `p_vat_rate` (DROP+CREATE, 7-arg; new arg defaults 0 ‚Üí existing caller unbroken): stores the receipt NET + snapshots `vat_rate`, so the receipt poster (`post_stock_receipt_to_gl`, spec 208 U4b) splits `Dr 1300 Input VAT` via its no-PR fallback. `p_unit_cost` stays GROSS (ADR 0045); WP charged NET. At `p_vat_rate=0` byte-identical. migration `20260813004000`; pgTAP 228 +section F (107@7% ‚Üí Dr1500 100/Dr1300 7/Cr2100 107, WP carries 100) + signature pins ‚Üí 29/29. Migration db:push'd; only the known file-200 reconciliation drift fails (pre-existing, unrelated). Danger-path (money RPC) ‚Üí held.

**U11c-B (PR #?? ‚Äî shipping; code-only):** the unified guided `ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ýπÄý∏≠ý∏á` form ‚Äî completes U11c. New `<SelfPurchaseForm>` replaces U11a's two cards: item is catalog (picker) OR free-text (mode toggle); `ý∏àý∏≥ý∏ôý∏ßý∏ôýπÄý∏áý∏¥ý∏ô` GROSS; `ý∏°ý∏µýπÉý∏öý∏Åý∏≥ý∏Åý∏±ý∏öý∏Ýý∏≤ý∏©ý∏µ` splits Input VAT (1300); `ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ýπÉý∏äýπâý∏óý∏µýπàý∏áý∏≤ý∏ôý∏ôý∏µýπâýπÄý∏•ý∏¢` shown for catalog items only (store keys on `catalog_item_id`). Routes catalog+use-now ‚Üí `sitePurchaseUseNow` (VAT-aware via U11c-A), else ‚Üí `recordSitePurchase` (books WP; success reveals item-photo + receipt uploaders). Plumbing: `validateSitePurchase` + both actions gain `vatRate` (neither self-purchase form collected VAT before). `SelfPurchaseSection` renders the one form; old `SitePurchaseForm`/`SitePurchaseUseNow` now unused ‚Üí follow-up task to delete. Test-first `validate-site-purchase` (VAT) + `self-purchase-form.test.tsx` (both routes + VAT). 1823/1823.

**U9b (PR #?? ‚Äî HELD, danger-path auth/access; operator-merge; operator chose Option A):** the accounting voucher's PO becomes a live link into the PO detail. New `PO_DETAIL_VIEW_ROLES = PURCHASING_ROLES + accounting` opens the PO detail route to accounting **read-only**; accounting reads the PO via the admin client (org-wide money posture, no RLS grant/migration), sees money (`canSeeMoney = isBackOffice || isAccounting`), but write actions stay gated out (`isBackOffice`/`RECEIVE_ROLES` exclude accounting). `load-voucher` exposes `poId`; the voucher's `ýπÉý∏öý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠` row links to `/requests/orders/[poId]` with `?from=` ‚Üí its back chip returns to the voucher (PO detail back chip gains `?from` via `safeBackHref`). Test-first `role-sets.test.ts`. Auth change ‚Üí held.

**U9c (PR #?? ‚Äî HELD, danger-path accounting; operator-merge):** register grouped by PO (`accounting-ap-03`). The accounting purchase register now reads a PO at a time ‚Äî each PO group gets a `<PoNumberTag>` header + a gross subtotal; direct/site buys (no PO) group last (`ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ý∏ïý∏£ý∏á (ýπÑý∏°ýπàý∏°ý∏µýπÉý∏öý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠)`). New pure `groupRegisterByPo()` in `purchases-view.ts` (generic, satang-summed, PO groups first-appearance order, no-PO last); `load-purchases` resolves a `poNumber` per row. Test-first `purchases-view.test.ts`. Display-only, no schema; `src/app/accounting/**` ‚Üí held.

**Spec 211 COMPLETE** ‚Äî U1‚ÄìU11 + cross-engine band SSOT + U6b + U8 + U9a/b/c + dead-form cleanup. (Dead-self-purchase-form cleanup done via #118.)
