## Spec 206 ‚Äî WHT certificate recording UI (2026-06-26)

Status: **SHIPPED ‚Äî 2026-06-26** (code-only, NO schema/db:push). Audit gap **G11** ‚Äî
the last accounting back-office write gap. The `record_wht_certificate` RPC, the
`wht_certificates`/`wht_rates` tables, the GL-posting trigger, the pure
`validateWhtCertificate` validator, and the read-only register `/accounting/wht`
all existed since spec 149 U6, but the validator was **orphaned** ‚Äî no write
surface. This unit adds the "+ ý∏öý∏±ý∏ôý∏óý∏∂ý∏ÅýπÉý∏öý∏´ý∏±ý∏Åý∏Ýý∏≤ý∏©ý∏µ" form over the existing RPC.

**Live-DB gate verification (the key find).** Before wiring the gate I queried the
live RPC: `record_wht_certificate` admits `pm/super/project_director` ‚Äî the
migration _file_ shows pm/super, but `‚Ä¶0751‚Ä¶_project_director_rpc_gates.sql`
widened it live (the "live-body re-source trap"). Same for `create_client_billing`

- `post_journal_entry` (both pd live). So the gate reuses **`PM_ROLES`** (the
  role-home SSOT) via `isManagerRole`, NOT a fresh `[pm,super]` literal ‚Äî matching
  the journal/G8 precedent and the live RPC. Among ACCOUNTING_ROLES reachers only
  super_admin is a manager, so today the write affordance lights up for super_admin
  only (auto-widens if spec 166 re-adds PM/PD to the page gate).

**TDD:** `resolveWhtRate(incomeType, override, rates)` (mirrors the RPC's
`coalesce(p_wht_rate, default_rate)` ‚Äî finite override wins incl. 0; else the
income-type default; unknown+no-override ‚Üí null) written RED-first (6 cases) then
GREEN. `validateWhtCertificate` unchanged (still the amount gate). **Files:**
`resolveWhtRate` in `wht-certificate.ts`; `loadWhtFormData` in `load-registers.ts`
(income types + 3 party masters, parallel reads); `wht/actions.ts`
`recordWhtCertificate` (gate PM_ROLES, pre-validate, deducted-needs-party friendly
error, omit-optional args, call on auth.supabase, revalidate); `wht/record-wht-form.tsx`
(`'use client'` bottom-sheet ‚Äî direction/form/income-type‚Üírate-autofill/13-digit
tax id/base/party[supplier|contractor for ýπÄý∏£ý∏≤ý∏´ý∏±ý∏Å, client for ý∏ñý∏πý∏Åý∏´ý∏±ý∏Å]/date/note +
live wht_amount preview); wired into `wht/page.tsx` (canWrite = isManagerRole,
parallel load). Reuses `AccountingActionResult` + `ACCOUNTING_ACTION_ERROR` from
`billing-actions.ts`. Verified: typecheck ¬∑ lint ¬∑ full vitest **1723/1723** green.
Visual preview skipped (no dev-auth path ‚Äî LINE OAuth only; the form is a 1:1 twin
of the shipped spec-204 billing form). Money renders via the canonical `baht()`
SSOT.

**Open questions / follow-ups:**

- `BILLING_WRITE_ROLES = [pm,super]` (spec 204) is now confirmed **stale vs its
  live RPC** (which admits project_director) ‚Äî a pre-existing UI under-grant (PD
  can post billings per the RPC but the form never offers it). Not a security hole;
  flagged for the role-set SSOT consolidation (architecture-audit **rank 2**:
  fold billing/journal/wht's identical pm/super/pd accounting-write sets into one
  `ACCOUNTING_WRITE_ROLES`). Not fixed here (scope).
- `pay_source_table`/`pay_source_id` linking (cert ‚Üí its source payment), WHT-cert
  auto-record at billing certify, and ý∏Ý.ý∏á.ý∏î. PDF generation remain deferred.

## Gap G8 ‚Äî manual journal-entry UI (2026-06-25)

Status: **SHIPPED ‚Äî 2026-06-25** (code-only, NO schema/db:push). Audit gap **G8** ‚Äî
the `post_journal_entry` / `reverse_journal_entry` RPCs existed since spec 149 with
no screen. New `/accounting/journal` (general journal): a multi-line entry form
(date + memo + account-picker/debit/credit rows, one-sided enforced at input, LIVE
double-entry balance preview via `validateJournalLines`) + a recent-entries list
with a per-entry ý∏Åý∏•ý∏±ý∏öý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ (reverse) control. **Gate = `PM_ROLES`** (pm/super/
project_director ‚Äî exactly who the RPCs admit; verified LIVE that a later migration
widened the gate to add project_director, NOT what the original migration file
shows). Placed in /accounting but gated to PM_ROLES (the journal-POST capability is
distinct from accounting-VIEW; only super_admin overlaps = today's operator); entry
point on the accounting landing shown only to `isManagerRole` so accounting-role
gets no dead link. **TDD:** `canReverseJournalEntry(status, alreadyReversed)`
predicate (mirrors the RPC guard ‚Äî original stays 'posted' after reversal since
append-only, so reversibility needs both) written RED-first then GREEN. **Loader**
`load-manual-journals.ts` parallelises fetches (specs 147/148) + one account map
resolves picker labels AND line codes (no N+1). **Riders (audit rank 7+12):**
standardised the new action on `requireActionRole`; hoisted the shared
`ACCOUNTING_ACTION_ERROR` next to `AccountingActionResult` and pointed billings at
it (DRY); wired the previously-dormant `validateJournalLines` (rank 12 ‚Äî its
intended consumer). Verified: typecheck ¬∑ lint ¬∑ full vitest 1698/1698 ¬∑ build
green; loader queries confirmed against live schema. Visual preview skipped (no
dev-auth path ‚Äî LINE OAuth only). **Open:** PM/PD have no nav entry point (can't
reach /accounting landing); accounting-role can't post manual JEs (RPC excludes it,
accounting is v3) ‚Äî both deferred, not blockers. Money renders via the canonical
`baht()` SSOT (audit rank 1 consolidation is a separate follow-up unit).

## Audit ‚Äî architecture & quality + GL test de-brittle (2026-06-25)

Status: **SHIPPED.** Ran an 8-lens multi-agent architecture/quality audit (49
agents, adversarial verify) before resuming feature builds: 40 candidates ‚Üí 24
confirmed + 16 partial, 0 refuted ‚Üí 15-item ranked register
(`docs/architecture-quality-audit-2026-06.md`), woven into the phase plan. Codebase
healthy; debt = latent drift/hygiene. **Acted on the one real blocker (rank 3):**
de-brittled GL pgTAP 81/85/86/87/88 (asserted table-wide aggregates that go red
once real revenue posts; 81 was already RED against the live DB's 27 real
journal_posted rows). 81 ‚Üí fixture-scoped audit count (target_id); 85/86/87 ‚Üí clear
gl_posting_outbox before the enqueue (mirrors 84); 88 ‚Üí project-scope the
4100/1210 totals + delta the global retention control, keeping Œ£debit=Œ£credit +
tie-out flags. Full pgTAP 175/175, 2983 assertions, 0 failures. Next recommended:
rank 1 money-SSOT, rank 2 role-set dedup (incl. drain omits project_director).

## Spec 204 ‚Äî client billing + retention write UI (2026-06-25)

Status: **SHIPPED ‚Äî 2026-06-25** (code-only, NO schema/db:push). Audit gap **G10** ‚Äî the
single biggest "backend-shipped-no-UI" gap. Spec 149 U5 shipped the client_billings /
retention_receivables tables, the create/certify/mark-due/release RPCs, the GL-posting
triggers, and the read-only registers ‚Äî but no UI to create a claim, certify it (book
AR/revenue/VAT/WHT + accrue retention), mark retention due, or release it. The GL had a
cost side, no revenue side. This unit adds the write surfaces.

- TDD (RED first): `tests/unit/billing-actions.test.ts` (status predicates
  canCertifyBilling/canMarkRetentionDue/canReleaseRetention mirroring the RPC guards) ‚Üí
  `src/lib/accounting/billing-actions.ts` (+ `BILLING_WRITE_ROLES` SSOT =
  [project_manager, super_admin], matching the RPC gate exactly ‚Äî NOT project_director).
- Actions: `billings/actions.ts` (createClientBilling, certifyClientBilling), `retention/
actions.ts` (markRetentionDue, releaseRetention) ‚Äî gate the AUTHED session, call the RPC
  on auth.supabase (never admin), revalidate both registers.
- UI: `CreateBillingForm` (BottomSheet + live computeBillingBreakdown preview), certify via
  ConfirmActionButton on draft/submitted rows; `RetentionRowActions` (mark-due date +
  release). Controls gated to BILLING_WRITE_ROLES (beta: super_admin only; auto-widens when
  spec 166 re-adds PM). Accounting role stays READ-ONLY on billing/retention (RPC = pm/super).
- QUALITY (architecture-first bar): extracted the copy-pasted server-action role-check into
  a shared `requireActionRole()` in `src/lib/auth/action-gate.ts` (periods/billings/retention
  now use it); extracted `baht()` into `src/lib/i18n/labels.ts` (3 of ~12 inline copies
  migrated); parallelized the billings-page reads (no waterfall, spec 147/148). Adversarial
  `/code-review high` ‚Üí 3 real findings FIXED (rate-field reset leak across claims; canWrite
  hardcoded‚ÜíSSOT drift; baht DRY); rest refuted (input type=date guards dates; empty‚âÝerror).
- Verify: typecheck + lint + full vitest green; pgTAP 85/86 + client-billing.test already
  cover the RPCs/math. Operator visual-confirm on /accounting/billings + /accounting/retention
  as super_admin.

Open follow-ups (architecture-audit sweep): accounting‚Üícertify yes/no (a spec/product call ‚Äî
RPC currently pm/super); migrate the remaining ~9 inline `baht` copies and the rest of the
server-action files to `requireActionRole()`.

## Spec 203 U2 ‚Äî widen post_purchase_to_gl gate + remediate the 9 (2026-06-25)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-25** (0f13656, mig 20260813002100, pgTAP 225 plan 14).
Applied via db:push after operator OK; a manual `drain_gl_posting(100)` posted the 9 ‚Üí
**verified: gl_posting_outbox purchase jobs = 27 posted / 0 failed / 0 pending; 9 new
WP-bound purchase entries, AP total ý∏ø102,453.57** (matches the stranded amount exactly).
4-lens review ‚Üí logic ships safe; the one blocker (seed aborting on CHECKs) was fixed. The drain-outage left 9 WP-bound PRs that advanced purchased‚Üídelivered
before any drain ran; `post_purchase_to_gl` gated on purchased/site_purchased only, so it
refused them (~ý∏ø102k unposted). U2 widens the gate to the committed-and-not-voided set
(purchased/site_purchased/on_route/delivered) and re-enqueues the 9 (failed‚Üípending) so the
cron posts them. SAFE (no double-book): the enqueue trigger fires ONLY at the purchased
transition, so one job per PR; WP-less stays suppressed (return null); divert reverses
directly (doesn't rely on the refusal). Re-sourced from LIVE 20260813001000.

mig 20260813002100 (gate + remediation UPDATE) ¬∑ pgTAP 225 (plan 12, RED on delivered/
on_route posting). A applied first verified green (224 + 85/86/87). **A's drain surfaced a
test-fragility casualty: 81-journal test 28 counts journal_posted audit rows table-wide
(have 20 want 2) ‚Äî same class the prune author flagged for 82/84/88. Folds into a
test-hardening sweep (scope all such counts to their fixtures) ‚Äî own follow-up, AFTER U2
posts the 9.**

## Spec 200 U2 ‚Äî assign a worker to a project at creation (2026-06-24)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-24** (no DB; lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ vitest green).

new DC needed a second step to assign. U2 lets you pick the project on day one.

project calls `createWorker` with `projectId`. 1 RED ‚Üí green.

**App (no DB ‚Äî reuses both existing RPCs):**

- The add-worker form gains the same optional **ýπÇý∏Ñý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£** `<select>` (`AddWorkerForm`
  now takes the `projects` prop).
- `createWorker` captures the new worker's id and, when a project was chosen, calls
  `assign_worker_to_project` ‚Äî a **create + assign**, avoiding a `create_worker`
  signature widen (no DROP+CREATE / re-grant trap). The assign is soft: the worker
  exists either way; a failed assign returns a message + can be set in the edit sheet.

## Spec 200 ‚Äî assign a worker to a project (the missing UI) (2026-06-24)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-24** (no DB; lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ vitest green).
Operator asked "how do we assign DC to projects?" ‚Äî the answer was _you couldn't
from the app_. The `assign_worker_to_project` RPC + `workers.project_id` (one
project at a time, audit trail) existed since spec 172/the assignment migration,
but `/workers` only created/edited/set-rate/invited ‚Äî no assign control. This
wires the engine to the UI. No DB change.

**Test-first** (RED): extended `tests/unit/worker-roster-manager.test.tsx` ‚Äî shows
the current project on the row; the edit sheet's ýπÇý∏Ñý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ select ‚Üí `assignWorkerToProject`
on save; no assign when unchanged. 3 RED ‚Üí green (existing 7 still pass).

**App:**

- `assignWorkerToProject({ workerId, projectId })` action ‚Äî relays to the
  `assign_worker_to_project` definer (gate PM/super/director/procurement); `""`/null
  unassigns (RPC `p_project` defaults null ‚Üí cleared).
- `WorkerRosterManager`: `ManagedWorker` += `project_id`; new `projects` prop
  (`AssignableProject[]`, default `[]`); each row shows its current project (or
  "ý∏¢ý∏±ý∏áýπÑý∏°ýπàý∏£ý∏∞ý∏öý∏∏ýπÇý∏Ñý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£"); the per-row edit sheet gains a ýπÇý∏Ñý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ `<select>` ‚Üí
  assign-on-save (reassign moves it; one project at a time).
- `/workers` reads `workers.project_id` + the assigner's RLS-scoped projects.

**Note for the operator:** assignment is NOT required to log labour (the WP
labour picker lists everyone, ordering the project's crew first) ‚Äî it powers the
/store custody-receiver picker + the labour-picker ordering. Labour itself is
logged as full/half **days** (DC paid per day), per worker per day, on the WP
ý∏óý∏µý∏°ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô (#wp-labor) tab. **Out of scope:** assign-at-creation (the add form still
makes a project-less worker ‚Äî `create_worker` takes no project; follow-up if wanted).

## Spec 198 ‚Äî divert a delivered WP-bound line into the store, U2 (2026-06-24)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-24** (ADR 0064; mig 20260813000900; pgTAP
216; lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ vitest ¬∑ db:test). **SPEC 198 COMPLETE (U1+U2).**
Operator chose Option 3 (AskUserQuestion) ‚Äî the cost-clean version of "pre-fill
ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ from a delivery." Investigation found the simple pre-fill has no target
(store-bound lines already auto-check-in via spec 195 P3; stocking a WP-bound
line as-is double-counts), so U2 is an inventory **diversion with a GL transfer**.

**ADR 0064** ‚Äî divert a delivered WP-bound purchase into the store (cost transfer
WP-WIP ‚Üí Inventory). Surfaced the GL risk + the design BEFORE building; operator
approved "build it + push (with ADR)."

**GL transfer (net):** reverse the WP purchase (Dr 2100 AP / Cr 1400 WP-WIP) +
the new stock_receipt (Dr 1500 Inventory / Cr 2100 AP) ‚Üí WP-WIP 0 ¬∑ Inventory
+cost ¬∑ **AP unchanged**. A later ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å returns it Dr 1400 / Cr 1500 (cost lands on
the WP once, at usage ‚Äî the store-bound model).

**DB (migs 20260813000900 + 20260813001000):**

- New `divert_purchase_to_store(p_request_id)` definer RPC ‚Äî gate `SITE_STAFF`
  (procurement read-only in the store, spec 197), membership `can_see_project`,
  guards (delivered, WP-bound, catalogued, not already diverted ‚Äî the
  `stock_receipts_pr_uniq` index is the hard guard). It **reverses the WP purchase
  GL entry directly** (`reverse_journal_internal`, Dr 2100/Cr 1400) + **skips any
  pending purchase outbox job**, then inserts the `stock_receipt` (PR-stamped,
  all-in cost ‚Üí auto-enqueues Dr 1500/Cr AP), rolls `stock_on_hand`, sets the PR
  `work_package_id = NULL`.
- **`post_purchase_to_gl` is UNCHANGED.** The first attempt (000900) re-enqueued a
  `purchase` job to do the reversal + reordered `post_purchase_to_gl`'s
  reverse-before-suppress ‚Äî but that poster only accepts `purchased`/`site_purchased`,
  so a _delivered_ PR raises `P0001` and the reversal never fires. **pgTAP 216
  caught this** (WP-WIP stayed 300 = double-count). The corrective mig 001000
  reverts `post_purchase_to_gl` to LIVE and rewrites the RPC to reverse directly +
  skip the stale job. **Lesson: the async GL poster won't process a status it
  gates on ‚Äî reverse synchronously in the RPC.**
- **Async-safe:** purchase posted ‚Üí reverse it; still pending ‚Üí skip it (no WP-WIP
  ever). Both converge.

**App:** `divertPurchaseToStore({ requestId })` action + `DivertToStoreList`
client component (per-line `ý∏¢ýπâý∏≤ý∏¢ýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á` via `ConfirmActionButton`). The ý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á page
reads the project's delivered + WP-bound + catalogued PRs not yet diverted (RLS

- filter out those with a `stock_receipts.purchase_request_id`), gated to
  `canIssue` (SITE_STAFF); renders the list after `StoreManager`. `db:types` regen.

**Test-first** (RED): `tests/unit/divert-to-store-list.test.tsx` (list + confirm
‚Üí `divertPurchaseToStore`). **pgTAP 216 (plan 17, the ledger proof):**
structure/anon-deny; pre-divert WP-WIP=300; guards (visitor/non-member 42501,
not-delivered/WP-less 22023); happy divert; receipt-stamped + on-hand rolled + PR
WP-less; post the GL; **WP-WIP nets to 0 ¬∑ Inventory=300 ¬∑ AP net credit=300**;
already-diverted 22023.

**v1 scope:** whole-line divert only (no partial); the PR loses its WP identity in
the list (trace via `stock_receipts.purchase_request_id`); full undo of a divert
(re-attach the WP) is out of scope.

## Spec 198 ‚Äî multi-line ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ (bulk stock check-in), U1 (2026-06-24)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-24** (mig 20260813000800; pgTAP 215; lint ¬∑
typecheck ¬∑ vitest ¬∑ db:test green). Operator: "checking in items is too
difficult, one at a time ‚Äî the check-in list should be the same as delivery."
The ý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á `ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ý∏™ý∏ïýπäý∏≠ý∏Å` form was single-item-per-submit; U1 makes it a multi-row
**grid** recorded in one atomic call. (Operator chose **Both** grid + pre-fill-
from-delivery; U2 = pre-fill, sequenced next.)

**Test-first** (RED): `tests/unit/store-checkin-grid.test.tsx` ‚Äî opens a grid with
one draft row + `ýπÄý∏ûý∏¥ýπàý∏°ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£` add-row + disabled `ý∏öý∏±ý∏ôý∏óý∏∂ý∏Åý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏´ý∏°ý∏î`; enables on a
complete row; records every complete row in one `recordStockInBulk` call; drops
incomplete rows. 4 RED ‚Üí green.

**DB:** new `record_stock_in_bulk(p_project_id uuid, p_lines jsonb)` definer RPC
(mig 20260813000800) ‚Äî same role gate as the post-197 `record_stock_in`
(site_admin + PM tier + procurement + director), membership (`can_see_project OR
procurement`), per-line validation (qty>0, cost‚â•0, active item, supplier exists),
**atomic** (any bad line rolls back the whole batch ‚Äî no partial check-in), each
line inserts a `stock_receipts` row + rolls into `stock_on_hand`. Returns the
count. `CREATE` (new fn) + explicit grants (anon revoked). The single
`record_stock_in` is **KEPT** (spec 195 P3 auto-receipt depends on it). pgTAP 215
(plan 18): structure/anon-deny ¬∑ 2-line additive happy ¬∑ 7 validations ¬∑ atomicity
(good+bad line ‚Üí 0 receipts) ¬∑ visitor/non-member 42501 ¬∑ site_admin member happy.

**App:**

- `recordStockInBulk({ projectId, lines })` action (`src/app/store/actions.ts`) ‚Äî
  maps rows ‚Üí jsonb `[{catalog_item_id, qty, unit_cost, supplier_id, note}]`,
  calls the RPC, maps 42501/22023, `revalidatePath` the project store.
- `StoreManager` ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ sheet rewritten as a draft-row grid (`DraftReceiptRow[]`
  state, `+ ýπÄý∏ûý∏¥ýπàý∏°ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£` add, per-row `ý∏•ý∏ö` remove, one `ý∏öý∏±ý∏ôý∏óý∏∂ý∏Åý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏´ý∏°ý∏î` submit;
  enabled once ‚â•1 complete row; incomplete rows dropped on submit). Reuses the
  category-grouped item `<select>` per row. Swapped `recordStockIn`‚Üí`recordStockInBulk`.
- Updated the two spec-177 `store-manager.test.tsx` ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ tests to the grid
  (button `ý∏öý∏±ý∏ôý∏óý∏∂ý∏Åý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏´ý∏°ý∏î`, asserts `recordStockInBulk` with a `lines` array).
- `db:types` regenerated (new RPC).

**U2 (pre-fill ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ from a PO delivery ‚Äî incl. the anti-double-book rule vs
spec 195 P3 auto-receipt) NOT started.**

## Spec 197 ‚Äî empty-ý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á state, U3 (2026-06-24)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-24** (no DB; lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ vitest green).
**SPEC 197 COMPLETE (U1‚ÄìU3).** A brand-new project's store is empty; a bare
"no stock" line was a dead end. The empty state now:

- Leads with the `ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ý∏™ý∏ïýπäý∏≠ý∏Å` action as the **primary** CTA (the secondary
  header ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ button is dropped when empty, so there is one clear lead).
- Shows one line ‚Äî "ý∏¢ý∏±ý∏áýπÑý∏°ýπàý∏°ý∏µý∏Çý∏≠ý∏áýπÉý∏ôý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á ‚Äî ýπÄý∏£ý∏¥ýπàý∏°ý∏àý∏≤ý∏Åý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ ý∏´ý∏£ý∏∑ý∏≠ý∏úýπàý∏≤ý∏ôýπÅý∏úý∏ôý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏´ý∏≤" ‚Äî where
  **ýπÅý∏úý∏ôý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏´ý∏≤** links to the project's supply-plan chip, but only when the viewer
  can reach it (`SUPPLY_PLAN_ROLES`); for site_admin (can't plan supply) it stays
  plain text, never a dead link.
- Suppresses the `ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏àý∏ôý∏±ý∏öý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á` full-stocktake (U2) while the store is empty
  (counting zero items is meaningless).

**Test-first** (RED): `tests/unit/store-empty-state.test.tsx` ‚Äî leads with ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ +
links ýπÅý∏úý∏ôý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏´ý∏≤ when `emptyStateSupplyPlanHref` set; plain text (no link) when null.
2 RED ‚Üí green. Also updated the spec-177 `store-manager.test.tsx` empty assertion
(old "ý∏¢ý∏±ý∏áýπÑý∏°ýπàý∏°ý∏µý∏™ý∏ïýπäý∏≠ý∏ÅýπÉý∏ôý∏™ýπÇý∏ïý∏£ýπå" ‚Üí "ý∏¢ý∏±ý∏áýπÑý∏°ýπàý∏°ý∏µý∏Çý∏≠ý∏áýπÉý∏ôý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á").

**App:**

- `StoreManager` gains `emptyStateSupplyPlanHref?: string | null` (default null).
  Non-null ‚Üí empty state renders ýπÅý∏úý∏ôý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏´ý∏≤ as a `<Link>` to the supply plan +
  a `BUTTON_PRIMARY` ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ lead; null ‚Üí plain text. Header ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ now shows only
  when `onHand.length > 0`.
- `app/projects/[projectId]/store/page.tsx`: `canPlanSupply = SUPPLY_PLAN_ROLES`,
  passes `emptyStateSupplyPlanHref={canPlanSupply ? supplyPlanHref(id) : null}`;
  gates `ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏àý∏ôý∏±ý∏öý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á` on `canIssue && onHand.length > 0`.

Ties into the first-real-project adoption arc (spec 192). Placement/UX only.

## Spec 197 ‚Äî ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏àý∏ôý∏±ý∏ö unified into ý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á, U2 (2026-06-24)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-24** (no DB; lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ vitest green).
Retires the standalone `/stock-count` route and folds counting into the
per-project `ý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á` surface. Two modes, one home, one `record_stock_count`:

- **Mode A ‚Äî spot count** (unchanged): the per-row `ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏àý∏ôý∏±ý∏ö` on `StoreManager`,
  already on the ý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á page for SITE_STAFF (`canIssue`).
- **Mode B ‚Äî full stocktake** (relocated): the `/stock-count` page's
  `StoreCountManager` (the focused count-only item list) now renders on the
  ý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á page behind a `ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏àý∏ôý∏±ý∏öý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á` toggle, gated to `canIssue` (SITE_STAFF).

**Test-first** (RED): `tests/unit/store-full-stocktake.test.tsx` ‚Äî `FULL_STOCKTAKE_LABEL`,
`hidePicker` suppresses the picker, `collapsible` hides the list behind the
`ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏àý∏ôý∏±ý∏öý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á` toggle until opened. 3 RED ‚Üí green.

**App:**

- `StoreCountManager` gains `hidePicker` (default false ‚Üí existing B2 picker
  behavior preserved) + `collapsible` (default false ‚Üí list always open). When
  `collapsible`, the list starts closed behind a `ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏àý∏ôý∏±ý∏öý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á` toggle.
- `app/projects/[projectId]/store/page.tsx` renders `<StoreCountManager hidePicker
collapsible>` after `StoreManager` (maps the on-hand to `CountStockRow[]`),
  gated to `canIssue`.
- `labels.ts`: `+FULL_STOCKTAKE_LABEL = "ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏àý∏ôý∏±ý∏öý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á"`; retired `STOCK_COUNT_LABEL`
  (its only consumer, the `/stock-count` page, is gone).
- `app/stock-count/page.tsx` ‚Üí thin `redirect("/projects")`.
- Removed the site_admin `/stock-count` `SettingsLink` (+ orphan `ClipboardCheck`
  / `STOCK_COUNT_LABEL` imports).
- `bottom-tab-bar.tsx`: `SETTINGS_TAB.match` drops `/stock-count`.
- `nav-back-affordance.test.ts`: `/stock-count` ‚Üí EXCLUDED (now a redirect).

**Scope note / open question:** the spec describes Mode B as a "batch submit"
full-list form (every item with an inline counted-qty field, one submit). The
actual `/stock-count` component is **per-row** (a sheet per item) ‚Äî the spec
author's recollection of the existing surface was inaccurate. Per the spec's own
overriding rule ("no new store capability ‚Äî relocation + consolidation only"), I
**relocated the existing per-row component** rather than build a new batch form
(that would be new capability). If the operator genuinely wants a single-submit
batch grid, that is a follow-up spec, not U2.

**Dead-but-tested:** `StoreCountManager`'s picker `onChange` still pushes
`/stock-count?project=‚Ä¶` ‚Äî unreachable now (prod always passes `hidePicker`; the
standalone route redirects), but kept so the spec-178-B2 picker test stays green.

**U3 (empty-ý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á state) NOT started.**

## Spec 197 ‚Äî ý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á as a per-project surface, U1 (2026-06-24)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-24** (mig 20260813000700; pgTAP 181; lint ¬∑
typecheck ¬∑ vitest ¬∑ db:test green). The store stops being a global `/settings`
drill-down reached through a project picker and becomes a **per-project
destination**: a sub-route `/projects/[projectId]/store` reached from a `ý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á`
header icon-chip (after `ýπÅý∏úý∏ôý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏´ý∏≤`), exactly like `ý∏ïý∏≤ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏áý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô` / `ýπÅý∏úý∏ôý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏´ý∏≤`.

**Test-first** (RED): `tests/unit/store-project-surface.test.tsx` ‚Äî pins the
testable contract (`storeHref` builder, `STORE_LABEL = "ý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á"`, the settings tab
drops `/store`, the projects tab lights on `/projects/[id]/store`, StoreManager
`hidePicker` hides the picker). 4 RED ‚Üí green.

**App:**

- New route `app/projects/[projectId]/store/page.tsx` mirroring
  `schedule/page.tsx`: own `DetailHeader` (back ‚Üí project), gated to
  `WP_DETAIL_ROLES`, projectId from the route (RLS scopes the viewer), renders
  the existing `StoreManager` with `hidePicker`. `canIssue = SITE_STAFF` (admits
  site_admin's ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å; procurement stays read-only); P&L stays super/director.
- `app/projects/[projectId]/page.tsx`: `canSeeStore = WP_DETAIL_ROLES.includes(role)`
  - a `Warehouse` `ý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á` chip after `ýπÅý∏úý∏ôý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏´ý∏≤`.
- `StoreManager` gains an optional `hidePicker` prop (default false) ‚Äî the
  per-project route supplies the project, so the global picker is suppressed.
- `labels.ts`: `STORE_LABEL` changed `"ý∏™ýπÇý∏ïý∏£ýπåý∏´ý∏ôýπâý∏≤ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô"` ‚Üí **`"ý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á"`** (the
  destination SSOT, used by chip + sub-route header).
- `project-paths.ts`: new `storeHref(projectId)` builder.
- Removed the two `/store` `SettingsLink`s (procurement + isManager blocks) +
  the now-orphan `Warehouse` / `STORE_LABEL` imports in `settings/page.tsx`.
- Legacy `/store` ‚Üí thin `redirect("/projects")`.
- `bottom-tab-bar.tsx`: `SETTINGS_TAB.match` drops `/store` (the projects tab
  owns the new sub-route; `/projects` prefix already lights it). `/stock-count`
  kept ‚Äî retired in U2.
- `nav-back-affordance.test.ts`: legacy `/store` moved to EXCLUDED (now a
  redirect). **Also swept in 4 pre-existing-unclassified spec-196 accounting
  routes** (ledger/payables/periods/purchases) that had left this anti-drift
  completeness test RED ‚Äî same test file, kept the suite green.

**DB:** mig 20260813000700 ‚Äî `record_stock_in` definer widened to admit
`site_admin` (was BACK_OFFICE tier only). `CREATE OR REPLACE`, **same signature**
(uuid,uuid,numeric,numeric,uuid,text) so grants + pgTAP positional pins hold;
body reconstructed verbatim from LIVE pg_proc, the only edited line is the role
gate `in (...)` set. pgTAP 181 +1 (plan 24‚Üí25): a site_admin member records a
ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ into their own project. No `db:types` (signature unchanged).

**Scope held:** placement + access-widening only; no new store capability. U2
(unify ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏àý∏ôý∏±ý∏ö, retire `/stock-count`) and U3 (empty-ý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á state) NOT started.

**Open / deferred:** the legacy `/store` `actions.ts` still `revalidatePath("/store")`
(harmless ‚Äî `router.refresh()` drives the per-project route UI; left untouched
per scope). Action gates beyond ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤/ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å (ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏àý∏ôý∏±ý∏ö widening to site_admin) land
naturally in U2 when the count surface unifies.

## Spec 195 follow-up ‚Äî dashboard store-material spend (2026-06-24)

Status: **DONE ‚Äî 2026-06-24** (no DB; lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ vitest 1575 green). Closes
the "Open follow-up" flagged in `docs/feature-specs/195-project-level-purchasing.md`:
the manager dashboard's materials-by-project showed direct WP-bound purchase spend
only, so a project that procures heavily **into the on-site store** read as
artificially low (the cost is real ‚Äî it sits in `wp_profit` + Store P&L, just not
on the dashboard). **Fix:** `materials` now adds the project's non-reversed
`stock_issues` (ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å) at **cost** (`total_cost`). Disjoint sources ‚Äî store-bound
material comes from WP-less PRs, which `sumMaterials` excludes (ADR 0063) ‚Äî so
additive with no double-count (mirrors `wp_profit`'s two-term materials, but at COST
not the sell layer: the dashboard is budget-vs-**money-out**, like labor-at-cost and
purchases-at-supplier-amount; the store's internal transfer margin is not external
spend). New pure helper `sumStoreIssues` (`src/lib/dashboard/spend.ts`, tested) +
two admin reads (`stock_issues` by project_id, `stock_reversals` for the non-reversed
filter) in `src/app/dashboard/page.tsx`. Test-first (dashboard-spend.test.ts).
**Open:** dashboard total ignores store stock procured-but-not-yet-ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å'd (inventory,
not yet WP-consumed) ‚Äî deliberate, consistent with the cost-at-usage model.

## Spec 194 ‚Äî super_admin override on an approved supply plan (2026-06-24)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-24** (commit 58dc371; mig 20260813000100; pgTAP
209; lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ vitest 1567 ¬∑ db:test 159/0 green). Operator: a super_admin
should be able to revert the status / edit an approved (frozen) supply plan, with
the plan labeled "overridden by [name]" (chose **supply plans first** via
AskUserQuestion). The lifecycle is one-way past approval (PM submit ‚Üí PD/super
approve ‚Üí frozen; only rejected re-editable); this is the operator escape hatch.
**mig:** `supply_plans` += overridden_by / overridden_at; `reopen_supply_plan(plan)`
definer ‚Äî **super_admin only** ‚Äî reopens a submitted/approved plan to draft
(clearing the submit/approve stamps), recording overridden_by + overridden_at; the
marker PERSISTS through a later re-approval. **pgTAP 209** (super reopens, non-super
42501, draft can't reopen, execute lockdown). `reopenPlan` action + a "ýπÄý∏õý∏¥ý∏îýπÅý∏ÅýπâýπÑý∏Ç
(ý∏úý∏πýπâý∏îý∏πýπÅý∏•ý∏£ý∏∞ý∏öý∏ö)" button on a frozen plan (canOverride = super) + a "¬∑ ý∏õý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÅý∏ÅýπâýπÇý∏îý∏¢ [name]"
marker (name resolved via admin client). The pattern (reopen RPC + overridden_by
stamp + label) is reusable for other approved entities. **PAIRED REQUEST #1 ‚Äî supply
plan ‚Üí store redesign ‚Äî NOT built (design proposal presented, awaiting the
operator's direction):** the operator wants supply-plan items to procure into the
on-site STORE (project-level, no WP), then WPs ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å (withdraw) per-WP ‚Äî moving WP
attribution to withdrawal (the store already does the ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å half, spec 177). Key
fork = how procurement lands in the store (light: plan ‚Üí pre-filled stock-in; full:
project-level PRs ‚Üí PO ‚Üí receive-into-store, needs PR work_package_id nullable).

## Spec 193 ‚Äî in-app feedback (bug report / feature request) (2026-06-24)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-24** (commit d7b7b0d; mig 20260813000000; pgTAP
208; lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ vitest 1564 ¬∑ db:test 158/0 green; /feedback compiles +
gates unauth ‚Üí /login, preview). Operator: add a bug-report / feature-request
channel in settings, **designed so CC has enough to fix or build without a
round-trip** (chose the guided single details field via AskUserQuestion). A
ý∏Ñý∏ßý∏≤ý∏°ý∏äýπàý∏ßý∏¢ýπÄý∏´ý∏•ý∏∑ý∏≠ row in /settings ‚Üí /feedback (settings sub-surface, getClaims so every
authenticated role can file): type toggle (ýπÅý∏àýπâý∏áý∏õý∏±ý∏çý∏´ý∏≤ / ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏üý∏µýπÄý∏àý∏≠ý∏£ýπå) + title + ONE
details box whose placeholder swaps by type (bug: did ‚Üí expected ‚Üí actual; feature:
what + why) + optional "related screen". **The design point ‚Äî auto-attach the triage
context the user shouldn't type:** role_snapshot (most bugs are role/RLS-gated),
app_version (package.json, server-side), user_agent (mobile vs desktop), best-effort
page_path. **mig:** feedback table + feedback_type / feedback_status enums; writes
via submit_feedback definer (stamps submitted_by + role_snapshot); RLS = submitter
reads own, super_admin reads all (operator + CC's read role). **pgTAP 208** (submit,
role-stamp, RLS scoping, execute lockdown). Files: lib/feedback/validate.ts (pure),
app/feedback/{page,actions}.ts, components/features/feedback/feedback-form.tsx,
settings row. Guards: feature-components-structure (+feedback domain),
nav-back-affordance (+feedback STATIC_DETAIL). **CC reads submissions via
`pnpm exec supabase db query --linked` on the feedback table** (full per-row
context). **Deferred:** in-app super_admin review list ¬∑ screenshot attachment ¬∑
status-update RPC + a Telegram ping on new feedback.

buttons need a global "pick a ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô" picker (v1 surfaces the actions per-WP instead).
**Spec 192 = U1 membership guard + U2 onboarding + U3 non-member explainer + U4 SA

## Spec 192 ‚Äî first-real-project readiness, U2+U3 (2026-06-24)

Status: **U2 + U3 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-24** (commits 93225df, 14b842e; NO DB;
lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ vitest 1549 green). Continuing the adoption arc after U1. **U2 ‚Äî
onboarding leads with the team:** the setup checklist listed "ýπÄý∏ûý∏¥ýπàý∏°ý∏óý∏µý∏°ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô" third with
no rationale, but team is the access prerequisite (a project is only visible to its
members) ‚Äî moved it FIRST + a one-line why ("ýπÄý∏ûý∏¥ýπàý∏°ý∏Åýπàý∏≠ý∏ôýπÄý∏õýπáý∏ôý∏≠ý∏±ý∏ôý∏îý∏±ý∏öýπÅý∏£ý∏Å ýπÄý∏ûý∏∑ýπàý∏≠ýπÉý∏´ýπâý∏ûý∏ßý∏ÅýπÄý∏Çý∏≤
ýπÄý∏´ýπáý∏ôýπÇý∏Ñý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ýπÅý∏•ý∏∞ýπÄý∏£ý∏¥ýπàý∏°ý∏óý∏≥ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ôýπÑý∏îýπâ"); rows gained an optional `hint`. **U3 ‚Äî non-member
explainer, not a bare 404:** when the user session can't see a project, an
admin-client exists-check distinguishes RLS-hidden (not a member) from truly-gone ‚Äî
gone still 404s, hidden renders `NoAccessNotice` ("ý∏Ñý∏∏ý∏ìý∏¢ý∏±ý∏áýπÑý∏°ýπàýπÑý∏îýπâý∏≠ý∏¢ý∏πýπàýπÉý∏ôý∏óý∏µý∏°ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏áýπÇý∏Ñý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£
ý∏ôý∏µýπâ ‚Äî ý∏ïý∏¥ý∏îý∏ïýπàý∏≠ý∏úý∏πýπâý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ýπÇý∏Ñý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ýπÄý∏ûý∏∑ýπàý∏≠ý∏Çý∏≠ýπÄý∏ûý∏¥ýπàý∏°ýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ý∏óý∏µý∏°") in the standard chrome with a back
link (super_admin / coordinator see-all, never reach it). Tests:
`onboarding-checklist.test.tsx` (+1), `no-access-notice.test.tsx`. **U4 ‚Äî site-admin
daily home: DESIGN APPROVED (mock-first, show_widget ‚Üí AskUserQuestion: build it +
make it the SA's landing), NOT YET BUILT.** It's a central nav rework (roleHome +
SA_TABS + SA_HUB_NAV + 4 pinned nav tests) ‚Üí its own focused unit; full plan in
`docs/feature-specs/192-first-project-readiness.md` ¬ßU4.

## Spec 192 ‚Äî first-real-project readiness, U1 (2026-06-24)

Status: **U1 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-24** (commit 69d94b0, NO DB; lint ¬∑ typecheck
¬∑ vitest 1547 green). After dark mode the operator said "decide and proceed" ‚Äî I
pivoted from feature-breadth to **adoption** (the app is feature-complete but
operator-incomplete; ~0 real data in prod, so the machinery is hollow without a
first real project producing data). Two cliffs mapped from the live code; U1 fixes
the **silent membership dead-end**: project visibility is membership-gated
(`can_see_project` = a `project_members` row OR the project_lead, ADR 0056), and
`removeProjectMember` had **no guard** ‚Äî a PM could remove the last member (or
themselves) and orphan the project (visible only to super_admin), with no in-app
recovery for the locked-out person. **Fix (invariant: a project keeps ‚â•1 member):**
`lib/projects/member-removal.ts` pure `evaluateMemberRemoval({totalMembers,
removingSelf})` ‚Üí `{blocked (last), needsConfirm (self + others remain)}`;
`removeProjectMember` counts first and refuses the last removal (load-bearing Thai
error); `settings-form.tsx` (+`currentUserId` prop) disables the last member's ‚úï
with a hint, routes a self-removal through the themed `ConfirmDialog`
("‚Ä¶ý∏Ñý∏∏ý∏ìý∏àý∏∞ýπÑý∏°ýπàýπÄý∏´ýπáý∏ôýπÇý∏Ñý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ý∏ôý∏µýπâý∏≠ý∏µý∏Å‚Ä¶"), keeps other-removal one-click, marks the current
user "(ý∏Ñý∏∏ý∏ì)". **NO migration** ‚Äî an action-level UX-footgun guard at the right
altitude (member ops are direct table writes under RLS, not RPCs; projects are
never hard-deleted, so no DELETE-cascade concern a DB trigger would hit). Tests:
`member-removal.test.ts` + 3 `settings-form.test.tsx` cases. **U2+ (next):**
onboarding leads with "add your team" (+ the visibility why); a non-member 404 ‚Üí
"ask a PM to add you" explainer (admin exists-check); a **site-admin daily-loop
home** (log labor/photos/PR in one tap) ‚Äî net-new surface, wants a mock-first pass.

## Spec 190 ‚Äî dark mode (opt-in night theme), U1 (2026-06-24)

Status: **U1 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-24** (commit 40358c6, NO DB; lint ¬∑ typecheck
¬∑ vitest 1528 green; verified light+dark via the SSR cookie path + preview
screenshots). Operator asked "how hard is dark mode in settings?" ‚Üí I assessed it
against the actual token system and recommended **default light + opt-in toggle**
(operator confirmed). Cheap because the design system was built for re-theming:
100% semantic tokens (enforced by `design-doctrine.test.ts`), OKLCH colors (dark =
invert the L channel, hue preserved), and `@custom-variant dark` already declared
(globals.css even comments "a future night-shift palette overrides :root"). So the
lift is a `.dark` palette + toggle plumbing ‚Äî **components are untouched.**
**Default LIGHT is deliberate** (spec-20 sun-first: dark is _worse_ in direct sun =
the field photo-capture flow; dark serves indoor/PM/back-office/portal/night). **U1:**
`lib/ui/theme.ts` (tri-state light/dark/system in a year `theme` cookie; pure
`resolveTheme`/`parseThemeSetting`, default light); no-flash (`layout.tsx` async,
reads the cookie server-side ‚Üí `dark` class + `color-scheme` on `<html>` pre-paint,
`ThemeScript` resolves `system` before paint, `suppressHydrationWarning`);
`globals.css` `.dark {}` overriding every `--color-*` + the shadcn primitives (OKLCH
dark, WCAG floors held, amber fill keeps near-black ink, `*-soft` grounds ‚Üí low-L
tints); `ThemeToggle` on `/settings` (`ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ýπÅý∏™ý∏îý∏áý∏úý∏•`, every role; ý∏™ý∏ßýπàý∏≤ý∏á/ý∏°ý∏∑ý∏î/ý∏£ý∏∞ý∏öý∏ö, 44px,
tracks OS live on ý∏£ý∏∞ý∏öý∏ö). Tests: `theme.test.ts`, `theme-toggle.test.tsx`. **TRAP:
the husky Tailwind class-sorter (`prettier-plugin-tailwindcss`) mangled the
whitespace around a `${ternary}` inside the `className` template literal ‚Äî
`antialiased${c?" dark":""}` became `antialiaseddark` (junk class; dark only
survived via the JS script, not SSR). Fix: keep the separator space in the STATIC
part of the literal (`antialiased ${c?"dark":""}`); re-read className literals after
the commit hook.** No new route; new components in the existing `features/chrome`
domain (no spec-122 change). **U2 (next, optional polish):** screen-by-screen audit
on authenticated surfaces (cards, `*-soft` status grounds, brand band), shadow/
elevation tuning for dark, PWA status-bar `themeColor` per theme.

## Spec 183 ‚Äî approvals are awareness, not a tab (PM) (2026-06-23)

Operator: "ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏à menu should not be the main menu, but rather notification of
how many Approvals are pending." Design challenge ("don't agree blindly"). Verdict:
operator's instinct ~70% right ‚Äî a bottom tab is a _place_, the review queue is a
_transient, often-empty worklist_; the count is the actionable signal. But the
proposal tangles two jobs: **awareness** (count ‚Üí badge/card) and **doing the work**
(the queue needs a real home). Since approving IS the PM's primary job, we demote
the _tab-as-urgency-signal_, not the _queue-as-a-place_: relocate it onto the
dashboard (the new PM home) + count badge on the ý∏Ýý∏≤ý∏ûý∏£ý∏ßý∏° nav. Operator delegated
placement ("design with ux in mind") and said other approval types get awareness
in later units. Mocked the before/after with show_widget before building.

3-unit arc: U1 count + dashboard hero card ¬∑ U2 drop the tab + reroute home + badge
pins ¬∑ U3 the nav count badge (self-fetching). U4+ = other approval types (PR,
bank/consent) ‚Äî later.

**U1 SHIPPED 2026-06-23 (NO DB).** `src/lib/approvals/pending-summary.ts`: pure
`summarizePendingApprovals(rows, projectsById)` ‚Üí `{count, oldest}` (oldest = min
updated_at, code tiebreak ‚Äî mirrors /review ordering) + async
`getPendingApprovalsSummary` (RLS-scoped, mirrors the /review queue query so count
== list). `features/dashboard/pending-approvals-card.tsx`: presentational, attention
(amber) palette when pending / calm muted when empty, links ‚Üí /review. Wired into
/dashboard for the PM tier only (site_admin sees the dashboard, not the card).
Tests: pending-approvals-summary (pure, 5) + pending-approvals-card (render, 3).
LESSON: a new `features/<domain>/` folder must be added to the
`feature-components-structure.test.ts` ALLOWED_DOMAINS allowlist (spec 122 guard) ‚Äî
caught by the full suite, not the targeted run. vitest 1489/0.

**U2 SHIPPED 2026-06-23 (NO DB).** The nav flip. Dropped `ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏à` from `PM_TABS`
(bottom-tab-bar) + `ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏à` from `PM_HUB_NAV` (hub-nav). Rerouted `roleHome`
PM tier (isManagerRole) ‚Üí `/dashboard` (was `/review`). Gave `DASHBOARD_TAB`
`match: ["/review"]` so the queue lights ý∏Ýý∏≤ý∏ûý∏£ý∏ßý∏° as its sub-surface (bottom bar);
`/review` page passes HubNav `currentHref="/dashboard"` for the same effect on
desktop. `/review` stays a live route reached from the U1 card. Removed the unused
`ClipboardCheck` import. Pin churn (all 5 located up-front): role-home, role-sets,
require-role (redirect target), hub-nav (PM_ITEMS + assertion), bottom-tab-bar
(PM_TABS pin + two ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏à-lighting cases rewritten to ý∏Ýý∏≤ý∏ûý∏£ý∏ßý∏°). The `match` choice
minimized churn AND kept the sun-mode test green (it asserts an active tab exists on
/review ‚Äî ý∏Ýý∏≤ý∏ûý∏£ý∏ßý∏° now provides it). vitest 1489/0; lint¬∑tc green.

**U3 SHIPPED 2026-06-23 (NO DB).** The count badge on the ý∏Ýý∏≤ý∏ûý∏£ý∏ßý∏° bottom tab (PM
tier) ‚Äî the literal "number on the main menu" the operator asked for.
`features/dashboard/pending-approvals-badge.tsx` ('use client'): pure
`formatBadgeCount` (null at 0, cap 99+) + presentational `ApprovalsBadge({count})`

- self-fetching `PendingApprovalsBadge` (browser client, RLS-scoped `work_packages`
  status=pending_approval head-count ‚Äî same visibility as /review; best-effort,
  errors leave it hidden). Wired into bottom-tab-bar over the ý∏Ýý∏≤ý∏ûý∏£ý∏ßý∏° icon, gated
  `tab.href==="/dashboard" && isManagerRole(role)` (site_admin shares the tab, no
  badge ‚Äî doesn't approve). Tests: formatBadgeCount (3) + ApprovalsBadge render (3).
  The self-fetch is inert in jsdom (network fails ‚Üí count 0 ‚Üí renders nothing), so the
  18 existing tab-bar tests stayed green untouched. amber attn pill (bg-attn/on-attn).
  **Desktop hub-strip badge DEFERRED** (HubNav is a server component with no role prop
  ‚Äî threading it to every call site is its own change; the phone bottom bar IS the
  "main menu" the operator named, so this is the core win). vitest +6.

**U4 SHIPPED 2026-06-23 (NO DB).** Desktop hub-strip badge ‚Äî operator "both, one at
a time" (this first). `ApprovalsBadge`/`PendingApprovalsBadge` gained a
`position: "overlay" | "inline"` prop (overlay = the bottom-tab icon overlay,
default, unchanged; inline = `relative ml-1` pill after a text label). `HubNav`
gained an optional `role?: string`; when `isManagerRole(role)` it renders
`<PendingApprovalsBadge position="inline" />` on the `/dashboard` item. Passed
`role` at all 5 HubNav call sites (settings/review/requests/projects/dashboard ‚Äî all
already had ctx.role/role). TRAP: `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` rejects forwarding
`position={undefined}` to an optional prop ‚Üí default it in the wrapper
(`position = "overlay"`). Optional role = call sites that omit it render no badge
(hub-nav component tests pass no role ‚Üí badge inert, stayed green). Test +1 (inline
variant asserts no `absolute`, has `ml-1`).

**SPEC 183 ARC U1‚ÄìU4 SHIPPED 2026-06-23 (one session, NO DB):** count helper +
dashboard hero card ‚Üí drop the ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏à tab + reroute PM home to ý∏Ýý∏≤ý∏ûý∏£ý∏ßý∏° ‚Üí count badge
on the home tab (phone) ‚Üí same badge on the desktop hub strip. ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏à is awareness
(card + badge on both nav surfaces), not a main-menu tab; the queue lives under
ý∏Ýý∏≤ý∏ûý∏£ý∏ßý∏° (/review one tap from the card). DEFERRED: other approval types (PR,
bank/consent ‚Äî NEXT, operator's second "both"); header bell for a unified inbox.

## Spec 184 ‚Äî approval-type awareness (PR + bank changes) (2026-06-23)

Operator's second "both": extend spec-183 awareness to the other PM-tier approval
flows. Explore mapped them: PR = `purchase_requests.status='requested'`, lives on
the ý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ /requests tab; bank-change = `contractor_bank_change_requests.status=
'pending'`, NO nav surface (only on a contractor's detail page = blind spot);
consent = no approval queue (recorded, not approved ‚Üí out of scope). `project_director`
is on all three policies (mig 20260752), so all gate to `isManagerRole`. **Design
rule: a tabbed flow gets a tab badge; a tabless flow gets a dashboard card.** So WP
(183) = badge+card, PR = ý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ tab badge, bank-change = dashboard card (U2).

(`loadPendingWpApprovals`, `loadPendingPurchaseDecisions`); `PendingApprovalsBadge`
(WP) kept as a thin wrapper (ý∏Ýý∏≤ý∏ûý∏£ý∏ßý∏° wiring unchanged) + new
`PendingPurchaseDecisionsBadge` (label "ý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏ûý∏¥ý∏àý∏≤ý∏£ý∏ìý∏≤"). `ApprovalsBadge` gained an
optional `label` (aria noun, default "ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏à"; local `label`‚Üí`text` to avoid the
collision). Wired onto the ý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ tab (bottom-tab over the icon) + /requests hub item
(inline), gated `href==="/requests" && isManagerRole(role)` ‚Äî SA/procurement share the
tab but don't decide ‚Üí no badge. Module-level loaders = stable refs, so the `[load]`
effect runs once. Test +1 (custom label aria). vitest 1497/0; lint¬∑tc green.

**U2 SHIPPED 2026-06-23 (NO DB).** Bank-change dashboard card ‚Äî the blind-spot flow
(contractor bank changes had NO nav surface, only a contractor's detail page).
`src/lib/approvals/pending-bank-changes.ts`: `getPendingBankChangeCount` (RLS-scoped
head-count of `contractor_bank_change_requests` status='pending'; error‚Üí0).
`features/dashboard/bank-change-awareness-card.tsx`: presentational, renders ONLY
when count>0 (exception-driven ‚Äî unlike the always-on ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏à card, bank changes are
occasional, so no zero-state clutter), amber attn, links ‚Üí /contacts/subcontractors
to drill in and decide. Wired into /dashboard under the ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏à card, PM tier only.
Tests: card render (nothing at 0; count+link at >0). Typecheck confirmed the table +
'pending' status in the generated types. vitest +2.

**SPEC 184 ARC COMPLETE U1‚ÄìU2 2026-06-23 (NO DB):** PR awaiting-decision badge on the
ý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ tab + bank-change awareness card on the dashboard. With spec 183, the PM now
sees all three PM-tier approval flows without hunting (WP card+badge, PR tab badge,
bank-change card). Design rule held: tabbed flow ‚Üí tab badge, tabless flow ‚Üí dashboard
card. DEFERRED: dedicated bank-change queue page (vs drilling from the contractor

## Spec 185 ‚Äî one number for all approvals (unified pending on the home) (2026-06-23)

Operator: "proceed" ‚Üí "design the process yourself." Capstone of the awareness arc.
Original ask (183) was "how many APPROVALS are pending" ‚Äî a single total. Rejected a
header bell: this app has NO global header (chrome = BottomTabBar + HubNav, pages
render their own/none), so a bell has no consistent home. Instead fold the total into
the existing idiom: ý∏Ýý∏≤ý∏ûý∏£ý∏ßý∏° badge = total, dashboard = complete inbox.

**U1 SHIPPED 2026-06-23 (NO DB).** Complete the dashboard inbox + generalize the card.

(renders only when count>0, attention palette, ArrowRight) and REMOVED the
spec-184 `bank-change-awareness-card.tsx` (+ its test) ‚Äî subsumed. Added
`src/lib/approvals/pending-purchase-decisions.ts` `getPendingPurchaseDecisionCount`
(server, RLS head-count of `purchase_requests`='requested'; error‚Üí0). Dashboard (PM
tier) now renders, under the ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏à hero: PR card (‚Üí/requests, ShoppingCart) + bank
card (‚Üí/contacts/subcontractors, Landmark), via the generic card. Test: AwarenessCard
(nothing at 0; count+label+link at >0). Net vitest 1499 (‚àí2 bank test, +2 awareness).

**U2 SHIPPED 2026-06-23 (NO DB).** ý∏Ýý∏≤ý∏ûý∏£ý∏ßý∏° badge = the TOTAL. Added pure
`sumApprovalCounts(values)` (null‚Üí0) + a `loadPendingBankChanges` client loader +
`loadTotalPendingApprovals` (Promise.all the 3 head-counts ‚Üí sum). `PendingApprovalsBadge`
now loads the total + label "ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏≠ý∏ôý∏∏ý∏°ý∏±ý∏ïý∏¥" (was WP-only "ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏à"); `PendingPurchaseDecisionsBadge`
stays PR-only on ý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠. So the number on the main menu = all pending approvals, and
the dashboard breakdown (ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏à hero + PR + bank cards, U1) reconciles to it. Test +2
(sumApprovalCounts). vitest 1501/0; lint¬∑tc green.

**SPEC 185 ARC COMPLETE U1‚ÄìU2 2026-06-23 (NO DB):** the capstone ‚Äî one number (ý∏Ýý∏≤ý∏ûý∏£ý∏ßý∏°
badge = WP+PR+bank total, "ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏≠ý∏ôý∏∏ý∏°ý∏±ý∏ïý∏¥") + the dashboard as the complete inbox. **APPROVALS-
AWARENESS ARC (183+184+185) COMPLETE:** ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏à went from a main-menu tab ‚Üí a total count
on the home + a per-type dashboard inbox + per-surface tab badges, across all three
PM-tier approval flows. DEFERRED: bank-change queue page; worker-DC bank changes (ADR
0062 U4c-2 unbuilt).

## Spec 174 ‚Äî project Google-Maps link (precise pin) (2026-06-22)

Operator: "Add Pinned map, or attach link from gMap." Chose (one question)
**attach a gMap link** ‚Äî paste the Maps "Share" URL on the project; the ‚ìò sheet's
"open in Maps" link opens the EXACT pin, replacing the spec-173 address-search
fallback. (Embedded pinned map declined ‚Äî needs an API key + coordinates.)

**SHIPPED to prod 2026-06-22.** **mig 20260798:** `projects.gmap_url` (nullable) +
CHECK https-only; DROP+CREATE `update_project_settings` with a trailing
`p_gmap_url` (mirrors site_address: null preserves, '' clears) + re-applied EXECUTE
grants. **mig 20260799:** grant SELECT on the column ‚Äî `projects` has COLUMN-level
SELECT grants (budget excluded), so the new column did NOT inherit SELECT; granted
authenticated + anon (mirrors site_address). **mig 20260800:** revoke anon EXECUTE
on the recreated RPC ‚Äî Supabase default privileges silently re-granted anon EXECUTE
on the DROP+CREATE'd function; the pre-174 RPC had anon revoked (pgTAP 32 pins it).
App: `validateGmapUrl` (https + Google host allowlist ‚Äî rejects javascript:,
non-https, look-alike domains, >2048); the form gains a URL field; `gmapUrl` rides
the single settings payload; the project detail page computes
`mapsUrl = gmap_url ?? address-search ?? null` for the existing ‚ìò link (no
component change). **LESSONS (both caught by db:test, fixed forward):** (1) a new
column on a column-SELECT-granted table does NOT inherit SELECT ‚Üí must grant
explicitly; (2) DROP+CREATE of an RPC re-triggers Supabase's default-privilege
EXECUTE grant to anon ‚Üí must revoke anon (CREATE OR REPLACE would have avoided
both, but a new param forces DROP+CREATE). pgTAP 32 (+3, 11-arg sig); unit
validateGmapUrl + settings-form (+1 each). db:types regenerated (signature change).
Verify: db:test 120 / 2301 / 0; pnpm test green; lint¬∑typecheck¬∑build green. Builds
on [[spec173-procurement-project-visibility]].

## Spec 173 ‚Äî procurement read-only project visibility (2026-06-22)

Operator: "Procurement needs to see Project information and schedule." Clarified
(two questions): besides the schedule, procurement also needs the deliverables
(ý∏áý∏ßý∏îý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô) list, to open WP details, and the project info fields (team, PM, dates,
Google-Maps link, address, status). Audit: procurement already reads /projects +
/projects/[id] + the ‚ìò sheet (spec 102), but a spec-102 early-return branch gave
it a minimal names+status WP list, and `deliverables` / `work_package_dependencies`
/ `project_members` SELECT had no procurement arm ‚Üí empty ý∏áý∏ßý∏î grouping, empty
schedule swimlanes/deps, empty team list.

**SHIPPED to prod 2026-06-22.** **U1 (RLS, mig `20260797000000`):** procurement
OR-arm added to `deliverables` + `work_package_dependencies` SELECT (kept the
`can_see_project` / `can_see_wp` predicate ‚Äî files 70/73 pins hold) and appended to
the flat `project_members` staff role-list (team-name resolution; project_director
kept, file 91). Read-only widening, no write arm. New pgTAP file 173 (+12):
procurement reads cross-project ý∏áý∏ßý∏î + dependency + members; a membership-scoped PM
still sees only its own; quals still name the helpers. **U2 (schedule):** new
`SCHEDULE_VIEW_ROLES` (= SITE_STAFF + procurement, coordinator still excluded per
spec 154) gates `/projects/[id]/schedule` + the calendar chip (`canOpenSchedule`).
**U3 (WP nav):** `canOpenWp` re-expressed as `WP_DETAIL_ROLES.includes(role)` so
procurement's WP rows link to the (read-only, spec 171) detail. **U2+U3 removed the
spec-102 procurement early-return branch** ‚Äî procurement now flows through the main
project page, every write affordance already `isPmRole`/`isManagerRole`-gated and
`WorkPackageList` role-agnostic (procurement gets the ý∏áý∏ßý∏îý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô lens by default).
**U4 (project info):** the ‚ìò `ProjectInfoButton` sheet gains ý∏™ý∏ñý∏≤ý∏ôý∏∞ (status),
ý∏Åý∏≥ý∏´ý∏ôý∏îý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ (start + planned-completion dates, formatThaiDate), and a ýπÄý∏õý∏¥ý∏îýπÉý∏ô Google
Maps link derived from `site_address` (no geo columns ‚Üí address search URL); status
is always present so the sheet always renders. New const unit-tested
(`SCHEDULE_VIEW_ROLES`); ProjectInfoButton test extended (+2). Verify: db:test
**120 files / 2298 / 0**; pnpm test green; lint¬∑typecheck¬∑build green. RLS-only
migration ‚Üí db:types regen skipped. Out of scope: project reports/settings/budget
(stay PM/super/director; budget admin-only), any procurement WRITE, geo/embedded
map, coordinator schedule access (spec 154 preserved). Builds on
[[spec171-procurement-purchase-requests]] [[spec172-procurement-back-office-domains]].

## Spec 172 ‚Äî procurement owns contractors / equipment / DC onboarding (2026-06-21)

Operator: "procurement also takes care of 1. Contractors 2. Equipment Rentals 3. Onboarding DCs." Decisions: FULL ownership (procurement **sets DC pay rate** +
**manages contractor bank**), build all three. **Phase A (equipment) = ALREADY
ENABLED, no-op:** audit found procurement already gated into every equipment
surface (registry CRUD/movements/rates/rental-batch+allocation+usage RPCs +
/equipment via EQUIPMENT_MOVE_ROLES/BACK_OFFICE_ROLES, spec 141/146). The only gap
is app-wide (no UI to view/manage rental batches+allocations) ‚Äî a SEPARATE feature,
not a procurement-permission item. **Phase B (contractors incl. bank) SHIPPED to
prod (commit 8f431bc, mig `20260795000000`):** procurement now curates contractors
like suppliers ‚Äî RLS adds procurement to contractors INSERT/UPDATE; CREATE OR
REPLACE `set_contact_bank` (staff bank-write) + `set_work_package_contractor`
adding procurement, **bodies sourced from the LIVE prod definition** (project_director
rides along, file 91). App: `/contacts/subcontractors` + the back-office branch of
`/contacts/[type]/[id]` (contractors+suppliers) gate on BACK_OFFICE_ROLES;
createContractorRecord/updateContractorRecord/setContactBank ‚Üí backOfficeSession;
the PM-only detail workflows (bank-change approval, doc upload, portal invite,
consent) gated to isPmTier so procurement gets a clean read+bank surface without
broken affordances; `contact_bank` stays zero-grant (admin-read behind the gate).

  session had settled (origin/main at the Phase B docs commit, tree clean, no fresh

  `set_worker_day_rate` adding `procurement` to each role gate ‚Äî **every body
  re-sourced from the LIVE prod definition** (pg_get_functiondef), signatures
  UNCHANGED so the authenticated-only EXECUTE lockdown (pgTAP 36) is preserved and
  db:types needs no regen; project_director rides along in every list (file 91).
  Bank/tax/phone/day_rate are written through these definer bodies (zero
  column-grant bypassed); the READ path stays admin-only ‚Äî this opens onboarding,
  not PII. App: new `WORKER_ROSTER_ROLES` (= PM_ROLES + procurement) gates
  `/workers`; nav wired (the spec-171-U4 lesson) ‚Äî ý∏óý∏µý∏°ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô ‚Üí `/workers` added to
  PROCUREMENT_HUB_NAV (desktop) + the `role==='procurement'` /settings block (phone

## Spec 171 ‚Äî procurement can make purchase requests from the WP screen (2026-06-21)

Status: **U1 + U2 COMPLETE ‚Äî shipped to prod 2026-06-21.** Operator: "there are
times procurement needs to make a purchase request instead of the site admins ‚Äî
enable them to see [the WP] just like site admins, but only able to edit the PRs."
Clarified: procurement **opens the work-package screen** read-only, only the
request is editable. Requests are created only in WP context (`work_package_id`
NOT NULL); that screen was `SITE_STAFF_ROLES`-gated (procurement excluded, spec
70). procurement is **cross-project** (spec 102 / ADR 0056) ‚Üí `can_see_wp()` is
false for it, so it could neither read the WP's photos/labour/approvals (pure
`can_see_wp` SELECTs) nor insert a request (the INSERT also gates on
`can_see_wp`). **U1 (migration `20260780000000`, commit af754b9):** added a
`current_user_role() = 'procurement'` arm BESIDE the existing `can_see_wp` arm on
`photo_logs` / `labor_logs` (staff) / `approvals` SELECT and on the
`purchase_requests` INSERT policy (DROP+CREATE in place, names unchanged ‚Üí files
70/73 `can_see_wp` pins hold). No UPDATE (can't approve/decide), no other writes;
column-scope INSERT grant already to `authenticated`. **Bug caught by db:test +
fixed-forward:** the PR-insert arm named `project_manager` without
`project_director` (I copied the role list from the pre-director migration file,
not the live post-director policy) ‚Üí pgTAP file 91 "no RLS policy names
project_manager without project_director" failed; added `project_director` and
re-applied the policy to prod. **TDD:** file 17 E.6 flipped (procurement INSERT
`throws 42501` ‚Üí `lives_ok`); new file **115** pins the four arms (procurement +
can_see_wp present). **db:test 115 files / 2201 / 0.** db:types = no drift (RLS
only). **U2 (commit e378d30):** admit procurement via `WP_DETAIL_ROLES`
(SITE_STAFF_ROLES + procurement) + `isReadOnlyWpViewer(role)` (the single
read-only flag the page branches on); photos render the read-only `PhaseGallery`
(extracted from /review into `components/features/photos/phase-gallery.tsx`,
behaviour-preserving) not the capture zone; labour `locked + showFlags=false`;
notes static; site-purchase / contractor-assign / assign-prompt / report-defect
suppressed; ý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ stays planner-only. **TDD:** role-sets.test.ts pins
WP_DETAIL_ROLES + isReadOnlyWpViewer (RED first). lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ vitest 201 /
1336 ¬∑ build green. **U3 (commit 1a990be):** added `procurement` to `contractors`
SELECT (mig `20260781000000`) so the WP's assigned-contractor name shows in
procurement's read-only info sheet ‚Äî contractors is global master data (plain
role-level read, like the suppliers master); no app change (page already renders
it read-only). file 115 +1, db:test **115 / 2202 / 0**. **U4 (commit 9776c38) ‚Äî
the "procurement still can't access WP details" fix:** root cause was
NAVIGATION, not permission ‚Äî spec 102's separate read-only procurement project
view rendered WP rows as plain cards (no link), predating U2's read-only detail
access. Made each row a `<Link>` to `workPackageHref`; no DB change. Landed amid a
concurrent payroll/DC-payment session ([[concurrent-session-hazard]]): shared HEAD
was on its feature branch + its WIP left the tree mid-compile, so the one-file fix
was committed in isolation and ff-pushed to main (their branch/tree untouched).
**Editing an existing request's content is out of scope** (no such feature for any
role ‚Äî site admins only create).

Operator: hide ý∏öý∏±ý∏çý∏äý∏µ (GL) from PMs for beta, keep ý∏Ñýπàý∏≤ý∏àýπâý∏≤ý∏á (payroll). **NO DB change** ‚Äî GL tables are zero-grant, read via admin client behind requireRole, so the route guard is the gate. Tightened `ACCOUNTING_ROLES` [accounting, pm, super, director] ‚Üí **[accounting, super_admin]** (auto-tightens all 4 /accounting route guards ‚Äî PM/director now bounce) + gated the settings ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ýπÄý∏áý∏¥ý∏ô ‚Üí ý∏öý∏±ý∏çý∏äý∏µ link to ACCOUNTING_ROLES (ý∏Ñýπàý∏≤ý∏àýπâý∏≤ý∏á/payroll stays under isManager). Reversible (re-add the 2 roles ‚Üí link + guards follow). **TDD:** RED first ‚Äî role-sets.test.ts pins `ACCOUNTING_ROLES === [accounting, super_admin]` (+ not-contains pm/director). **Verify:** lint¬∑typecheck¬∑**vitest 200/1324**¬∑**build green** (no db:test ‚Äî no DB change). **Money-surface audit result: Nova operator-only ‚úì ¬∑ GL now operator-only for beta ‚úì ¬∑ project/WP pages leak no money ‚úì ‚Äî core-ops beta surfaces are money-safe.**

## Spec 164 U3 - bulk ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô‚Üíý∏áý∏ßý∏î mapping + ungrouped funnel (2026-06-21)

Status: **COMPLETE ‚Äî 2026-06-21.** The biggest click-saver: map many WPs into a ý∏áý∏ßý∏î at once instead of one-at-a-time on each WP detail. **NO DB change** ‚Äî loops the existing `set_work_package_deliverable` RPC (spec 155; membership-gated, PM is a member ‚Üí can_see_wp passes). `assignWorkPackagesToDeliverable(projectId, workPackageIds[], deliverableId)` action (PM_ROLES gate, uuid-validate/filter, sequential RPC loop, 42501/22023 map + mid-batch "ý∏¢ýπâý∏≤ý∏¢ýπÑý∏îýπâ N ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô" report). `GroupWorkPackagesSheet` (target ý∏áý∏ßý∏î `<select>` + ýπÄý∏•ý∏∑ý∏≠ý∏Åý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏´ý∏°ý∏î + scrollable WP checkbox list, each `aria-label="code name"` + "ý∏¢ýπâý∏≤ý∏¢ N ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô" ‚Üí action ‚Üí refresh; Set preserves selection order). Amber funnel banner (`bg-attn-soft`/`border-attn-edge`/`text-attn-ink`) in DeliverablesManager ("N ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ôý∏¢ý∏±ý∏áýπÑý∏°ýπàý∏≠ý∏¢ý∏πýπàýπÉý∏ôý∏áý∏ßý∏î" + ý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏Åý∏•ý∏∏ýπàý∏°ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô trigger) shown when deliverables exist AND ungrouped WPs > 0; ungrouped list derived in page.tsx from the loaded WPs. **TDD:** RED first ‚Äî `group-work-packages-sheet.test.tsx` (4: disabled-until-ý∏áý∏ßý∏î+ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô; assign checked‚Üí`(p1,[w1,w2],d1)`+refresh; select-all checks all; inline error no-refresh). **Verify:** lint¬∑typecheck¬∑**vitest 196/1309** (no db:test ‚Äî RPC unchanged). **NEXT: U4 onboarding nudge (checklist item + banner).** Lesson: wrapping-label text content drops CSS-gap whitespace ‚Üí put explicit `aria-label="code name"` on each checkbox so getByLabelText (and screen readers) get the spaced name.

## Spec 164 U1 - create ý∏áý∏ßý∏îý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô in-app (2026-06-21)

Status: **COMPLETE ‚Äî 2026-06-21.** Root fix for "nowhere to add deliverables": `create_deliverable` was deferred (ADR 0059 ¬ß1) so ý∏áý∏ßý∏î only ever came from `seed-deliverables.sql`. U1 adds the in-app door. **DB:** `create_deliverable(p_project_id,p_code,p_name) returns uuid` (migration 20260774000000, pushed) ‚Äî SECURITY DEFINER, gate PM/super/director (mirrors `set_work_package_deliverable`), validate code‚â§50/name‚â§200 non-empty (22023), project exists (22023), `sort_order=coalesce(max,0)+1`, dup (project_id,code)‚Üí23505; grants revoke public/anon + authenticated; no closed-project block (benign metadata). **App:** `validate-new-deliverable.ts` + `createDeliverable` action (PM_ROLES gate, 23505/42501/22023 error map) + `AddDeliverableSheet` (mirror AddWorkPackageSheet) + `DeliverablesManager` "ý∏áý∏ßý∏îý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô" section on the project page (PM-only/open, lists code¬∑name¬∑N ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô + add button, empty-state) placed above ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô; counts derive from the WP list already loaded (no new query). Operator direction: "both, import-first"; ý∏áý∏ßý∏î NOT in WP sheet ‚Üí creation = paste-list (U2) + manual (U1), mapping = in-app (U3). **TDD:** RED first ‚Äî pgTAP 111 (14: exists/secdef; PM/super/director create + sort_order 1/2/3; dup‚Üí23505; empty/over-long code, empty name, unknown project‚Üí22023; site_admin/visitor‚Üí42501) + `add-deliverable-sheet.test.tsx` (3: disabled-until-valid; create+refresh; inline error). **Verify:** db:push¬∑db:types¬∑**db:test 111 files/2152/0**¬∑lint¬∑typecheck¬∑**vitest 194/1302**. Lessons: deliverables SELECT is membership-scoped (spec 143/ADR 0056) ‚Üí pgTAP readback needs the PM as lead+member (create itself is role-only); plan(N) must equal asserted count; a transient Mgmt-API 502 on file 21 cleared on rerun. **NEXT: U2 bulk-paste ý∏áý∏ßý∏î list ‚Üí U3 bulk ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô‚Üíý∏áý∏ßý∏î mapping + ungrouped funnel ‚Üí U4 onboarding nudge.**

## Spec 163 U1 - paste work packages from Google Sheets (2026-06-21)

Status: **COMPLETE ‚Äî 2026-06-21.** The U7 importer (`parseAndValidate`, `src/lib/wp-import/parse.ts`) now accepts a Google-Sheets cell paste ‚Äî **tab-delimited, no header, code+name** ‚Äî backward-compatible with the comma+`code,name,description` CSV. Auto-detect: `detectDelimiter` (TAB anywhere ‚Üí `\t` else `,`) + `hasHeaderRow` (first non-empty line's first cell == `code`, case-insensitive, BOM-tolerant via `.trim()`) ‚Üí header-by-name vs positional (col1 code / col2 name / col3 description). Both shapes normalise to the same `RawRow[]` ‚Üí the existing validation loop (required code/name, in-file dup, existing-code, 1-based data-row numbers) is UNCHANGED. U7 sheet (`import-work-packages-sheet.tsx`) relabelled for paste-from-sheet ("ý∏ßý∏≤ý∏áý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô" / "ý∏ßý∏≤ý∏áý∏áý∏≤ý∏ôý∏àý∏≤ý∏Å Google Sheet ý∏´ý∏£ý∏∑ý∏≠ CSV" / "ý∏ßý∏≤ý∏áý∏Çýπâý∏≠ý∏°ý∏πý∏•ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô" + tab-example placeholder); stale "ý∏ïýπâý∏≠ý∏áý∏°ý∏µý∏´ý∏±ý∏ßý∏ïý∏≤ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏á" empty-result message in `importWorkPackagesCsv` fixed. NO DB change ‚Äî reuses the `create_work_package` loop. **TDD:** RED first ‚Äî 5 new `wp-import-parse.test.ts` cases (tab/headerless 2-col; 3rd tab col ‚Üí description; header honoured even with tabs; headerless validation row-numbers; headerless trim) + updated `import-work-packages-sheet.test.tsx` copy. **Verify:** lint¬∑typecheck¬∑**test 193 files/1299 pass**. Acceptance: pasting the spec's 262-row tab example creates 262 WPs (operator on-device). Out of scope: parser length validation, status/extra-column import, manual delimiter picker.

## Spec 161 U13 - Nova economy overview on the console (2026-06-21)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-21.** An at-a-glance economy snapshot atop `/nova`. NO DB change. **`summarizeLedger(postings)`** (`src/lib/nova/economy-summary.ts`) ‚Äî pure + tested: `issued` (Œ£ positive), `returned` (Œ£ |negative| = redeemed + confiscated), `outstanding` (the live coin liability = issued ‚àí returned), `holders` (workers with net balance > 0), `bySource` (net per coin_source). The /nova page renders a **ý∏Ýý∏≤ý∏ûý∏£ý∏ßý∏°ýπÄý∏´ý∏£ý∏µý∏¢ý∏ç** card (outstanding ¬∑ ýπÅý∏àý∏ÅýπÅý∏•ýπâý∏ß ¬∑ ýπÉý∏äýπâ/ý∏£ý∏¥ý∏öý∏Ñý∏∑ý∏ô ¬∑ holders + an issued-by-source line) from the ledger it already loads ‚Äî no new query. **TDD:** `nova-economy-summary.test.ts` (4: empty zeros; issued/returned/outstanding; holders count nets per worker; string-amount coercion). **Verify:** lint¬∑typecheck¬∑test + **`pnpm build` green** (full production build ‚Äî 40 static pages, all new /nova routes compile). Final autonomous polish.

## Spec 161 U12 - worker Nova detail + per-worker actions (operator UI) (2026-06-21)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-21.** The operator's per-worker window ‚Äî closes the visibility/action gaps (vesting, redeem, saver-bonus, confiscate had no UI). **`/nova/worker/[id]`** (super_admin, dynamic route ‚Üí auto-classified DETAIL, renders DetailHeader). NO DB change. **Reads use BOTH clients:** table reads (worker, coin_postings ledger, shop_redemptions, coin_confiscations, active shop_items) via the **admin client**; the **gated vesting derives** (`coin_balance`/`coin_unvested_balance`/`coin_vested_balance`/`coin_spendable_balance`) via the **RLS server client** (the super JWT the SECURITY DEFINER gate needs ‚Äî the admin/service-role client has no auth ctx ‚Üí would 42501). Shows the balance breakdown (ý∏¢ý∏≠ý∏î ¬∑ ý∏™ý∏∏ý∏Åý∏áý∏≠ý∏°-theirs ¬∑ ýπÉý∏äýπâýπÑý∏îýπâ-spendable ¬∑ ý∏¢ý∏±ý∏áýπÑý∏°ýπàý∏™ý∏∏ý∏Åý∏áý∏≠ý∏°-at-risk) + ledger/redemptions/confiscations history. **`NovaWorkerActions`** (client): ý∏°ý∏≠ý∏öýπÇý∏öý∏ôý∏±ý∏™ý∏≠ý∏≠ý∏° (`awardSaversBonusAction`), ýπÅý∏•ý∏Åý∏Çý∏≠ý∏áý∏£ý∏≤ý∏áý∏ßý∏±ý∏• (item picker ‚Üí `redeemShopItemAction`), ý∏£ý∏¥ý∏öýπÄý∏´ý∏£ý∏µý∏¢ý∏ç (reason picker + note ‚Üí themed `ConfirmActionButton` ‚Üí `confiscateCoinsAction`). `worker-actions.ts` (super-only, RLS client, conditional params); `confiscation.ts` = reason SSOT (Thai labels). /nova balance rows now link to the detail. **TDD:** `nova-worker-actions.test.tsx` (4: saver bonus call+refresh; redeem chosen item; confiscate chosen reason after themed confirm ‚Üí `(worker,'theft','')`; inline error). **Verify:** lint¬∑typecheck¬∑test (full suite). Verified-by-checklist (auth-gated). **The Nova economy is now fully observable + operable per worker.** NEXT (optional): economy overview/dashboard; worker-facing surfaces (gift-first, later).

## Spec 161 U11 - defect-clawback button on the settlement UI (2026-06-21)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-21.** Makes the U10 clawback operable. NO DB change. `clawBackProjectAction(projectId, note?)` (super-only, RLS server client ‚Üí `claw_back_project_coins`; params built conditionally for `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`). `NovaSettlementList`: a **DISTRIBUTED** project now shows a danger **"ý∏£ý∏¥ý∏öýπÄý∏´ý∏£ý∏µý∏¢ý∏ç (ý∏ïý∏≥ý∏´ý∏ôý∏¥)"** button guarded by the themed **`ConfirmActionButton`/`ConfirmDialog`** (spec 18) ‚Üí clawback + refresh. **First-RED caught the doctrine:** initial `window.confirm` tripped `design-doctrine` ("uses no window.confirm in src" ‚Äî native dialogs break Field-First; destructive actions use the themed dialog) ‚Üí swapped to `ConfirmActionButton`. **TDD:** `nova-settlement-list.test.tsx` (now 6: + claws after confirming the themed dialog ‚Üí `clawBackProjectAction(id)`+refresh; + cancel ‚Üí not called). **Verify:** lint¬∑typecheck¬∑test (full suite). Verified-by-checklist (auth-gated). NEXT = U12 worker Nova detail (vesting view + redeem/saver-bonus/confiscate per worker).

## Spec 161 U10 - per-project defect clawback (ADR 0060 design-rule 1) (2026-06-21)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-21.** Completes the flagged follow-up (a defect-reopen claws back). Migrations `20260773000000_coin_postings_project.sql` + `20260773000100_project_coin_clawback.sql`. **Project tag:** `coin_postings.source_project_id` (nullable FK‚Üíprojects, additive); **`post_coins` gained `p_source_project uuid default null`** (DROP+CREATE ‚Äî a defaulted-param add via create-or-replace would make a 2nd overload + ambiguate the 4-named-arg callers; plpgsql callers bind at runtime so DROP is safe; only pgTAP 96's signature pin needed updating, calls use ‚â§5 positional args). **`distribute_project_coins` now TAGS** its profit_share postings (HT cut + DC shares) with the project. **`claw_back_project_coins(p_project, p_note)`** super-only: per worker, claws `least(project's REMAINING in-tail coins [signed Œ£ of the project's in-window postings ‚Üí nets prior clawbacks ‚Üí idempotent], worker's total `coin_unvested_balance`)` as a negative `confiscation` (reason `defect_rework`, project-tagged) + a `coin_confiscations` row; per-worker advisory lock. **VESTED coins (past the tail) + OTHER projects' coins are never touched** (the trust invariant). **DECISION ‚Äî manual, NOT auto-fired from spec-144 reopen:** (a) a single-WP defect auto-clawing a whole project's distribution is too blunt; (b) reopen is site_admin/PM-callable but minting/forfeiting is super-only ‚Üí an auto-call hits the super gate or forces an ungated post_coins bypass. Operator claws back via this RPC (UI next). **pgTAP 110** (12: column + secdef; pm‚Üí42501; super claws P ‚Üí W1 keeps other-project Q coins [500] but loses P-unvested [1000‚Üíbal 500], W2 P-unvested 800‚Üí0, W3 P-coins VESTED‚Üíuntouched 2000; 2 defect_rework confiscations + 2 project-tagged confiscation postings; idempotent re-run claws 0; unknown‚ÜíP0001). Updated pgTAP 96 (post_coins 6-arg signature). **Verify:** lint¬∑typecheck¬∑db:test 110 files/2138/0¬∑db:types¬∑test. Lesson: adding a defaulted param to a function with named-arg callers needs DROP+CREATE (overload would ambiguate), grep the signature pins.

## Spec 161 hardening - coin-engine fixes from adversarial review (2026-06-21)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-21.** Migration `20260772000000_nova_hardening.sql` (5 create-or-replace, signatures unchanged ‚Üí grants kept; behaviour unchanged for 104-108). Post-arc adversarial review (3 `caveman:cavecrew-reviewer` agents over the engine SQL + UI) surfaced three real fixes (noise filtered: negative weights impossible via `nova_dials value>=0` CHECK; "vesting locks forever" was a backwards misread ‚Äî tail=0 ‚Üí fully vested; UI was clean). **A ‚Äî `settle_project`:** a MISSING `coin_multiplier` dial silently produced a NULL pool ‚Üí now guards ‚Üí P0001 (the dial is seeded + RPC-update-only, but a deleted dial must fail loud). **B ‚Äî `distribute_project_coins`:** per-worker `round()` drifted ‚Äî Œ£ shares could over-mint or lose coins ‚Üí the LAST worker (by worker_id, via `row_number()/count() over()`) now takes the **exact remainder** (`v_dist ‚àí Œ£ allocated`) so **Œ£(minted) == pool EXACTLY**, never over-mints. **C ‚Äî TOCTOU race:** `redeem_shop_item`/`confiscate_coins`/`award_savers_bonus` did balance-check-then-post; two concurrent calls could both pass ‚Üí overspend/double-confiscate/double-bonus. Added a **per-worker `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtextextended('nova_coin:'||worker,0))`** (the `log_labor_day` pattern; auto-released at commit). **pgTAP 109** (6: missing-multiplier‚ÜíP0001; distribute 10000 over 3 equal weights ‚Üí Œ£==10000 exactly + dc_distributed==10000 + each ~3333.33 + 3 postings). **Verify:** lint¬∑typecheck¬∑**db:test 109 files/2126/0** (104-108 unchanged). SQL-only ‚Äî no JS/db:types delta. Lessons: per-row round() in a loop drifts ‚Üí allocate the last as remainder; balance-check-then-post needs a per-actor advisory lock.

## Spec 161 U9 - Nova shop admin (operator UI) (2026-06-21)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-21.** Completes the operator UI suite. **`/nova/shop`** (super_admin, drills from /nova). NO DB change. Reads `shop_items` via the ADMIN client (to show inactive too; numeric ‚Üí `Number()`). **`NovaShopAdmin`**: a create form (name + coin price ‚Üí `upsertShopItem`) + a row per item (edit price ‚Üí `upsertShopItem({id,‚Ä¶})`, toggle ýπÄý∏õý∏¥ý∏î/ý∏õý∏¥ý∏î ‚Üí `setShopItemActive(id, !active)`). `shop-actions.ts`: super-only, RLS server client, UUID-validated, `revalidatePath`; **upsert params built CONDITIONALLY** (omit absent optionals ‚Äî `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` forbids passing `undefined`). Prices = coins (no baht peg). **TDD:** `nova-shop-admin.test.tsx` (4: create gate name+price>0; create‚Üí`upsertShopItem({name,priceCoins})`+refresh; toggle active‚Üí`setShopItemActive(id,false)`+refresh; inline error). Updated `nav-back-affordance` (+`nova/shop`). **Verify:** lint¬∑typecheck¬∑**test (full suite)**. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated; component test carries the contract). **Two test bugs caught + fixed:** (1) `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` typecheck on undefined rpc args ‚Üí conditional params; (2) `getByLabelText` collision ‚Äî item-row price label was identical to the create form's ‚Üí renamed to distinct "ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏Ñý∏≤". **SPEC 161 OPERATOR UI SUITE COMPLETE (U7 dials ¬∑ U8 settle/distribute ¬∑ U9 shop).** The whole arc (U1‚ÄìU9) is now operable without SQL.

## Spec 161 U8 - settlement + distribution operator flow (operator UI) (2026-06-21)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-21.** The close-out lifecycle without SQL. **`/nova/settlement`** (super_admin, drills from /nova). NO DB change. Reads `projects` + zero-grant `project_settlements` + `project_coin_distributions` via the ADMIN client behind requireRole (numeric ‚Üí `Number()`), keyed into per-project state. **`NovaSettlementList`** renders each project by state and offers only the applicable action: OPEN ‚Üí none ("ý∏õý∏¥ý∏îýπÇý∏Ñý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ý∏Åýπàý∏≠ý∏ô"); CLOSED-unsettled ‚Üí **ý∏™ý∏£ý∏∏ý∏õý∏Åý∏≥ýπÑý∏£** (`settleProjectAction` ‚Üí `settle_project`); SETTLED-undistributed ‚Üí shows pool/banked-baht/WP counts/equipment-flag + **ýπÅý∏öýπàý∏áýπÄý∏´ý∏£ý∏µý∏¢ý∏ç** (`distributeProjectCoinsAction` ‚Üí `distribute_project_coins`); DISTRIBUTED ‚Üí shows HT cut + DC split + "ýπÅý∏öýπàý∏áýπÅý∏•ýπâý∏ß". Actions in `settlement-actions.ts`: settle gated **super+director**, distribute **super-only** (matches the RPCs), both via the RLS server client (not admin ‚Äî JWT-bound gates), UUID-validated, `revalidatePath`. Coins = points, banked profit = baht. **TDD:** `nova-settlement-list.test.tsx` (4: settle closed-unsettled‚Üíaction+refresh; distribute settled‚Üíshows pool 16,400 + action+refresh; open offers no settle; inline error). Updated `nav-back-affordance` (+`nova/settlement`). **Verify:** lint¬∑typecheck¬∑**test (full suite)**. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated; component test carries the contract; operator on-device = acceptance). **NEXT = U9 shop admin.**

## Spec 161 U7 - Nova dials calibration console (operator UI) (2026-06-21)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-21.** First operator UI for the economics arc ‚Äî makes the SQL-only dials tunable. **`/nova/dials`** (super*admin, drills down from /nova). NO DB change. Reads the zero-grant `nova_dials` + `sell_rate_table` via the **ADMIN client** behind `requireRole(["super_admin"])` (numeric ‚Üí `Number()`); tunes via `setNovaDial`/`setSellRate` server actions. **KEY: setters use the RLS SERVER client (user JWT), NOT the admin client** ‚Äî `set_nova_dial`/`set_sell_rate` gates read `current_user_role()` off the caller's JWT, which the service-role admin client lacks (would deny). Actions re-check super (defence in depth) + `revalidatePath`, mirroring `awardCoins`. **`NovaDialsForm`** client component: a row per dial (9: coin_multiplier¬∑ht_cut_pct¬∑level_weight*{senior,mid,junior,apprentice}¬∑external_factor¬∑vesting_tail_days¬∑savers_bonus_rate) + a row per sell-rate level (cost/internal/external), each with its own edit-state + save ‚Üí refresh; inline error. `src/lib/nova/dials.ts` = SSOT for Thai labels/hints/order + `WORKER_LEVEL_LABEL`. Link added on /nova. **TDD:** `nova-dials-form.test.tsx` (4: renders seeded values; saves dial via `setNovaDial(key,value)`+refresh; saves rate via `setSellRate(level,cost,internal,external)`; inline error). Updated `nav-back-affordance` (+`nova/dials` STATIC_DETAIL). **Verify:** lint¬∑typecheck¬∑**test 189 files/1272**. **Verified-by-checklist** (page auth-gated super_admin+LINE ‚Üí can't e2e on cloud PC; component test carries the contract; operator on-device = acceptance ‚Äî spec 162 precedent). Operator can now calibrate every seeded placeholder before go-live. **NEXT = U8 settle/distribute operator flow ‚Üí U9 shop admin.**

## Spec 161 U6b - saver's bonus + vesting + narrow confiscation (ADR 0060 ¬ß6 + decision c) (2026-06-21)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-21.** Migrations `20260771000000_add_confiscation_coin_source.sql` (enum-add) + `20260771000100_nova_vesting.sql`.
