The 85% of long forms recovered; short ones froze "for good". **Fix:** `repaintScroller` now ALSO does a scroll-INDEPENDENT repaint ‚Äî a 1px `transform: translateY(1px)` nudge on `<main>`, restored on the next rAF (preserves any existing transform; the 1px shift is invisible for one frame) ‚Äî re-rasterizing the layer regardless of content height. Belt-and-suspenders with the existing scroll nudge; both the focusout (force=false, keeps the mid-edit field-to-field skip) and visual-viewport-resize (force=true, the still-focused recurrence) paths get it. TDD: 3rd test in `viewport-scroll-guard.test.tsx` asserts the transform is set inside the repaint and cleared next frame. **Verified-by-checklist** (jsdom has no layout/WebKit; the unit test pins the DOM contract, the real proof is the operator's device test). If it recurs again: next levers are lowering the 80px keyboard-down threshold, nudging on `geometrychange`, or firing across more frames of the ~250ms keyboard slide.

**Recurrence #3 ‚Äî "hidden, not frozen" (2026-06-17):** operator reported the prior fix didn't take and reframed the symptom ‚Äî the screen feels **hidden** (pushed off), not blank/frozen. That's a DIFFERENT bug than the missing-repaint one. The layout comment (`app/layout.tsx`) says the guard's job is to "snap the locked document back to top ‚Ä¶ (otherwise the header is pushed off)", but `git log -S scrollTo` proves the guard **never** snapped to top ‚Äî only the net-zero nudge ever existed, so the header-pushed-off case was unhandled the whole time. Mechanism: body is locked (spec 64 overflow:hidden) so `window`/document scroll should always be 0, but iOS pans the document up to reveal a focused field near the bottom and doesn't always undo that pan on keyboard-close ‚Üí the sticky header sits ABOVE the visible viewport ("hidden"). The net-zero nudge can't fix a real offset. **Fix:** `repaintScroller` now also `window.scrollTo(0, 0)` on keyboard-close (always safe ‚Äî a locked body cannot legitimately scroll; does NOT touch `<main>`'s own scroll = the reading position); gated by the same force/mid-edit logic so it never yanks the viewport while the keyboard is still up. TDD: +2 tests (snaps to top on close; does NOT snap while another field is being edited). **1001 unit** ¬∑ lint ¬∑ typecheck green. **Still NOT device-verified** ‚Äî operator must confirm on a real iOS PWA. If still seen: the pan may be a visual-viewport offset rather than a layout scroll ‚Üí next lever is also zeroing `document.documentElement.scrollTop` / handling `visualViewport.offsetTop`.

**Recurrence #4 ‚Äî "BOTTOM BLANK GAP" ‚Äî actual root cause (2026-06-17):** prior two fixes (repaint, snap-to-top) didn't take. Diagnostic questions cracked it: operator says a **blank gap at the BOTTOM** (not top, not frozen), recovered by **rotate / navigate / refresh but NOT by scrolling**. That discriminator rules out both earlier hypotheses ‚Äî not a paint bug (scrolling would fix it), not a scroll offset (snap-to-top didn't fix it). It's a **stale viewport HEIGHT**: iOS standalone PWA leaves the locked body/`<main>` at the keyboard-REDUCED `100%`/`h-full` height after the keyboard closes, so the vacated bottom strip stays blank; rotate/navigate/refresh fix it because they all force a fresh relayout at the restored height. **Fix:** `repaintScroller` now also pins `document.body.style.height` to the live `window.innerHeight` for one frame, then releases it back to the CSS `h-full` lock on the next rAF ‚Äî replicating the rotate relayout so `100%` re-resolves against the correct viewport (own rAF, restores even with no `<main>`). TDD: +1 test (pins to innerHeight, clears next frame). **1002 unit** ¬∑ lint ¬∑ typecheck green. The guard now does FOUR things on keyboard-close: height relayout (bottom gap) ¬∑ window snap-to-top (hidden header) ¬∑ scroll nudge (blank scrollable) ¬∑ transform nudge (blank short form). **Still NOT device-verified** ‚Äî if the bottom gap persists, escalate from the one-frame toggle to PERMANENT height tracking (set body height = innerHeight on every resize/orientationchange, the `--app-height` pattern) rather than relying on iOS to recompute `100%`.

**Recurrence #5 ‚Äî STOP guessing, instrument (2026-06-17):** the height-relayout toggle (#4) also didn't take on device ‚Äî operator: "problem still there". Three blind fixes missed because the surface is auth-gated + iOS-PWA-only (no desktop repro), so the actual stale dimension is still unknown. Switched from guessing to MEASURING: new **`ViewportDebug`** flag-gated diagnostic overlay (`src/components/features/chrome/viewport-debug.tsx`, mounted in `app/layout.tsx`) ‚Äî renders null unless `?vpdebug=1` (or `localStorage.vpdebug="1"`), otherwise a fixed top-left overlay printing live `innerHeight / visualViewport.height/offsetTop/pageTop / documentElement.clientHeight / body.rectH / main.rectH+clientH+scrollH+scrollTop / window.scrollY / doc.scrollTop / activeElement`, updated on resize/scroll/orientationchange/focusin/focusout (+vv resize/scroll). Operator opens the broken screen with `?vpdebug=1`, closes the keyboard, screenshots the numbers in the blank-gap state ‚Üí tells us which dimension is wrong ‚Üí one definitive fix instead of a 4th guess. TDD: `viewport-debug.test.tsx` (renders null without flag; overlay with flag). **App-only, flag-gated, zero impact on normal use.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ test green. **TEMPORARY ‚Äî remove once the real fix lands.** The viewport-scroll-guard fixes (#2‚Äì#4) stay in (each is harmless and may help one of the sub-cases); the next fix targets whatever the device numbers reveal.

**REGRESSION + REVERT (2026-06-17):** fixes #2‚Äì#4 were NOT harmless ‚Äî operator: the orange ý∏ñýπàý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏£ý∏πý∏õ hero bar (phase-uploader, `position: fixed inset-x-0 bottom-0`, a descendant of PageShell `<main>`) was "hovering all over the screen". Cause: the #4 `transform: translateY(1px)` nudge on `<main>` ‚Äî a CSS transform makes the element the **containing block for its `position:fixed` descendants**, so the fixed capture bar (and any fixed chrome: tab bar, modals, toasts) detaches from the viewport and tracks `<main>`. Worse, the guard's resize path fires `repaintScroller(true)` whenever `innerHeight - vv.height < 80`, which is TRUE even with no keyboard (vv.height ‚âà innerHeight), so incidental iOS visualViewport resize/scroll events re-applied the transform constantly ‚Üí the bar wandered. **Action:** reverted `viewport-scroll-guard.tsx` + its test to the last-good pre-regression commit `4b90a3a` (436ee6f baseline = net-zero `scrollBy` nudge only); dropped the transform nudge, the `window.scrollTo(0,0)`, and the body-height pin (all three unverified, all three fired on the always-true gate). **The keyboard bug itself is still UNFIXED** ‚Äî but the guard is back to a known-good state and the regression is gone. The `ViewportDebug` overlay (af25bc5/6253d14) stays ‚Äî it's a fixed top-left `pointer-events:none` box that transforms no ancestor, so it's safe ‚Äî and it's now the path to a real diagnosis. **Lesson:** never put a `transform` on the scroll container ‚Äî it reparents all fixed chrome; and the `< 80` resize gate is too loose (fires without a keyboard), so anything behind it must be a true no-op when no keyboard is present.

**Recurrence #7 ‚Äî THE FIX (browser-verified) (2026-06-17):** operator sent the actual screenshots. They settled it: post-revert the orange bar is correctly bottom-fixed (regression gone), and the `/requests` create form is **physically clipped at the focused field (ý∏àý∏≥ý∏ôý∏ßý∏ô/ý∏´ý∏ôýπàý∏ßý∏¢) with a giant blank gap below** ‚Äî the scroller is collapsed to the keyboard-reduced height, not merely unpainted. Confirms the stale-height mechanism: the locked `html ‚Üí body ‚Üí main` chain is all `h-full` (=100%); iOS standalone shrinks the web view for the keyboard and never recomputes `100%` on close, so the root stays short ‚Üí `<main>` clipped ‚Üí blank gap. The #4 one-frame toggle failed because it released back to the broken `100%`. **Fix = new `AppHeightTracker`** (`src/components/features/chrome/app-height-tracker.tsx`, mounted in `app/layout.tsx`): pins `documentElement` + `body` height to the live `window.innerHeight` and re-applies on `resize` / `visualViewport resize` / `orientationchange` / `pageshow` / `focusout`(+100ms settle), so the keyboard-close resize restores the full height for real instead of relying on iOS to recompute `100%`. `<main>`'s `h-full` follows the corrected root. **No transform** (the regression lesson) ‚Äî only an explicit root height. **Browser-VERIFIED in the preview** (the first fix in this arc confirmed before shipping): at 812 the tracker pins html/body/main = 812px; shrink the viewport to 500 + fire resize ‚Üí all track to 500; restore to 812 + fire resize ‚Üí all restore to 812 (the keyboard open‚Üíclose cycle); zero console errors. TDD: `app-height-tracker.test.tsx` (pins on mount; re-applies the restored height on resize). lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1002 unit** (147 files) green. **Still owed: operator device confirmation** on the real iOS PWA (the in-app keyboard fires the resize/visualViewport events the preview can't), but the mechanism is now proven, not guessed. `ViewportDebug` overlay stays until confirmed, then both it and any vestigial guard nudge can be removed.

**Recurrence #8 ‚Äî wrong API found by research; corrected to visualViewport (2026-06-17):** #7 didn't work either, and web research (martijnhols.nl OSK article, htmhell viewport guide, quirksmode) explained why: **on iOS the soft keyboard is a SEPARATE LAYER ‚Äî the layout viewport never changes, so `window.innerHeight` stays constant and the window `resize` event does NOT fire when the keyboard opens/closes.** #7's `AppHeightTracker` was built on innerHeight + window resize, so it literally could not respond to the keyboard. Also confirmed: `interactive-widget=resizes-content` is NOT implemented in iOS Safari, so the declarative fix is out. **The only reliable signal is `window.visualViewport`** (`.height` shrinks; the `resize`/`scroll` events fire). **Rewrote `AppHeightTracker`** to publish `Math.round(visualViewport.height)` as the **`--app-vh`** CSS variable on `documentElement`, driven by `visualViewport.resize`/`scroll` (+ window resize/orientationchange/pageshow + focusout fallbacks + a 300ms delayed re-read for iOS's close-lag where visualViewport.height doesn't settle immediately). **PageShell's `<main>`** is now `style={{ height: "var(--app-vh, 100%)" }}` (h-full class stays as the pre-JS/non-iOS fallback) ‚Äî so the scroller tracks the actually-visible area: it shrinks above the keyboard and restores to full on close. CSS variable only ‚Äî **NO transform** (the reverted regression) and no scroll change, so fixed chrome is untouched. **Browser-VERIFIED in the preview:** at 812 ‚Üí `--app-vh` 812px, main computed 812px; shrink the visual viewport to 500 ‚Üí main 500 (the `visualViewport.resize` listener fires automatically, unlike window resize); restore to 812 ‚Üí main 812; zero console errors. TDD: `app-height-tracker.test.tsx` rewritten with a mocked `window.visualViewport` (publishes --app-vh on mount; restores on visualViewport resize). lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ test green. **Device pass still owed** ‚Äî the preview proves the wiring + the right API, but only the real iOS keyboard fires the genuine visualViewport events. If it STILL fails, enable `ViewportDebug` (corner√ó5 / `?vpdebug=1`) and screenshot ‚Äî the live vv.height vs innerHeight numbers will confirm the model.

**Recurrence #9 ‚Äî THE ACTUAL CAUSE, from on-device numbers (2026-06-17).** Made `ViewportDebug` ON by default (#8.5) so the next screenshot carried the metrics. It did, and it ended six rounds of guessing. **Broken state, keyboard CLOSED (`active=body`): `window.scrollY = 389`, `document.documentElement.scrollTop = 389`** (also `vv.height=744`, `vv.offsetTop=49`, `doc.clientH=793`). The body is LOCKED (spec 64 overflow:hidden) so the document must always be at scroll 0 ‚Äî it was at **389**. iOS scrolls the documentElement to reveal the focused input when the keyboard opens and **does not reset it on close**, leaving the whole page shifted up ~389px = exactly the bottom blank gap. **Not paint, not main-height, not a stale 100% ‚Äî a stuck DOCUMENT scroll.** Every prior fix was the wrong axis; the `--app-vh`/visualViewport-height experiment (#8) even made `<main>` 49px short (744 vs the 793 layout) and is now REVERTED. **Fix:** `ViewportScrollGuard` rewritten to force `documentElement.scrollTop` / `body.scrollTop` / `window.scrollTo(0,0)` back to 0 when the keyboard is DOWN (`innerHeight - visualViewport.height < 100`, so it never fights iOS revealing the input mid-edit), fired on focusout + visualViewport resize/scroll + orientationchange/pageshow, with 300ms/600ms re-reads for iOS's late settle. Only the document scroll is touched ‚Äî `<main>`'s reading scroll is left alone; no transform, no height changes. Reverted: deleted `AppHeightTracker` + its test, removed it from layout, PageShell `<main>` back to plain `h-full`. TDD: guard test rewritten (resets doc scroll to 0 when keyboard down; does NOT reset when keyboard up). lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ test green; preview confirms no regression (main full height, no console errors). `ViewportDebug` stays default-on for the confirmation screenshot ‚Äî expectation: in the broken-flow re-test, `window.scrollY`/`doc.scrollTop` now read 0. Remove the overlay once the operator confirms.

**Recurrence #10 ‚Äî THE REAL ROOT: iOS auto-zoom on input focus (2026-06-17).** After the doc-scroll-reset, operator: **"zooming out to 100% solves the blank portion."** That reframes it ‚Äî the page was ZOOMED IN, and the `scrollY=389` pan was the zoom-pan, not an independent document scroll. Cause confirmed in code: the form fields are NOT the `ui/input` primitive (which is `text-base`=16px on mobile) ‚Äî they're hand-rolled `<input>`/`<select>`/`type=date`/`<textarea>` at **`text-sm` = 14px** (`purchase-request-form.tsx` lines 201/222/241/297/308/358, and the same pattern across other forms). **iOS auto-zooms into any focused input whose font-size < 16px**, and a standalone PWA leaves the page zoomed + panned after blur ‚Üí the blank portion; manual zoom-to-100% clears it. **Fix (design-preserving, global):** viewport meta now `width=device-width, initial-scale=1, maximum-scale=1, user-scalable=no` (Next `viewport` export: `maximumScale:1, userScalable:false`) ‚Äî a standalone PWA honors it and iOS no longer auto-zooms on focus, so the field-first `text-sm` sizes stay unchanged. **Trade-off:** disables pinch-zoom app-wide (acceptable for a native-feel ops PWA; operator framed zoom as the bug). Alternative if pinch-zoom must stay: bump every form input to 16px (enlarges field text, many files) ‚Äî offered to operator. Verified in preview: the rendered `<meta name=viewport>` content = `‚Ä¶maximum-scale=1, user-scalable=no`; no console errors. Added `vv.scale` to `ViewportDebug` so the operator can confirm scale stays 1.00. The doc-scroll-reset (#9) stays as defense. lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ test green.

## Spec 95 ‚Äî RESOLVED + cleanup (2026-06-18)

Operator confirmed on device: **no more zoom, no blank portion.** Root cause = iOS auto-zoom on `<16px` form inputs; fix = `maximum-scale=1` in the root `viewport` export (global ‚Äî the only `viewport` export in the app, so it covers every form on every screen). **Cleanup refactor:** removed the temporary `ViewportDebug` overlay (component + test + layout mount + corner-tap gesture); confirmed no dangling `--app-vh` / `AppHeightTracker` / `vpdebug` references remain. **Kept** `ViewportScrollGuard`, reframed as defense-in-depth for the spec-64 body-lock invariant (document scroll stays 0 if iOS scrolls the documentElement to reveal a focused input) ‚Äî a cheap no-op in the normal case; its header comment now points at `maximum-scale=1` as the primary fix. Browser-verified: overlay gone, viewport meta still `‚Ä¶maximum-scale=1, user-scalable=no`, no `--app-vh` leftover, no console errors. lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ test green. The spec-95 saga (#1‚Äì#10) is closed; memory `ios-keyboard-repaint-freeze` rewritten to lead with the auto-zoom root cause + the process lesson (for device-only bugs: ship a metrics overlay first, get one screenshot, then fix the named dimension). **Possible follow-up (not done, scope):** the 14px hand-rolled inputs duplicate styling across forms ‚Äî a shared form-field input component would centralize the size decision and document the <16px-zoom trap.

## Spec 138 U1 ‚Äî ý∏ïýπâý∏≠ý∏áý∏ïý∏¥ý∏îý∏ïý∏≤ý∏°ý∏îýπàý∏ßý∏ô urgent-follow-up panel (2026-06-18)

Status: COMPLETE ‚Äî 2026-06-18. **App-only, no migration**; no db:push. lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1003 unit** (+4 new TDD) ¬∑ green. **Source:** a Claude Design (claude.ai/design) handoff bundle ("Mobile-first redesign for tablets", primary file `PRC Ops Worklist Mobile.dc.html`) ‚Äî the operator mocked the desktop‚Üímobile worklist UX and asked to implement the relevant aspects. Audit found most of the mock already shipped on the procurement `/requests` view (navy `AppHeader`, the 2√ó2 `BuyerStat` KPIs, the spec-104 bands, `PoGroupCard`/`PhonePoBasket`, `BottomSheet`, `BottomTabBar`); the net-new affordances become spec-138 units. **U1 = the mock's headline addition:** a "ý∏ïýπâý∏≠ý∏áý∏ïý∏¥ý∏îý∏ïý∏≤ý∏°ý∏îýπàý∏ßý∏ô" panel that lists the ACTUAL overdue deliveries (the items behind the ýπÄý∏Åý∏¥ý∏ôý∏Åý∏≥ý∏´ý∏ôý∏î count, which until now was only a number/toggle), each row tapping into the request, with a footer link to the full overdue chase filter. **Pure helper** `src/lib/purchasing/overdue-attention.ts` `selectOverdueFollowUp(rows, todayIso, limit=4)` ‚Üí in-transit rows with `eta < today`, most-overdue first (earliest ETA, ties by PR number), capped, mapped to `{id, prNumber, itemDescription, supplier, eta, amount, overdueDays}`; "overdue" reuses `procurementBand(status)==='in_transit'` + `eta<today` so the set is exactly the spec-105 `procurementSummary.overdue` KPI's (4 unit tests, TDD). **Component** `OverdueFollowUpPanel` (server-safe, presentational, no `'use client'`): `bg-card`/`border-edge`/`rounded-card` panel, danger-toned header + count, each item a `<Link>` to `/requests/{id}` (danger dot ¬∑ PR-#### + item name [truncate, spec-47 card parity] ¬∑ supplier ¬∑ `ýπÄý∏Åý∏¥ý∏ô N ý∏ßý∏±ý∏ô` ¬∑ amount font-mono), footer `<Link>` "ý∏îý∏πý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏´ý∏°ý∏îý∏óý∏µýπàýπÄý∏Åý∏¥ý∏ôý∏Åý∏≥ý∏´ý∏ôý∏î" ‚Üí the overdue filter. Field-First tokens only (danger trio + ink/edge/card) ‚Äî passes design-doctrine (no green-\*, no min-h-9). **Page** (`src/app/requests/page.tsx`): computes `attentionItems` for procurement after the existing `amountById` admin read (the panel shows amount ‚Äî already back-office for procurement; no new query, no new money exposure), renders the panel in the procurement column right after the KPI grid, before the filters, only when non-empty; the footer href reuses `buildWorklistQuery({ ...filter, overdue: true })` (same target as the ýπÄý∏Åý∏¥ý∏ôý∏Åý∏≥ý∏´ý∏ôý∏î tile). Named distinct from the existing `AttentionCard` callout primitive (different thing). **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated procurement page ‚Üí no preview; the pure helper carries correctness). Spec doc `138-mobile-worklist-redesign.md` (index updated; also backfilled the missing 136/137 index rows). **Next units (not started, one-per-session):** U2 KPI hero restyle (amber-gradient ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ tile + icons + tap-to-filter); U3 scrollable status-chip filter with live counts (replace the status `<select>`). **Refinement (2026-06-18, operator: "Remove ý∏ïýπâý∏≠ý∏áý∏ïý∏¥ý∏îý∏ïý∏≤ý∏°ý∏îýπàý∏ßý∏ô section from phone view"):** wrapped the render in `hidden lg:block` so the panel now shows only on tablet/desktop (lg+) ‚Äî its origin in the design (the desktop `.dc.html` added it first, mobile mirrored). On the phone the ýπÄý∏Åý∏¥ý∏ôý∏Åý∏≥ý∏´ý∏ôý∏î KPI tile (its filter toggle) stays the chase entry point; the panel read as redundant there. className-only, no logic/helper/test change; lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ test green.

## Spec 138 U2 ‚Äî desktop KPI hero row (2026-06-18)

Status: COMPLETE ‚Äî 2026-06-18. **App-only, no migration**; no db:push. lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1007 unit** (+4 new TDD) ¬∑ green. **Source:** the SECOND file of the same Claude Design handoff ‚Äî the DESKTOP `PRC Ops Worklist.dc.html` (operator sent it after the mobile one). Audit: the desktop procurement view already has the dense grid (spec 108/109), filters (spec 110), KPIs, and the U1 attention panel on lg+; the desktop mock's net-new is the KPI HERO styling + a side-by-side KPI/attention layout (plus status chips + sortable columns, deferred). **U2 = the KPI hero row.** **Pure helper** `src/lib/purchasing/worklist-kpis.ts` `buildWorklistKpis({summary, outstanding, overdueHref, overdueActive})` ‚Üí the four tile descriptors `{key,label,value,caption,tone,icon,href,active}` in order (ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ hot ¬∑ ý∏Åý∏≥ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏™ýπàý∏á shipping ¬∑ ýπÄý∏Åý∏¥ý∏ôý∏Åý∏≥ý∏´ý∏ôý∏î danger-when-overdue/active ¬∑ ý∏Ñýπâý∏≤ý∏áý∏àýπàý∏≤ý∏¢ neutral); only the ýπÄý∏Åý∏¥ý∏ôý∏Åý∏≥ý∏´ý∏ôý∏î tile carries href/active (the spec-110 chase toggle) (4 unit tests, TDD). **Component** `WorklistKpiTile` (server-safe, presentational): icon chip (lucide Clock/Truck/AlertTriangle/Wallet keyed by `icon`) + big value + label + caption, toned per tile (hot = amber fill `bg-attn`; shipping = white card + `text-action` value + `bg-action-soft` chip; danger = `bg-danger-soft`; neutral = white); a tile with href renders as a `<Link>` with a pressed ring when active. **Field-First, flat amber ‚Äî NOT the mock's raw gradient** (doctrine: no raw palette; passes no-green-\*/no-min-h-9). **Page** (`src/app/requests/page.tsx`): builds the tiles + maps to `WorklistKpiTile`; wraps the 2√ó2 KPI grid + the U1 attention panel in `grid lg:grid-cols-[minmax(0,1fr)_332px] lg:items-start` so they sit SIDE BY SIDE on lg+ and stack on phone (panel stays `hidden lg:block`); **deleted the now-dead `BuyerStat` local**. Click behaviour preserved ‚Äî only ýπÄý∏Åý∏¥ý∏ôý∏Åý∏≥ý∏´ý∏ôý∏î is a filter toggle (the mock's clickable ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠/ý∏Åý∏≥ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏™ýπàý∏á tiles need the band‚Üístatus filter mapping = U3, deferred). **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated procurement page ‚Üí no preview; the pure helper carries correctness; the side-by-side layout + tile styling are the operator's device/desktop look-feedback acceptance). Spec doc updated (U2 Change section). **Next (not started):** U3 status-chip filter with live counts (introduces the band‚Üístatus mapping, then the ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠/ý∏Åý∏≥ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏™ýπàý∏á tiles can become filters too); sortable grid columns are a possible later unit (care: it overrides the priority/PO-contiguity order).

## Cleanup ‚Äî remove temporary BootTiming overlay (2026-06-18)

Removed the `BootTiming` boot-profiling overlay (commit 9d1008c) ‚Äî it was a TEMPORARY, default-on, top-left Navigation-Timing readout added to measure the operator-reported ~2s cold launch ("remove after measuring", same measure-first pattern as the spec-95 `ViewportDebug`). Operator asked "do we still need it?" ‚Üí no: it had served its measuring purpose and was visible to every user in prod. Deleted the component + its test + the `layout.tsx` mount/import; grep confirms no `BootTiming`/`boot-timing` refs left. **App-only, no schema.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1006 unit** (‚àí1, the boot-timing test) ¬∑ green. Re-adding to profile boot again is one `git revert 9d1008c`. The boot perf itself was NOT changed here (measurement tool only); if the ~2s persists, the fix targets whatever the captured Navigation-Timing split named (server auth round-trips vs redirect chain vs hydrate).

## Spec 138 U3 ‚Äî scrollable status-chip filter with live counts (2026-06-18)

Status: COMPLETE ‚Äî 2026-06-18. **App-only, no migration**; no db:push. lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1015 unit** (+8 new TDD: 5 chip helper + 3 band axis) ¬∑ green. **U3 = the desktop/mobile mock's status chips.** Replaces the procurement worklist status `<select>` with a horizontally-scrollable row of BAND pills (ý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏´ý∏°ý∏î / ý∏≠ý∏ôý∏∏ý∏°ý∏±ý∏ïý∏¥ýπÅý∏•ýπâý∏ß=`to_order` / ý∏Åý∏≥ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏™ýπàý∏á=`in_transit` / ýπÄý∏Åý∏¥ý∏ôý∏Åý∏≥ý∏´ý∏ôý∏î=overdue), each with a LIVE count. **Introduces the band filter axis** the U2 seam flagged ‚Äî a single raw `status` enum can't express the in_transit band's purchased-OR-on_route set, so a later unit can now make the ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠/ý∏Åý∏≥ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏™ýπàý∏á KPI tiles tap-to-filter (reuse this axis). **Filter axis** (`src/lib/purchasing/worklist-filter.ts`): `ProcurementFilter` += `band: ProcurementBand | null`; `matchesProcurementFilter` AND-composes it (`procurementBand(row.status) === filter.band`); `buildWorklistQuery` serializes `?band=to_order|in_transit` (between project + status). The raw `status` axis STAYS (no UI now, but `?status=rejected` still surfaces the banded-out rejected/cancelled history ‚Äî the only escape, kept to avoid a regression). **Pure helper** `src/lib/purchasing/worklist-status-chips.ts` `buildWorklistStatusChips({rows, filter, todayIso})` ‚Üí 4 chip descriptors `{key,label,count,href,active}` in order (all¬∑to_order¬∑in_transit¬∑overdue); counts reuse the spec-105 `procurementSummary` (so they agree with the KPI hero) + `all` = rows with a non-null band; `rows` is the supplier/project-narrowed set ‚Üí counts are LIVE to those axes; each `href` reuses `buildWorklistQuery` preserving supplier/project/status while setting band+overdue per chip; `active` = overdue wins, else band, else all (5 unit tests, TDD). **Component** `WorklistStatusChips` (server-safe, presentational, no `'use client'`): `overflow-x-auto` `<Link>` pill row, each label + count badge, active pill `bg-fill/text-on-fill` ‚Äî mirrors the spec-137 `worklistChipClass`, min-h-11 tap floor, Field-First tokens only (passes design-doctrine: no green-\*/no-min-h-9). **Page** (`src/app/requests/page.tsx`): parses `?band` (validated against `PROCUREMENT_BANDS`), builds the chips over supplier/project-narrowed rows, renders `<WorklistStatusChips>` between the KPI grid and the supplier/project pickers; `filterActive` += `filter.band`. Band filtering flows through `matchesProcurementFilter` + the existing `groupByProcurementBand` (an active band yields that one group); the `status` flat-group branch is untouched. **`ProcurementFilters`**: removed the status `<select>` (+ its now-dead `STATUS_OPTIONS` / label import / `PurchaseRequestStatus` type); supplier/project pickers stay; the ý∏•ýπâý∏≤ý∏áý∏ïý∏±ý∏ßý∏Åý∏£ý∏≠ý∏á clear-all now also resets `band`. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated procurement page ‚Üí no preview; pure helpers carry correctness; operator device/desktop look-feedback is acceptance). Spec doc updated (U3 Change section). **Open question (decided):** removing the `<select>` drops the only VISIBLE path to filter rejected/cancelled ‚Äî kept reachable via the `?status=` URL but no longer a control (spec-sanctioned; bands already drop those from the pipeline). **Spec 138 = U1¬∑U2¬∑U3 done.** Next possible unit: make the ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠/ý∏Åý∏≥ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏™ýπàý∏á KPI tiles tap-to-filter (the band axis is now in place); sortable grid columns (care: overrides priority/PO-contiguity order).

## Spec 138 U4 ‚Äî KPI hero tiles tap-to-filter (2026-06-18)

Status: COMPLETE ‚Äî 2026-06-18. **App-only, no migration**; no db:push. lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1019 unit** (+4 net: the `worklist-kpis` test rewrote from 4 ‚Üí 8 cases) ¬∑ green. U4 = the final mock affordance: the ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ (`to_order`) and ý∏Åý∏≥ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏™ýπàý∏á (`in_transit`) KPI hero tiles are now clickable band toggles (deferred from U2 "needs the band‚Üístatus mapping", which U3 then built), reusing the U3 band axis. **Pure helper** (`src/lib/purchasing/worklist-kpis.ts`): `buildWorklistKpis` now takes `{ summary, outstanding, filter }` (dropped the pre-built `overdueHref`/`overdueActive` pair) and derives every tile's `href`/`active` internally via `buildWorklistQuery` ‚Äî mirroring the U3 `buildWorklistStatusChips` so the toggle logic lives in one place. A `bandTile(band)` helper builds the ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠/ý∏Åý∏≥ý∏•ý∏±ý∏áý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏™ýπàý∏á tiles as band toggles: `href` sets that band while clearing `overdue` (`band === key ? null : key` ‚Äî re-tapping the active tile clears it back to ý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏´ý∏°ý∏î); `active = !filter.overdue && filter.band === key` (so neither band tile reads active while the overdue chase is on). The ýπÄý∏Åý∏¥ý∏ôý∏Åý∏≥ý∏´ý∏ôý∏î tile is UNCHANGED ‚Äî exactly the existing chase toggle `buildWorklistQuery({ ...filter, overdue: !filter.overdue })` / `active = filter.overdue` / danger-when-`overdue > 0 || filter.overdue`; ý∏Ñýπâý∏≤ý∏áý∏àýπàý∏≤ý∏¢ stays static (`href: null`). **`WorklistKpiTile`: no change** ‚Äî its `href`‚Üí`<Link>`+pressed-ring branch (U2 built it for the ýπÄý∏Åý∏¥ý∏ôý∏Åý∏≥ý∏´ý∏ôý∏î tile) already covers the two new band tiles; the hot/shipping tones flow through it unchanged. **Page** (`src/app/requests/page.tsx`): passes `filter` to `buildWorklistKpis` (dropped the inline `overdueHref`/`overdueActive` args; `buildWorklistQuery` import stays ‚Äî the U1 panel footer still uses it); updated the stale "only the ýπÄý∏Åý∏¥ý∏ôý∏Åý∏≥ý∏´ý∏ôý∏î tile is a filter toggle" comment to note all three pipeline tiles toggle. 8 unit tests (TDD): order/captions, static tones, band-tile href+idle, active band toggles off, band hrefs preserve supplier/project + clear overdue, overdue chase toggle preserved, outstanding static, overdue tone. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated procurement page ‚Üí no preview; the pure helper carries correctness; operator device/desktop look-feedback is acceptance). Spec doc updated (U4 Change section + Units/Out-of-scope). **Spec 138 = U1¬∑U2¬∑U3¬∑U4 done ‚Äî the full mock is shipped.** Possible later unit: sortable grid columns (care: overrides priority/PO-contiguity order).

## Spec 139 U1 ‚Äî App-feel slice 3: optimistic worker active-toggle (2026-06-18)

Status: COMPLETE ‚Äî 2026-06-18. **App-only, no migration**; no db:push. lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1021 unit** (+2 new TDD) ¬∑ green. App-feel slice 3 (optimistic UI) was the last deferred slice ‚Äî done CAREFULLY per-surface, payroll-safe only. U1 = the worker active-toggle on `/workers` (`WorkerRow` in `src/components/features/labor/worker-roster-manager.tsx`), the clean safe pick: already a client component, the toggle reads `worker.active` only for presentation (name colour, `(ý∏õý∏¥ý∏îýπÉý∏äýπâý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô)` suffix, button label), nothing server-computed beside it. **Introduces React 19 `useOptimistic`** (was zero usage in the codebase). **Change:** replaced the toggle's `busy` + success-only `router.refresh()` with `committedActive = useState(worker.active)` (post-mount truth, advanced only by a successful flip) + `optimisticActive = useOptimistic(committedActive, (_, next) => next)` + `useTransition` (`isToggling`). `toggleActive` is now sync: inside `startToggle(async ‚Ä¶)` it `setOptimisticActive(next)` (instant flip), awaits `updateWorker({ id, active: next })`, and on ok `setCommittedActive(next)` (commit ‚Äî the optimistic falls through to it when the transition ends, **no `router.refresh`** ‚Üí the flicker is gone), on `!ok` `toast.error(result.error)` (the optimistic auto-reverts to `committedActive` = the rollback, now explained via the slice-1 toast channel ‚Äî the toggle previously failed **silently**). The row renders name colour / `(ý∏õý∏¥ý∏îýπÉý∏äýπâý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô)` suffix / button label from `optimisticActive`; button `disabled={isToggling}` (no double-fire race). **Untouched** (out of scope): the add-worker form + per-row edit-save stay pessimistic (they surface server validation + need the new/edited row to appear ‚Üí keep `router.refresh`; `useRouter` import stays); the action + RPC + page unchanged; no styling/class change (design-doctrine green). **Tests** (`tests/unit/worker-roster-manager.test.tsx`, +2): optimistic flip-before-server + commit (deferred-promise: label flips to ýπÄý∏õý∏¥ý∏îýπÉý∏äýπâý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô + `(ý∏õý∏¥ý∏îýπÉý∏äýπâý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô)` suffix appears while pending, `updateWorker({id:"w1",active:false})`, **no `router.refresh`**; resolve ok ‚Üí stays flipped); rollback + toast (`{ok:false}` ‚Üí reverts to ý∏õý∏¥ý∏îýπÉý∏äýπâý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô, suffix gone, `toast.error` fired, no refresh). The 4 existing notes/picker tests unchanged. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated pm/super `/workers` ‚Üí no preview; the component tests carry correctness; operator on-device eyeball = acceptance, the slice-76/77/78 convention). Spec doc `139-app-feel-optimistic-ui.md` written (index row added; spec-138 index row relabelled to the full mock). **Next:** more payroll-safe optimistic surfaces (later units, per-surface); slice 5 motion (CSS list-enter via `@starting-style`; route View Transitions = guarded spike only). **Care recorded:** money/payment + labor-day logging surfaces are explicitly NOT optimistic candidates (server-computed / auditable).

## Spec 140 U1 ‚Äî App-feel slice 5: staggered list-enter motion (2026-06-18)

Status: COMPLETE ‚Äî 2026-06-18. **App-only, no migration**; no db:push. lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1025 unit** (+4 new TDD) ¬∑ **build green** (CSS compiles ‚Äî ran `pnpm build` since CI/eslint don't cover globals.css). Pivoted to slice 5 (motion) after an investigation showed slice-3's remaining optimistic surfaces all carry desync (WP hold-status is a central field read across the page) or sensitivity (bank-change approval, consent) risk ‚Äî the worker toggle was the one uniquely-clean surface (operator chose the pivot). Slice 5 U1 = the **safe half** only: a CSS-driven, reduced-motion-gated staggered list-enter on the WP worklist (the most-opened screen). Route View Transitions stay a guarded spike (out of scope). **Pure helper** `src/lib/ui/list-enter.ts` `listEnterProps(index)` ‚Üí `{ className:"list-enter", style:{"--enter-index": step} }` where `step = clamp(trunc(index), 0, LIST_ENTER_STAGGER_CAP=8)` ‚Äî the cap is load-bearing (a long list's tail must not wait seconds; rows past the cap share the last step), the testable seam (4 tests: index, clamp-0/negative, cap-tail, trunc-fraction, TDD). **`globals.css`** `@keyframes list-enter` (opacity 0‚Üí1, translateY 6px‚Üí0) + `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference){ .list-enter{ animation: list-enter 240ms ease-out both; animation-delay: calc(var(--enter-index,0) * 35ms) } }` ‚Äî matches the spec-76 `toast-in` / spec-78 `sheet-up` pattern; `both` holds the row invisible through its stagger then settles visible; reduced-motion users get static fully-visible rows (the `from{opacity:0}` only applies while the animation is attached, which only happens inside the no-preference query ‚Üí no FOUC). **`WorklistRow`** gained optional `enterIndex?: number` (merged into the root `<Link>` ‚Äî appends the class + sets the style var; omitted ‚Üí every existing caller untouched). **`work-package-list.tsx`** passes `enterIndex={i}` from the map index at the 3 WorklistRow render sites (grouped-by-band / flat action-state / deliverable-group `<li>`) so the WP list glides in uniformly in every group mode. Server-safe (no `'use client'`; class+style are plain markup), zero data risk. **Verified-by-checklist + build** (auth-gated project page ‚Üí no preview; the helper test carries the cap logic; CSS animation isn't unit-testable ‚Üí operator on-device eyeball = acceptance, the slice-77 visual-slice precedent; design-doctrine green ‚Äî "list-enter" trips no green-\*/min-h-9 rule). Spec doc `140-app-feel-motion.md` written (index row added). **Next:** more lists (procurement worklist cards, contacts, suppliers, requests ‚Äî fast-follows reusing `listEnterProps`); route / View Transitions later (guarded spike, never the foundation); slice 3 optimistic continues per-surface (payroll-safe only).

## Spec 141 U1 ‚Äî equipment registry data layer (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-18.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1036 unit** (+11 new TDD validator) ¬∑ green; **pgTAP file 65 green post-push (22/22; full suite 65 files / 1363 asserts, 0 failures)**; `db:push` applied migrations 20260723000000/000100/000200; `db:types` reconciled; `db push --dry-run` = remote up to date. First unit of the equipment tracking ‚Üí intercompany rental system (ADR 0055; design + spec written and indexed earlier this session). **Owner-host decision (ADR 0055 open Q, operator-confirmed):** the equipment owner (sister company) lives in its OWN master `equipment_owners` ‚Äî not suppliers/service_providers ‚Äî so the future owner portal can bind to it the way the DC portal binds to contractors (ADR 0051); cost = a 3rd table this unit. **Migrations (3; ts 20260723000000/000100/000200; renumbered from a stale 20260713\* guess that collided with the already-applied PO-delivery migrations ‚Äî caught by db:push, nothing mis-applied):** `equipment_owners` (id/name/phone/created_by; masters posture ‚Äî staff SELECT, pm/procurement/super INSERT+UPDATE, no delete, created_by pin); `equipment_categories` (extensible lookup TABLE not enum per ADR 0055 ¬ß2, optional parent_id self-FK, same posture); `equipment_items` ‚Äî the serialized-first registry: `equipment_tracking` enum (unit|bulk) + `equipment_status` enum (available/on_site/in_use/maintenance/returned/lost), owner_id‚Üíequipment_owners + category_id‚Üíequipment_categories (both NOT NULL), unique partial index on asset_tag (where not null), CHECKs enforcing unit‚áîno-qty / bulk‚áíqty‚â•1 / bulk‚áíno-tag / name-nonblank / acq-cost‚â•0. **MONEY POSTURE (ADR 0055 ¬ß6, workers.day_rate pattern):** `acquisition_cost` + `acquired_at` get NO authenticated grant ‚Äî the column-scoped SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE grants OMIT them (admin-client-only read behind requireRole pm/super/procurement); both documented via `comment on column` (money-doc doctrine, file 38). Non-money columns field-visible (site_admin SELECT). **Policies eval-once-WRAPPED** `(select public.current_user_role())`/`(select auth.uid())` from day one (file-40 scanner). **No write RPC this unit** ‚Äî masters-pattern column-scoped grants + back-office policies + DB CHECKs carry the writes; money columns are simply unwritable by authenticated (capture lands later/P2). **Pure helper** `src/lib/equipment/validate-equipment-item.ts` `validateEquipmentItem` (decide-pattern union, Thai errors; 11 tests, TDD ‚Äî written FIRST, RED‚ÜíGREEN). **pgTAP file 65** (plan 22): catalog (3 tables + 2 enums + enum labels), id PK, RLS on all three, the 4 CHECK invariants (23514), money anti-grant (authenticated can't read acquisition_cost/acquired_at ‚Üí 42501), staff read of non-money cols, site_admin INSERT denied (42501), pm happy INSERT, created_by-pin enforced (42501), no-delete (42501), anon denied (42501). **Types:** NOT hand-extended ‚Äî no U1 consumer; `db:types` regenerates authoritatively at the gate (avoids drift on a generated file). **Verified-by-checklist** (no UI this unit; the validator carries TS correctness, pgTAP carries DB correctness post-push). Docs: ADR 0055 + spec 141 + both indexes written earlier; memory `spec141-equipment-rental` saved. **Operator gate (DONE 2026-06-18):** branch ‚Üí ff-merge ‚Üí push (commits 303eebc feat + 2ec5cdc renumber + 6cac689 types); `db:push` applied; `db:test` green; `db:types` reconciled (app + worker copy); drift check clean. **Lesson:** check the true max migration timestamp before authoring ‚Äî the first `db:push` failed because 20260713\* (a stale-glob guess) collided with the already-applied PO-delivery run (20260713‚Äì20260722); db:push caught it, nothing mis-applied, renumbered to 20260723\*. **Next units (P1 tracking, one-per-session):** U2 equipment list/detail UI; U3 `equipment_movements` append-only + current-location anti-join. Then P2 (money): U4 rental batches, U5 WP allocation + `freeze_wp_equipment_cost` (mirrors wp_labor_costs), U6 owner portal + WP-owner billing.

## Spec 141 U2 ‚Äî equipment management UI (2026-06-18)

Status: COMPLETE ‚Äî 2026-06-18. **App-only, no migration** (U1's grants/policies carry the writes). lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1043 unit** (+7 new TDD: equipment-manager) ¬∑ green; pushed to main, Vercel auto-deploys. Makes the shipped registry visible/usable on-device. **Route** `/equipment` (`requireRole(BACK_OFFICE_ROLES)` = pm/super/procurement, the U1 INSERT/UPDATE audience), mirrors `/workers` scaffold (PageShell + BottomTabBar + DetailHeader back‚Üí/settings). Reads via the **RLS server client only ‚Äî NO admin client** (no money on this surface: `acquisition_cost`/`acquired_at` stay admin-only, untouched). **`EquipmentManager`** (`src/components/features/equipment/`, new domain folder ‚Äî registered in feature-components-structure test): quick-add category + owner cards (bootstrap the masters), an add-item form, and per-item inline edit; the unit|bulk RadioChip swaps the asset-tag/quantity field; the U1 `validateEquipmentItem` gives Thai client-side errors before the action re-checks. No optimistic toggle (status = explicit edit, not a flip). **Actions** (`src/app/equipment/actions.ts`): `createEquipment` ¬∑ `updateEquipment` ¬∑ `createEquipmentCategory` ¬∑ `createEquipmentOwner` ‚Äî each `requireRole(BACK_OFFICE_ROLES)` (defense-in-depth + gives the caller id for the `created_by` pin), validates shape (`validateEquipmentItem` + UUID/enum), writes via the RLS client, decide-pattern returns, `revalidatePath`. **NO RPC** (unlike workers ‚Äî equipment's non-money writes have direct column-scoped grants). **Nav:** `SettingsLink` (ý∏≠ý∏∏ý∏õý∏Åý∏£ý∏ìýπå, Wrench icon) in the settings master-data section (pm/super) + `/equipment` added to `SETTINGS_TAB.match`. **Tests:** `tests/unit/equipment-manager.test.tsx` (7, TDD ‚Äî written FIRST: row render, add unit w/ tag, bulk-switch hides tag + passes qty, client-side reject before action, inline edit, quick-add category, quick-add owner); registered `/equipment` in the nav-back-affordance (STATIC_DETAIL) + feature-components-structure (ALLOWED_DOMAINS) anti-drift guards. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated page ‚Üí preview only renders /login; the component test + U1 validator/pgTAP carry correctness; operator on-device pass = acceptance). Spec 141 doc gained a U2 section. **Seams / OUT (deferred):** money (acquisition_cost entry/display ‚Äî admin-only); movements/deployment (U3); site_admin read-only view + its nav (until U3 gives the field a reason); procurement nav entry (page reachable by URL today; settings master-data section is pm/super-gated); category/owner edit/delete (no-delete by design); a standalone detail page. **Next:** U3 `equipment_movements` (append-only, deploy-to-project per the Case-A model) + current-location anti-join; then P2 money units.

## Spec 141 U3 ‚Äî equipment movements + current location (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-18** (commit/push/apply same as U1; commits a446dfe feat + bdd4e66 types/plan-fix). lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1049 unit** (+5 new TDD: current-location helper) ¬∑ green; **pgTAP file 66 green post-push (16/16; full suite 66 files / 1379 asserts, 0 failures)**; `db:push` applied `20260724000000`; `db:types` reconciled; `db push --dry-run` = remote up to date. Data layer only ‚Äî the move-equipment UI is U4. **Migration** `20260724000000_create_equipment_movements.sql`: `equipment_movement_kind` enum (received/deployed/returned/maintenance/lost); `equipment_movements` (item_id‚Üíequipment_items, kind, project_id‚Üíprojects [null], quantity‚â•1, occurred_at, note, created_by) ‚Äî **CHECK** `project_id is not null ‚áî kind='deployed'` (a set deploys to a PROJECT, every other kind has no project; the Case-A attach-to-project model), **append-only** (SELECT+INSERT grants only, NO update/delete ‚Äî corrections are compensating movements), staff SELECT+INSERT (site_admin/pm/super/procurement ‚Äî the field moves gear; tracking not money) with created_by pin, eval-once-WRAPPED policies. **SECURITY DEFINER AFTER INSERT trigger** `equipment_movement_derive_status` denormalizes `equipment_items.status` from the new movement (received‚Üíavailable, deployed‚Üíon_site, returned‚Üíreturned, maintenance‚Üímaintenance, lost‚Üílost) ‚Äî definer because site_admin records movements but can't UPDATE equipment_items under RLS (purchasing-derive-trigger posture). **Pure helper** `src/lib/equipment/current-location.ts` `currentEquipmentLocation` ‚Üí latest movement per item by occurred_at ‚Üí {kind, projectId} (the display source of truth; local enum union so it compiles pre-db:types; 5 tests, TDD first). **pgTAP file 66** (plan 15): catalog+enum, kind‚Üîproject CHECK, qty‚â•1, append-only (no update/delete‚Üí42501), staff INSERT + created_by pin, visitor denied, the status-derive trigger (deployed‚Üíon_site, returned‚Üíreturned), anon denied, staff-sees-2-movements. **Docs:** ADR 0055 ¬ß4 reworded (event-log latest-OCCURRED wins, not a supersede anti-join; status-derive trigger noted) + spec 141 U3 section. **Money posture:** unchanged (movements carry no money; fully field-visible). **Seams:** status-derive trigger is last-recorded-wins (backdated movement sets status to its own kind); the helper is latest-OCCURRED + is the display SoT ‚Äî they agree when movements are recorded in order. Bulk current-on-project quantity (signed-sum) = allocations/P2. No audit_log row (the append-only log is the trail; no new audit_action enum). **Next:** U4 move-equipment UI (deploy/return actions + where-is-it display reusing the helper); then P2 money (U5 daily usage logs + freeze, U6 owner portal).

## Spec 141 U4 ‚Äî move equipment + where-is-it display (2026-06-18)

Status: **COMPLETE (local) ‚Äî 2026-06-18; awaiting operator merge/push.** App-only, **no migration** (U3's `equipment_movements` grants + status-derive trigger + `currentEquipmentLocation` helper carry the write/derivation). lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1060 unit** (+11 new TDD: 7 equipment-location-label + 4 equipment-manager move) ¬∑ **153 files green**. The payoff unit: the deploy/return action + current-location badge now live on the back-office `/equipment` screen. Audience stays `BACK_OFFICE_ROLES` (the site_admin field move view + widened gate is a later unit). **Shipped:** (1) SSOT `EQUIPMENT_MOVEMENT_KIND_LABEL` in `src/lib/i18n/labels.ts` (received/deployed/returned/maintenance/lost ‚Üí Thai; consumed by the move-form kind picker + the badge); (2) pure `src/lib/equipment/equipment-location-label.ts` `equipmentLocationLabel(location, projectName)` ‚Üí "ý∏´ý∏ôýπâý∏≤ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô: <project>" for deployed (bare label if name missing), placeholder "‚Äî" when no movements (7 tests, TDD first, RED‚ÜíGREEN); (3) action `recordEquipmentMovement` in `src/app/equipment/actions.ts` ‚Äî `requireRole(BACK_OFFICE_ROLES)`, validates itemId UUID + kind enum + **project-IFF-deployed** + qty‚â•1 + note‚â§2000 (mirrors the U3 DB CHECKs so the UI fails friendly), inserts via the **RLS client** with `created_by` pin, `occurred_at` omitted (DB now()), `revalidatePath`; NO RPC (U3 grants + CHECKs + derive trigger are the guard); (4) page `/equipment` adds two RLS-client fetches (`projects` id/name + `equipment_movements` item_id/kind/project_id/occurred_at desc), maps movements to the helper record shape; (5) `EquipmentManager` gains `projects` + `movements` props, builds the location map with `currentEquipmentLocation`, renders a üìç where-is-it badge per row, and a per-row "ý∏¢ýπâý∏≤ý∏¢" toggle opening `MoveEquipmentForm` (kind select default deployed ‚Üí conditional project select [required when deployed] / bulk-only "ý∏àý∏≥ý∏ôý∏ßý∏ôý∏óý∏µýπàý∏¢ýπâý∏≤ý∏¢" qty / optional note ‚Üí `recordEquipmentMovement`). **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated page ‚Üí preview renders /login only; the component test covers the wiring through the mocked action; the U3 RLS + CHECKs + derive trigger carry DB correctness, already green in pgTAP file 66 on prod; operator on-device pass = acceptance). Spec 141 doc gained a U4 section. **Seams:** latest-event only (a bulk item split across projects shows its most recent deploy ‚Äî per-project on-hand tally is the P2 allocation concern); badge reads latest-OCCURRED while the status chip reads the trigger-derived last-RECORDED status (agree for chronological entry ‚Äî the U3 seam). **OUT (deferred):** site_admin field move view + nav + widened gate (next unit); backdating UI; movement edit/void (append-only ‚Äî correction = compensating movement); `in_use`; money; audit_log rows. **Next:** site_admin field move view (widen the gate, give the field its screen), then P2 money (U5 daily usage logs + freeze, U6 owner portal).

## Spec 141 U5 ‚Äî site_admin field move view (2026-06-18)

Status: **COMPLETE (local) ‚Äî 2026-06-18; awaiting operator merge/push.** App-only, **no migration** (U3's `equipment_movements` RLS already admits site_admin INSERTs; U4 recorded moves only from the back-office screen). lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1062 unit** (+2 new TDD: reduced-view hides management / move still works) ¬∑ **153 files green**. Gives the field its reason to be on `/equipment`: site_admin can view the registry + each item's where-is-it badge + record a move, but **not** edit the registry (stays back office). The deferred "site_admin view + nav + widened gate" from U4's OUT. **Numbering note:** this P1 tracking unit took U5; the P2 money units shift to U6 (daily usage logs + freeze) / U7 (owner portal). **Shipped:** (1) new `EQUIPMENT_MOVE_ROLES` allowlist in `src/lib/auth/role-home.ts` (= the U3 movements RLS audience: site_admin/pm/super/procurement; documented separately from PURCHASING_ROLES per role-doctrine); (2) `/equipment` page gate widened `BACK_OFFICE_ROLES`‚Üí`EQUIPMENT_MOVE_ROLES`, computes `canManageRegistry = BACK_OFFICE_ROLES.includes(ctx.role)` (still RLS-client only ‚Äî no money fetched, site_admin sees nothing money); (3) `recordEquipmentMovement` gate widened to `EQUIPMENT_MOVE_ROLES` (the 4 registry actions stay `BACK_OFFICE_ROLES` ‚Äî defense-in-depth); (4) `EquipmentManager` gains `canManageRegistry`: false hides the add-item form, both quick-add cards, and the per-row ýπÅý∏ÅýπâýπÑý∏Ç edit affordance + uses a field-appropriate empty state, while keeping the list + üìç badge + ý∏¢ýπâý∏≤ý∏¢ move; (5) a site_admin-visible ý∏≠ý∏∏ý∏õý∏Åý∏£ý∏ìýπå `SettingsLink` in `/settings` under a new "ý∏´ý∏ôýπâý∏≤ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô" section (field framing "ý∏îý∏πýπÅý∏•ý∏∞ý∏¢ýπâý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏≠ý∏∏ý∏õý∏Åý∏£ý∏ìýπåý∏´ý∏ôýπâý∏≤ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô"; pm/super keep the master-data link; `/equipment` already in `SETTINGS_TAB.match` so the ý∏ïý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏Ñýπàý∏≤ tab lights for the field user). **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated page; the component test covers the reduced UI + the still-working move through the mocked action; `requireRole` + the U3 RLS carry the gate; the operator + a site_admin device on-device pass = acceptance). Spec 141 doc gained a U5 section. **Seams:** one `/equipment` surface, role-conditional (no field-route duplication ‚Äî WP-centric single-surface); RLS is the real guard, `canManageRegistry` is presentation only (registry actions re-check BACK_OFFICE_ROLES). **OUT / still deferred:** procurement nav entry (URL-only, the standing U2 deferral ‚Äî unchanged); registry edit for site_admin; money; movement edit/void; P2 money units. **Next:** P2 money ‚Äî U6 daily usage logs + `freeze_wp_equipment_cost` (mirrors wp_labor_costs), U7 owner portal + WP-owner billing; these need the operator's rate/billing inputs (ADR 0055 P2 open Qs).

## Spec 142 U1 ‚Äî project onboarding data layer (create_project + suggest_project_code) (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-18.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1062 unit (153 files)** ¬∑ green; **pgTAP file 67 green post-push (15/15; full suite 67 files / 1394 asserts, 0 failures)**; `db:push` applied `20260725000000`; `db:types` reconciled (app + worker copy); red proven first (`to_regprocedure` both null pre-push). First front door for creating a project in-app (today projects are born only at the DB/console layer). DB foundation only ‚Äî the stub modal (U2) and onboarding checklist (U3) follow. Migration `20260725000000` (next after the equipment run's max `20260724000000`): `create_project(p_code,p_name,p_project_type,p_client_id) returns uuid` SECURITY DEFINER + role-gated (project_manager/super_admin) ‚Äî the sanctioned PM write path; the projects INSERT policy stays super-only (mirrors update_project_settings, spec 79). Validates code (non-empty, ‚â§50) + name (non-empty, ‚â§200) + client existence; duplicate code surfaces 23505 for the UI to re-suggest (no internal retry). Auto-adds the creator as a `project_members` row (the onboarding PM is on the team ‚Äî operator request 2026-06-18; forward-compat hook for the spec-143 PM-visibility change). `suggest_project_code() returns text` SECURITY DEFINER + stable (definer read bypasses RLS so a future scoped PM still gets a correct next number) ‚Üí next `PRC-YYYY-NNN` for the current year, advisory/editable. pgTAP file 67 (plan 15, test-first): catalog + both secdef, PM/super create, creator-auto-member, site_admin/visitor deny (42501), empty-name/unknown-client (22023), duplicate-code (23505), suggest format + visitor-deny + collision-proof max+1 property. Spec 142 + index written this session. **Verified-by-checklist** (no app UI this unit ‚Äî types regenerate authoritatively; pgTAP carries DB correctness post-push). **Seams / OUT:** no RLS policy change (the RPC is the PM path, INSERT policy stays super-only); contract_reference not accepted at create (deferred); `onboarding_dismissed_at` column lands in U3 where the checklist uses it. **Next:** U2 ‚Äî "New project" stub modal on `/projects` calling create_project (+ suggest as editable default) ‚Üí redirect to the new project's detail page.

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-18.** App-only, **no migration** (U1's `create_project` + `suggest_project_code` RPCs carry it). lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1072 unit (155 files; +10 new TDD: 5 validate-project-code + 5 new-project-sheet)** ¬∑ green. Spec 142 doc gained the U2 status. **Seams / OUT:** suggested code computed at page load (a stale suggestion just yields a friendly 23505 on submit ‚Äî the field is editable); no re-suggest-on-conflict (deferred); stub is identity-only by design ‚Äî dates/budget/team/WPs are the project page's job. **Next:** U3 ‚Äî onboarding checklist on the project detail page (derived done-state from project fields + member/WP counts, deep-links to settings/WP-add, dismissible via a new `onboarding_dismissed_at` column).

## Spec 142 U3 ‚Äî onboarding checklist on the project page (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-18.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1077 unit (156 files; +5 TDD: onboarding-checklist)** ¬∑ green; **pgTAP file 68 green post-push (19/19; full suite 68 files / 1413 asserts, 0 failures)**; `db:push` applied `20260726000000`; `db:types` reconciled. PM/super now see a dismissible "ýπÄý∏£ý∏¥ýπàý∏°ý∏ïýπâý∏ôýπÇý∏Ñý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ (X/5)" checklist atop the project page guiding enrichment. **Migration** `20260726000000_project_onboarding_status.sql`: `projects.onboarding_dismissed_at timestamptz null` (+comment); `project_onboarding_status(uuid) returns table(dates_lead_set, budget_set, team_added, work_packages_added, client_set, dismissed)` ‚Äî **SECURITY DEFINER + stable** so it reads the money-isolated `budget_amount_thb` (spec 79) and returns **only a boolean**, no amount leak; `dismiss_project_onboarding(uuid) returns boolean` (stamps the column, idempotent). Both **PM/super only** (42501 otherwise); neither re-checks per-project visibility (current model = PM/super see all; spec 143 will gate the page). **Money-safe by construction:** the page never reads budget ‚Äî it reads the booleans RPC. **UI (co-located under `src/app/projects/[projectId]/`):** `OnboardingChecklist` (presentational, derived ‚Äî hides when dismissed OR all 5 met, never stores checklist state; 5 rows: dates+lead / budget / team / client ‚Üí `projectSettingsHref`, work_packages ‚Üí `#work-packages` in-page anchor; met rows show a Check + line-through, unmet rows are tappable Links with a chevron); `DismissOnboardingButton` ('use client', dismiss action ‚Üí router.refresh); `dismissProjectOnboarding` action (PM_ROLES gate mirroring settings, maps RPC result). Page wiring: PM-only `rpc("project_onboarding_status")` ‚Üí `OnboardingChecklist`; WP section heading got `id="work-packages"` as the anchor target. **pgTAP file 68** (plan 19): column shape, both functions exist, EMPTY project ‚Üí all false, FULL project (dates+lead+budget+client+member+wp) ‚Üí 5 flags true, visitor status/dismiss denied (42501), PM dismiss returns true + status then dismissed. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated page ‚Üí preview renders /login; the 5 component tests cover render/done-markers/hidden-when-dismissed/hidden-when-complete/dismiss-wiring; the RPCs' correctness is green in pgTAP 68 on prod; operator on-device pass = acceptance). Spec 142 doc gained the U3 status. **Seams / OUT:** the work_packages row anchors to the WP list on the same page ‚Äî once U4 ships the in-app "add work package" button there, the row points straight at the action; auto-hide needs ALL 5 met (budget/client optional in practice ‚Üí dismiss is the escape); no undismiss UI. **Next:** U4 ‚Äî in-app "add work package" form + `create_work_package` RPC (baseline WP seeding; turns the checklist's last row into a real create flow).

## Spec 142 U4 ‚Äî in-app "add work package" (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-18.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1086 unit (158 files; +9 TDD: 6 validate-new-wp + 3 add-work-package-sheet)** ¬∑ green; **pgTAP file 69 green post-push (12/12; full suite 69 files / 1425 asserts, 0 failures)**; `db:push` applied `20260727000000`; `db:types` reconciled. Baseline WP seeding: PM/super get a **+ ýπÄý∏ûý∏¥ýπàý∏°ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô** button beside the ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô heading on the project page ‚Üí sheet (code, name, optional description) ‚Üí the WP appears in the list. This makes the U3 checklist's `ýπÄý∏ûý∏¥ýπàý∏°ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô` row a real create flow (the `#work-packages` anchor lands right on it). **Migration** `20260727000000_create_work_package_rpc.sql`: `create_work_package(p_project_id, p_code, p_name, p_description default null) returns uuid` ‚Äî **SECURITY DEFINER + PM/super gate** (same posture as create_project U1; a definer RPC sidesteps the work_packages table-grant question ‚Äî the create migration never granted authenticated INSERT, only the RLS policy). Validates code (‚â§50) + name (‚â§200) + **project existence** (22023); composite-unique `(project_id, code)` duplicate ‚Üí 23505. **UI (co-located `src/app/projects/[projectId]/`):** pure `validateWorkPackageCode`/`validateWorkPackageName` + `WP_CODE_MAX/WP_NAME_MAX` in `@/lib/work-packages/validate-new-wp`; `createWorkPackage` action appended to the project actions file (PM_ROLES gate, conditional `p_description` arg per exactOptionalPropertyTypes, maps 23505‚Üí"ý∏£ý∏´ý∏±ý∏™ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ôý∏°ý∏µý∏≠ý∏¢ý∏πýπàýπÅý∏•ýπâý∏ßýπÉý∏ôýπÇý∏Ñý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£"/42501/22023, returns `{ok,id}`, revalidates); `AddWorkPackageSheet` ('use client', reuses BottomSheet; submit gated on code+name; on success clears + closes + router.refresh). Page wiring: WP heading became a flex row with the PM-only trigger. **pgTAP file 69** (plan 12): catalog + secdef, PM/super create + row-exists, site_admin/visitor deny (42501), empty name/code (22023), unknown project (22023), duplicate-in-project (23505), same-code-different-project allowed. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated page ‚Üí preview renders /login; the 3 component tests cover submit-gate/success-refresh/error; create_work_package green in pgTAP 69 on prod; operator on-device pass = acceptance). Spec 142 doc gains the U4 status. **Decision (author):** direct RLS-client insert was rejected in favour of the RPC to avoid the missing table-grant + match U1 ‚Äî noted as a deviation from the spec's wording (it said RPC anyway). **Seams / OUT:** stub WP (code/name/description) ‚Äî owner/priority/schedule/deliverable stay in the existing WP detail + settings surfaces; no bulk add here (that's U7 CSV). **MVP onboarding loop (U1‚ÄìU4) is now end-to-end: create project ‚Üí land ‚Üí checklist ‚Üí add work packages.** **Next:** U5 ‚Äî WP template by `project_type` (`wp_templates` + `apply_wp_template`); U6 copy-from-project; U7 CSV import. U5/U6 carry small open design forks (template authoring = hardcode-via-migration v1 vs an editor; copy source = same-type vs any) ‚Äî leaning hardcode v1 + same-type unless the operator redirects.

## Spec 143 U1 ‚Äî membership-scoped project visibility (ADR 0056) (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-18.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1086 unit** ¬∑ green; **pgTAP file 70 green post-push (30/30; full suite 70 files / 1455 asserts, 0 failures)**; `db:push` applied `20260728000000`; `db:types` reconciled. Operator's 2nd ask: **project_manager + site_admin now see only the projects they're involved with** (a `project_members` row OR `project_lead_id`); **project_coordinator + super_admin see all**; **procurement keeps** its spec-102 cross-project read on projects/work_packages/purchase_requests. **Full RLS isolation** (operator decision) ‚Äî enforced on every project-scoped table, not just the hub. **ADR 0056** (amends 0013) written + indexed; spec 143 written + indexed. **Migration** `20260728000000_project_visibility_scope.sql`: two SECURITY DEFINER STABLE helpers ‚Äî `can_see_project(uuid)` (super/coordinator‚Üítrue; pm/site_admin‚Üímember-or-lead; else false, never NULL ‚Üí closes the [[rls-self-check-coalesce]] trap) and `can_see_wp(uuid)` (resolves WP‚Üíproject, coalesce false); definer so they read project_members/projects/work_packages without re-triggering the policies that call them (ADR 0011, no recursion). **Lead‚Üímember backfill** in the same migration (idempotent `on conflict do nothing`) keeps current leads visible. **Rewrote 7 SELECT policies** (writes untouched), each preserving its exceptions: projects/work_packages = `procurement OR can_see_project`; deliverables = `can_see_project`; photo_logs/approvals = `can_see_wp`; purchase_requests = `requested_by=auth.uid() OR procurement OR can_see_wp` (separate appsheet_writer policy untouched); reports = `can_see_project AND role<>'site_admin'` (spec 19 ‚Äî reports still never reach site_admin). Eval-once wrapped form throughout (file 40). **Authoritative current policies pulled from pg_policies first** (not stale migration files) ‚Äî caught that spec 102 had added procurement to projects+work_packages. **pgTAP file 70** (plan 30): helper behaviour across 8 role/involvement combos + can_see_wp; projects/work_packages end-to-end per-row visibility (member sees, non-member doesn't, procurement keeps all, visitor none); child-policy wiring (qual references the right helper; reports excludes site_admin; PR keeps requester self-read). **Blast radius (expected ‚Äî access-model change):** 15 existing pgTAP suites hard-coded the old "PM/site_admin see everything" and broke on the read; fixed by seeding each fixture's PM/site_admin test users as project_members of its fixture project (scoped by test code + `auth.users.email` subquery ‚Äî `public.users` has no email column, the first attempt's trap). One ADR-0026 qual pin (file 17: "PR SELECT admits site_admin site-wide") was obsoleted by 0056 and re-pinned to the new `can_see_wp` gate. **No app code changed** ‚Äî RLS filters every cross-project list automatically (hub, worklists, payroll, reports). **ROLLOUT HEADS-UP (operator):** turning this on hides existing projects from PMs/site_admins who aren't a member/lead. Prod data is tiny (2 projects, 2 PMs, 3 site_admins; backfill enrolled the 1 lead) + super_admin sees all, so add the right people to each project via project settings (spec 80 UI). **Verified-by-checklist** (RLS is the SUT; pgTAP 70 + the regression suite carry correctness; operator on-device pass = acceptance). **Next:** U2 ‚Äî enable `project_coordinator` as a real role (roleHome ‚Üí `/projects` + add to PROJECT_VIEW_ROLES so the see-all coordinator can actually browse; currently lands on /coming-soon). Then onboarding U5‚ÄìU7 (WP seeding) remain.

## Spec 143 U2 ‚Äî enable project_coordinator (routing + chrome) (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-18.** App-only, **no migration** (U1's RLS already lets coordinator read every project). lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1088 unit (+2 TDD: role-home coordinator pin + bottom-tab-bar coordinator set)** ¬∑ green. Makes U1's see-all coordinator actually reachable (it was DB-visible but `roleHome`‚Üí/coming-soon + excluded from PROJECT_VIEW_ROLES). **Shipped:** (1) `roleHome('project_coordinator')` ‚Üí `/projects`; (2) `PROJECT_VIEW_ROLES` += `project_coordinator` (gates `/projects` + `/projects/[id]`); (3) `COORDINATOR_TABS` = `[ýπÇý∏Ñý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£, ý∏ïý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏Ñýπàý∏≤]` + `tabsForRole` case (bottom-tab-bar) ‚Äî lean two-tab set because the coordinator is NOT admitted to /review, /requests, or /dashboard (SITE_STAFF/PM/procurement gates), so every tab stays a live destination; (4) `COORDINATOR_HUB_NAV` = `[ýπÇý∏Ñý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£, ý∏ïý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏Ñýπàý∏≤]` (desktop strip mirror); (5) `/projects` hub coordinator branch ‚Äî kicker "ý∏úý∏πýπâý∏õý∏£ý∏∞ý∏™ý∏≤ý∏ôý∏áý∏≤ý∏ôýπÇý∏Ñý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£" + COORDINATOR_HUB_NAV. Coordinator does NOT get the create-project button / suggest RPC / add-WP (all `isPm`-gated) ‚Äî it's read-only oversight by design. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated pages ‚Üí preview renders /login; role-home + bottom-tab-bar unit tests carry the wiring; RLS visibility green in pgTAP 70 on prod; operator on-device pass = acceptance). **Decision:** kept the coordinator tab set lean rather than widening /dashboard's SITE_STAFF_ROLES gate (scope) ‚Äî a coordinator dashboard is a later call. Spec 143 + ADR 0056 doctrine now complete (U1 RLS + U2 enablement). **Next:** onboarding U5‚ÄìU7 (WP seeding: template-by-type / copy-from-project / CSV import).

## Spec 142 U6 ‚Äî copy work packages from another project (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-18.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1091 unit (159 files; +3 TDD: copy-work-packages-sheet)** ¬∑ green; **pgTAP file 71 green post-push (11/11; full suite 71 files / 1466 asserts, 0 failures)**; `db:push` applied `20260729000000`; `db:types` reconciled. (Reordered ahead of U5-templates ‚Äî copy needs no invented content; U5's per-type seed is operator domain knowledge, saved for last.) PM/super get a **ý∏Ñý∏±ý∏îý∏•ý∏≠ý∏Åý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô** button beside add-WP ‚Üí pick a source project ‚Üí its WP skeleton clones in. **Migration** `20260729000000_clone_work_packages.sql`: `clone_work_packages(p_src,p_dst) returns integer` ‚Äî SECURITY DEFINER, PM/super; copies **skeleton only** (code/name/description; status defaults not_started, no logs/photos/owner/schedule/money); `on conflict (project_id,code) do nothing` ‚Üí **idempotent**, skips codes the dst already has; returns rows inserted; rejects src=dst + unknown project (22023). **UI:** `copyWorkPackages(sourceId, projectId)` action (PM_ROLES gate, maps 42501/22023, returns `{ok,count}`); `CopyWorkPackagesSheet` (BottomSheet + source picker ‚Üí action ‚Üí router.refresh). Page wiring: PM-only fetch of other projects `neq self` ‚Äî **RLS-scoped, so the source list is only the PM's own projects** (a nice consequence of spec 143); button shown only when sources exist. **pgTAP file 71** (plan 11): catalog+secdef, clone-2, skeleton landed (description copied, status not_started), idempotent re-clone (0), partial skip (1), site_admin deny (42501), src=dst + unknown-project (22023). Test needed PM enrolled in the clone fixtures (the clone RPC is definer + works, but the PM-session verification reads are membership-scoped now ‚Äî spec 143 interaction). **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated page ‚Üí /login in preview; 3 component tests + pgTAP 71 carry correctness; operator on-device = acceptance). **Decision:** source = any visible project (not same-type-only) ‚Äî more useful with few projects + RLS already scopes it. Spec 142 doc gains U6. **Next:** U7 ‚Äî CSV import surfaced in the UI (reuse `src/lib/wp-import/parse.ts`); then U5 ‚Äî WP template by project_type (mechanism + a starter seed for the operator to edit).

## Spec 142 U7 ‚Äî import work packages from CSV (paste) (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-18.** App-only, **no migration** (reuses the wp-import parser + U4's create_work_package RPC). lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1094 unit (160 files; +3 TDD: import-work-packages-sheet)** ¬∑ green. Surfaces the CLI-only `pnpm import:wp` flow in-app for the non-dev operator. PM/super get a **ý∏ôý∏≥ýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ CSV** button ‚Üí paste rows (`code,name,description` with header) ‚Üí valid rows created. **Chose paste-textarea over file upload** ‚Äî robust + testable (jsdom File.text() is flaky) + mobile-friendly (operator is on mobile). **Action** `importWorkPackagesCsv(projectId, csvText)`: PM_ROLES gate ‚Üí fetch existing codes (RLS read; PM is a member) ‚Üí `parseAndValidate(csv, existingCodes)` (ADR 0014 parser ‚Äî header-based, dup-in-file + already-exists + blank checks) ‚Üí on parser errors return first 8 joined (textarea lets fix+retry) ‚Üí else create each valid row via `create_work_package` **sequentially** (definer RPC sidesteps the work_packages table-grant; sequential so a mid-batch failure reports how far it got) ‚Üí `{ok, inserted}`. **UI:** `ImportWorkPackagesSheet` (BottomSheet + monospace textarea + multi-line `whitespace-pre-line` error alert + router.refresh). Page wiring: added to the PM-only WP-heading button group (now `ý∏ôý∏≥ýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ CSV` ¬∑ `ý∏Ñý∏±ý∏îý∏•ý∏≠ý∏Åý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô` ¬∑ `+ ýπÄý∏ûý∏¥ýπàý∏°ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô`, flex-wrap). **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated page ‚Üí /login in preview; 3 component tests cover submit-gate/success-refresh/error; the parser has its own unit suite; create_work_package green in pgTAP 69; operator on-device = acceptance). Spec 142 doc gains U7. **Seam:** per-row RPC (fine for onboarding-sized CSVs; a bulk RPC is a later optimization if imports get large). **Onboarding WP-seeding now: manual (U4) ¬∑ copy-from-project (U6) ¬∑ CSV (U7) all shipped.** **Next / last:** U5 ‚Äî WP template by project_type (`wp_templates` table + `apply_wp_template` RPC + a starter seed the operator edits).

## Spec 142 U5 ‚Äî WP template by project_type (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-18.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1096 unit (161 files; +2 TDD: apply-template-button)** ¬∑ green; **pgTAP file 72 green post-push (11/11; full suite 72 files / 1477 asserts, 0 failures)**; `db:push` applied `20260730000000`; `db:types` reconciled. The last seeding path: a typed project can seed a standard WP breakdown in one tap. **Migration** `20260730000000_wp_templates.sql`: `wp_templates` (project_type, code, name, description, sort_order; unique(project_type, code)) ‚Äî **reference data, read-only to authenticated** (SELECT `using(true)`; no write policies ‚Üí seeded by migration/service-role only; the apply RPC is definer so it reads regardless). **Seeded GENERIC starter phases** per type (new_building 7, renovation 5, factory_warehouse 6, infrastructure 5, systems 5; 'other' none) ‚Äî a starting point the **operator should refine** (a template editor is a future unit; flagged). `apply_wp_template(p_project_id) returns integer` ‚Äî SECURITY DEFINER, PM/super; reads the project's type, inserts matching templates into work_packages `on conflict do nothing` (idempotent), returns rows inserted; typeless project ‚Üí 0, unknown ‚Üí 22023. **UI:** `applyWpTemplate(projectId)` action (PM gate, maps 42501/22023) + `ApplyTemplateButton` ("ýπÉý∏äýπâýπÄý∏óý∏°ýπÄý∏ûý∏•ý∏ïý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô", pending + inline error + router.refresh). Page wiring: PM-only `wp_templates` count-by-type check ‚Üí button shown only when a template exists for the project's type. WP-heading button group is now `ýπÉý∏äýπâýπÄý∏óý∏°ýπÄý∏ûý∏•ý∏ïý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô ¬∑ ý∏ôý∏≥ýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ CSV ¬∑ ý∏Ñý∏±ý∏îý∏•ý∏≠ý∏Åý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô ¬∑ + ýπÄý∏ûý∏¥ýπàý∏°ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô` (flex-wrap). **pgTAP file 72** (plan 11): table+RLS, function+secdef, seed present+staff-readable, apply=template-count, WPs landed, idempotent re-apply (0), typeless‚Üí0, visitor+site_admin deny (42501). **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated page ‚Üí /login in preview; 2 component tests + pgTAP 72 carry correctness; operator on-device = acceptance). **Decision:** seeded generic defaults (the "hardcode v1" fork) rather than blocking on operator-authored content ‚Äî refine later via an editor. Spec 142 doc gains U5.

**SPEC 142 COMPLETE (U1‚ÄìU7 all on prod): create_project + suggest_code (U1) ¬∑ create modal (U2) ¬∑ onboarding checklist (U3) ¬∑ add WP (U4) ¬∑ template-by-type (U5) ¬∑ copy-from-project (U6) ¬∑ CSV import (U7).** Full onboarding: create a project ‚Üí guided checklist ‚Üí seed work packages four ways. SPEC 143 also complete (U1 RLS isolation + U2 coordinator enablement).

## Spec 143 U3 ‚Äî complete the membership-scoping U1 missed (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-18.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1096 unit** ¬∑ green; **full pgTAP suite 73 files / 1489 asserts, 0 failures** post-push; `db:push` applied `20260731000000` + `20260732000000` (fix-forward, below); `db:types` reconciled. A **full-project-lifecycle audit** (4 parallel explorers) found U1's "full isolation" was incomplete: three project-scoped child tables still had role-level SELECT (cross-project leak to PM/site_admin), and the SELECT-only U1 left the app write policies role-level (a non-member could write a row for a project they can't read). **Migration `20260731000000`:** scoped `labor_logs` (staff SELECT only ‚Äî the bound-contractor self-read untouched) + `work_package_dependencies` SELECT via `can_see_wp`; mirrored the membership gate onto the **8 app write policies** (photo_logs INSERT, approvals INSERT, purchase_requests app-INSERT, deliverables INSERT+UPDATE, reports INSERT, work_packages UPDATE ‚Äî each KEEPS its role list so `project_coordinator` stays read-only; `labor_logs`/`wp_dependencies` are RPC-write-only so no write policy to mirror). Authoritative current policies pulled from `pg_policies` first (not stale files). **Self-inflicted regression + fix-forward:** `20260731000000` also tried to scope `photo_markups` (SELECT‚Üí`can_see_photo_log`, + helper). `photo_markups`' INSERT policy self-references `photo_markups` (tombstone-target EXISTS) ‚Äî safe only under a trivial role SELECT policy; under a function SELECT policy Postgres raised **42P17 infinite recursion** on every markup INSERT (caught by pgTAP files 31 + the test, but the migration was already pushed ‚Üí prod markup-insert briefly broken). **`20260732000000`** reverts `photo_markups` to its original working policies + drops the unused `can_see_photo_log` ‚Äî prod un-broken. **pgTAP file 73** (plan 12): labor_logs + wp_dependencies + 7 write-mirror wirings + wp_dependencies end-to-end (member sees dep / non-member doesn't). **Two existing suites repaired:** `29-labor-capture` got a membership seed (its `correct_labor_log` id-lookup reads labor_logs as the PM ‚Äî now scoped); `31-photo-markups` returns to green once `photo_markups` reverted. **Verified-by-checklist** (RLS is the SUT; pgTAP 73 + the suite carry it). **Lesson:** scoping a SELECT policy on a table whose own write policy self-joins that table triggers 42P17 (ADR 0011 trap) ‚Äî needs a SECURITY DEFINER tombstone-target helper (pr_attachment_tombstone_target_ok precedent). **Remaining leak (documented, deferred to U4):** `photo_markups` is still role-level (low sensitivity ‚Äî drawing annotations). `labor_logs` (the sensitive one ‚Äî worker/day data) IS closed. **SPEC 143 now: U1 (RLS) + U2 (coordinator) + U3 (labor_logs/wp_dependencies + write-mirror) on prod; U4 (photo_markups via definer helper) is the one open item.**

## Spec 143 U4 ‚Äî scope photo_markups (recursion-free) (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-18. SPEC 143 COMPLETE.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1096 unit** ¬∑ green; **full pgTAP suite 74 files / 1501 asserts, 0 failures** post-push; `db:push` applied `20260733000000`; `db:types` reconciled. Closes the last read-leak the lifecycle audit found ‚Äî `photo_markups` was still role-level (cross-project). **Migration `20260733000000`:** recreates `can_see_photo_log(uuid)` (dropped in U3's revert) + adds `photo_markup_tombstone_target_ok(uuid,uuid)` ‚Äî a SECURITY DEFINER helper that performs the tombstone-target self-read (bypassing RLS, `auth.uid()` still the caller so the creator-pin holds). `photo_markups` SELECT ‚Üí `can_see_photo_log`; INSERT keeps the content/own-tombstone rule but the tombstone branch now calls the helper instead of an inline `EXISTS (select ‚Ä¶ from photo_markups ‚Ä¶)`, so the policy no longer self-joins the table ‚Üí **no 42P17** (the U3 failure mode). Role list kept (coordinator read-only). **pgTAP file 74** (plan 12): both helpers exist + secdef, SELECT/INSERT wiring, and behaviourally the **exact content+tombstone INSERT that recursed in U3 now `lives_ok`** for a member, non-member sees 0 + INSERT denied, visitor sees 0. **Existing `31-photo-markups` (the comprehensive markup insert/tombstone/current-view suite) passes under the scoped policies** (its users are members via the U1 seed) ‚Äî strong real-world coverage that the recursion is gone and the member path is intact. **Lesson applied:** the pr_attachment_tombstone_target_ok pattern (definer helper for a self-referential append-only policy) is the canonical fix whenever such a table gets a function-based SELECT policy. **ADR 0056 "full isolation" is now actually complete** across all project-scoped tables (projects, work_packages, deliverables, photo_logs, approvals, purchase_requests, reports, labor_logs, work_package_dependencies, photo_markups); the only remaining write-side note is the labor RPCs (log_labor_day/correct_labor_log) gate on role + WP-status but not membership ‚Äî residual defense-in-depth (UI is read-locked for non-members), recorded, not exploitable.

## Spec 144 U1 ‚Äî defect rework: 'rework' status + reopen RPC (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-18.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **1097 unit (+1: rework transition assert)** ¬∑ green; **full pgTAP suite 75 files / 1511 asserts, 0 failures** post-push; `db:push` applied `20260734000000` (enum) + `20260734000100` (RPC); `db:types` reconciled.
