The U9 count engine gets its UI: count an item, see the variance, reconcile on-hand. **Surface:** each on-hand row on `/store` gets a `ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏àý∏ôý∏±ý∏ö` button (alongside `ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å`), gated to the **manager tier** (`canIssue` = isManagerRole = PM/super/director ‚Äî the /store-access ‚à© `record_stock_count`-gate set; procurement on /store can't count, site_admin keeps the physical store but has no /store access ‚Üí a **site_admin store-count surface is flagged** like the U5 field-ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å gap, but counting is store-wide with no per-WP analog) ‚Üí a BottomSheet pre-scoped to that item: shows "ý∏£ý∏∞ý∏öý∏öý∏°ý∏µ N unit", a **ý∏àý∏≥ý∏ôý∏ßý∏ôý∏óý∏µýπàý∏ôý∏±ý∏öýπÑý∏îýπâ** input with a **live variance preview** (`ý∏™ýπàý∏ßý∏ôý∏ïýπàý∏≤ý∏á ¬±N (ý∏Çý∏≤ý∏î/ýπÄý∏Åý∏¥ý∏ô/ý∏ïý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏±ý∏ô)`, colored text-danger/text-action/text-ink-muted), + note ‚Üí `recordStockCount` ‚Üí refresh. **`StoreManager`** gains the count-sheet state (a third `useTransition`) + the per-row button; **`recordStockCount` server action** (`src/app/store/actions.ts`) validates uuids + `countedQty >= 0`, maps the RPC's 42501 ‚Üí no-permission and 22023 ‚Üí "ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏àý∏ôý∏±ý∏öýπÑý∏îýπâýπÄý∏âý∏ûý∏≤ý∏∞ý∏ßý∏±ý∏™ý∏îý∏∏ý∏óý∏µýπàý∏°ý∏µýπÉý∏ôý∏™ýπÇý∏ïý∏£ýπå ý∏´ý∏£ý∏∑ý∏≠ý∏Çýπâý∏≠ý∏°ý∏πý∏•ýπÑý∏°ýπàý∏ñý∏πý∏Åý∏ïýπâý∏≠ý∏á", `revalidatePath('/store')`. **No DB / migration / guards / nav** (rides the U9 RPC + existing /store route). **Test (written first, RED proven ‚Äî 3 failed/13 passed before, 16/16 after):** `store-manager.test.tsx` count describe (the ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏àý∏ôý∏±ý∏ö control per row + gating, the live variance preview, the `recordStockCount` call shape). Self-inflicted test fix: `getByText(/-2/)` also matched the `PRC-2026-002` project option ‚Üí tightened to `/ý∏™ýπàý∏ßý∏ôý∏ïýπàý∏≤ý∏á -2/`. **Browser pass = operator on-device.** **Out of scope (flagged):** a site_admin store-count surface; a count-history view; reversals of receipt/issue; found-untracked-stock costing; wiring issue cost into `wp_profit`; the late margin layer; the deferred /store-manager receiver picker + manager confirm-on-behalf. **NEXT (Phase 3 cont.):** reversals (append-only negative receipt/issue correction), then the late margin layer (sell-to-WP per-level, flip `wp_profit` to at-issue-sell-price ‚Äî the cost-first dial is still locked).

## Spec 177 ‚Äî Store + Stock-In, U11: reversals (undo a wrong ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ / ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å) (2026-06-22)

Status: **U11 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-22.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest 216 files / 1430** (DB-only ‚Äî no unit delta) ¬∑ **db:test 134 files / 2520 / 0** (file 185, 18 asserts) ¬∑ `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809000500`; db:types regenerated. The correction mechanism: a wrong stock movement is undone by an append-only reversal that reverses its on-hand effect ‚Äî never by editing the original. **Design (spec 177 ¬ßU11, DB-only; the reversal UI is U12):** `stock_reversals` (append-only: project_id, catalog_item_id, **a TYPED FK to the reversed `receipt_id` OR `issue_id` ‚Äî exactly one** per CLAUDE.md's no-mixed-content-reference rule, enforced by a `(receipt_id is not null) <> (issue_id is not null)` CHECK; `qty` + `value_delta` (the on-hand value change applied ‚Äî negative for a receipt reversal, positive for an issue reversal); note, reversed_by ‚Äî no UPDATE/DELETE policy). **A partial unique index per FK (`where ‚Ä¶ is not null`) blocks reversing the same movement twice (‚Üí 23505).** **RPC `reverse_stock_receipt(receipt_id, note default null)`** (BACK_OFFICE gate ‚Äî the ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ audience; membership = `can_see_project OR procurement`): loads the receipt, **locks on-hand and guards `qty_on_hand >= receipt.qty`** (you cannot un-receive stock that has since been issued out ‚Üí 22023), inserts the reversal (unique check first), then subtracts `qty` + `total_cost` (depletion ‚Üí value 0). **RPC `reverse_stock_issue(issue_id, note default null)`** (SITE_STAFF gate + `can_see_project`): inserts the reversal, then **adds the issue's `qty` + `total_cost` back** to on-hand (no negative risk). **READ:** `stock_reversals` SELECT = `can_see_project OR procurement` (wrapped role call). **Migration `20260809000500`, pgTAP `185`** (structure + anon-deny; procurement reverses a 30-receipt ‚Üí on-hand 50‚Üí20 / value 500‚Üí200 / value_delta ‚àí300; double-reverse ‚Üí 23505; a receipt whose qty > on-hand ‚Üí 22023; site_admin reverses a 10-issue ‚Üí on-hand 20‚Üí30 / value_delta +100; double-reverse issue ‚Üí 23505; visitor + non-member denies 42501; member reads the reversal history). **One trap caught by db:test + fixed forward ‚Üí [[prc-ops-db-migration-lessons]]:** the receipt reversal checked the on-hand guard (`qty >= receipt.qty`) BEFORE the insert, so a double-reverse ‚Äî where the first reversal already dropped on-hand below the receipt qty ‚Äî raised 22023 (guard) instead of the intended 23505 (unique). Fix: **do the unique insert FIRST**, then the on-hand guard (a failed guard still rolls the insert back since the whole RPC call aborts atomically); applied live via `CREATE OR REPLACE` + edited the migration file (file==live). Lesson: **when an RPC has both a uniqueness constraint and a state guard, attempt the unique write before the state check** so the "already done" error wins over the "state changed" error. **Out of scope (flagged):** **U12 = the reversal UI** (a ý∏Åý∏•ý∏±ý∏öý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ button on the recent-receipts/issues lists ‚Üí `reverse_stock_receipt`/`reverse_stock_issue`, with a confirm); the custody implication of reversing a CONFIRMED issue (the material returns after the worker attested ‚Äî allowed as a correction, surfaced later); intervening-activity makes a receipt reversal's avg approximate (exact for the immediate-mistake case); the late margin layer. **NEXT (Phase 3 cont.):** U12 ‚Äî the reversal UI, then the late margin layer (needs an operator decision ‚Äî it rewires `wp_profit`).

## Spec 177 ‚Äî Store + Stock-In, U12: the reversal UI (ý∏Åý∏•ý∏±ý∏öý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ on /store) (2026-06-22)

Status: **U12 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-22.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest 216 files / 1435 (+5: store-manager reversal)** ¬∑ `pnpm build` green. **No DB change** (reuses the U11 `reverse_stock_receipt` / `reverse_stock_issue` RPCs) ‚Üí no migration, no db:test delta. The U11 reversal engine gets its UI: undo a wrong ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤/ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å from /store with a confirm. **Surface:** `/store` gains a **ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ý∏•ýπàý∏≤ý∏™ý∏∏ý∏î** (recent receipts, last 10) list ‚Äî each row a `ý∏Åý∏•ý∏±ý∏öý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£` control; and the existing **ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Åý∏•ýπàý∏≤ý∏™ý∏∏ý∏î** list gains a `ý∏Åý∏•ý∏±ý∏öý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£` control per issue. Both use the **shared `ConfirmActionButton`** (spec 67 ‚Äî button ‚Üí themed ConfirmDialog ‚Üí action in a transition ‚Üí refresh; replaces window.confirm) with a per-row confirm message ("‚Ä¶ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏áý∏àý∏∞ý∏ñý∏πý∏Åý∏ïý∏±ý∏îý∏≠ý∏≠ý∏Åý∏àý∏≤ý∏Åý∏™ýπÇý∏ïý∏£ýπå" / "‚Ä¶ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏áý∏àý∏∞ý∏ñý∏πý∏Åý∏Ñý∏∑ý∏ôýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ý∏™ýπÇý∏ïý∏£ýπå"). **Gates (the key asymmetry):** receipt reversal = `reverse_stock_receipt` gate = **BACK_OFFICE = exactly the /store audience**, so the receipt ý∏Åý∏•ý∏±ý∏öý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ shows for **every /store user (incl. procurement ‚Äî it records ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤)**; issue reversal = `reverse_stock_issue` gate = SITE_STAFF ‚Üí on /store that is the **manager tier**, so the issue ý∏Åý∏•ý∏±ý∏öý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ is gated on `canIssue` (procurement can't reverse an issue). **`StoreManager`** gains a `receipts` prop + the two `ConfirmActionButton`s; **`reverseStockReceipt` / `reverseStockIssue` server actions** (`src/app/store/actions.ts`) map the RPCs' 42501 / 23505 ("ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ý∏ôý∏µýπâý∏ñý∏πý∏Åý∏Åý∏•ý∏±ý∏öýπÑý∏õýπÅý∏•ýπâý∏ß") / 22023 ("ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏áý∏ñý∏πý∏ÅýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Åý∏≠ý∏≠ý∏ÅýπÑý∏õýπÅý∏•ýπâý∏ß ý∏Åý∏•ý∏±ý∏öý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ýπÑý∏°ýπàýπÑý∏îýπâ"). **Page:** /store fetches the project's recent `stock_receipts` (last 10, joined catalog_items). **No DB / migration / guards / nav.** **Test (written first, RED proven ‚Äî 4 failed/17 passed before, 21/21 after):** `store-manager.test.tsx` reversal describe (the recent-ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ list + its ý∏Åý∏•ý∏±ý∏öý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ control for any user; the receipt reversal after confirm ‚Üí `reverseStockReceipt`; the issue ý∏Åý∏•ý∏±ý∏öý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ only when `canIssue`; the issue reversal after confirm ‚Üí `reverseStockIssue`). **Browser pass = operator on-device.** **Out of scope (flagged):** reversal controls on the WP-detail issues list (only /store for now); the custody implication of reversing a confirmed issue; the late margin layer. **NEXT (Phase 3 cont.):** the LATE MARGIN LAYER ‚Äî sell-to-WP at a per-level price, flip `wp_profit` to at-issue-sell-price. **This is a FORK that needs an operator decision** (it rewires `wp_profit` and unlocks the cost-first dial); bring the design choice via AskUserQuestion before building.

## Spec 178 ‚Äî Store margin layer (transfer pricing), U1: sell-rate data + setter (2026-06-22)

Status: **U1 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-22.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest 216 files / 1435** ¬∑ **db:test 135 files / 2534 / 0** (+1 file 186, +14 asserts) ¬∑ `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809000600`; db:types regenerated (app + worker). The BIG FORK resolved: operator AskUserQuestion locked all four recommended dials ‚Äî (1) **flat per-item sell rate** (global, not markup, not per-project), (2) sell **captured at issue**, (3) `wp_profit` materials = **ADD store-sell beside GL purchases** (disjoint source, no double-count), (4) external/3rd-party sales **deferred**. U1 = the data foundation (DB-only, mirrors `set_sell_rate` from spec 161 U1): `item_sell_rates` (PK `catalog_item_id`, `sell_rate numeric(12,2) >= 0`, **zero authenticated grant** = the margin-sensitive money posture) + `set_item_sell_rate(p_catalog_item_id, p_sell_rate)` definer (super_admin gate, upsert, audit row, anon EXECUTE revoked). mig `20260809000600`, pgTAP file 186. **Trap caught by db:test (1 file failed, 0 of its asserts ran): the test read the zero-grant `item_sell_rates` while `set local role authenticated` ‚Üí permission-denied aborts the WHOLE transactional file (the \_tap_buf inserts roll back too ‚Üí "not ok - 186" with 0 assertions). Fix = read the zero-grant table only as OWNER (before `set role` / after `reset role`); only the setter CALLS run under `authenticated` (the spec-161 pgTAP-98 pattern).** Convention note: the store family uses `22023` for value/existence validations (vs the labour family's `P0001`) ‚Äî kept `22023` here for store consistency.

## Spec 178 ‚Äî Store margin layer, U2: issue snapshots the sell price (2026-06-22)

Status: **U2 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-22.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest 216 files / 1435** ¬∑ **db:test 136 files / 2546 / 0** (+1 file 187, +12 asserts) ¬∑ `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809000700`; db:types regenerated (+sell_price/total_sell on stock_issues, app + worker). The WP "buys" the stock at issue: `stock_issues` += `sell_price numeric(12,2)` (nullable ‚Äî legacy/cost-only rows have none) + `total_sell numeric(16,2)` generated `qty * sell_price`. `issue_stock` (**same 6-arg signature ‚Üí CREATE OR REPLACE, body-only, grants + pgTAP 182/183 sig pins preserved, no DROP**) computes `v_sell = coalesce((select sell_rate from item_sell_rates where catalog_item_id=‚Ä¶), v_avg)` ‚Äî the **costing rule**: an unpriced item sells AT COST (zero store margin), never null, so wp_profit (U4) is never understated. The snapshot is immutable (append-only) ‚Äî changing a rate only affects future issues (pgTAP proves: bump 50‚Üí80, the old issue keeps 50). issue_stock is SECURITY DEFINER so it reads the zero-grant `item_sell_rates` as owner. **Lesson re-applied:** miswrote `plan(10)` for 12 assertions (the bare `set_item_sell_rate` call isn't an assertion but the 3 post-bump checks are) ‚Üí fixed to `plan(12)`. NEXT = U3 `store_pnl` read (per-item cost/sell/margin + shrinkage, reversed issues excluded).

## Spec 179 ‚Äî Purchase request links a catalog item (2026-06-22)

Status: **U1+U2 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-22.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest 217 files / 1441 (+6: validate-purchase-request catalogItemId ¬∑ purchase-request-form-catalog)** ¬∑ **db:test 137 files / 2554 / 0** (+1 file 188, +8 asserts) ¬∑ `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809000800`; db:types regenerated (app + worker). Procurement's request (spec 171): "items not selectable yet" ‚Üí operator AskUserQuestion chose the **real FK link** (not a UI snapshot). `purchase_requests` gains a **nullable `catalog_item_id` FK ‚Üí catalog_items** (spec 175 master); picking a catalog item links it AND prefills `item_description` (`baseItem` + ` specAttrs`) + `unit` (a human snapshot that survives a later catalog edit/deactivation); the off-catalog free-text path stays (link null). **Posture mirrors `reason_code` (spec 176 U4):** nullable, NO default (legacy rows null), link set ONCE at create ‚Üí INSERT-granted, not UPDATE-granted. **THE GRANT TRAP (caught proactively, not by a red db:test):** `purchase_requests` carries **column-scoped INSERT grants** to `authenticated` (mig `20260616000400` revoked table-level insert + re-granted an explicit column list), so a new column is NOT covered ‚Üí the session-client insert would be denied; the migration adds `grant insert (catalog_item_id) ‚Ä¶ to authenticated`. **SELECT is table-level (mig `20260608120000`, never revoked) ‚Üí the new column is readable without a grant** (unlike the spec-174 column-grant-SELECT trap ‚Äî different table, different posture). No RLS change (the INSERT policy pins requested_by/source/role only; the FK is unconstrained + nullable). **Validator** `validateCreatePurchaseRequest` += optional `catalogItemId` (uuid-or-null; blank/null/omitted ‚Üí null; bad uuid ‚Üí "ý∏£ý∏´ý∏±ý∏™ý∏ßý∏±ý∏™ý∏îý∏∏ýπÉý∏ôýπÅý∏Ñý∏ïý∏ïý∏≤ý∏•ýπáý∏≠ý∏ÅýπÑý∏°ýπàý∏ñý∏πý∏Åý∏ïýπâý∏≠ý∏á"); the exact-shape `toEqual` test updated with `catalogItemId: null`. **Action** `createPurchaseRequest` writes `catalog_item_id`. **Form** (`purchase-request-form.tsx`) += optional `catalogItems` prop (omitted/empty ‚Üí picker hidden = free-text-only, **so the existing form tests are unaffected**) + a `ýπÄý∏•ý∏∑ý∏≠ý∏Åý∏ßý∏±ý∏™ý∏îý∏∏ý∏àý∏≤ý∏ÅýπÅý∏Ñý∏ïý∏ïý∏≤ý∏•ýπáý∏≠ý∏Å` `<select>` grouped by `item_category` (mirrors the supply-plan picker; option `baseItem ¬∑ specAttrs (unit)`, a `ý∏ôý∏≠ý∏ÅýπÅý∏Ñý∏ïý∏ïý∏≤ý∏•ýπáý∏≠ý∏Å (ý∏ûý∏¥ý∏°ý∏ûýπåýπÄý∏≠ý∏á)` value="" escape hatch); picking prefills the description + unit (a catalog unit outside COMMON_UNITS falls to the ý∏≠ý∏∑ýπàý∏ô ýπÜ free-text unit input). **Both form callers feed the active catalog:** `projects/[id]/work-packages/[wpId]` (procurement + SA ‚Äî added to the existing Promise.all) and `review/work-packages/[wpId]` (PM review ‚Äî a new fetch). **Migration `20260809000800`, pgTAP `188`** (column nullable-uuid + no-default; FK to catalog_items; INSERT+SELECT granted, UPDATE not ‚Äî set once). **Test-first proven:** 6 validator/form cases RED before impl. **Out of scope (flagged):** reconciling PR ‚Üí stock-in ‚Üí on-hand by `catalog_item_id` (the payoff the link unlocks ‚Äî a later store unit); spend-by-item analytics; back-filling historical free-text requests; a typeahead/search picker (the grouped `<select>` matches supply-plan; a search box is a later UX unit if the catalog grows). **NEXT = the rest of procurement's "few requests" (operator to explain).**

## Spec 180 ‚Äî PR item is catalog-only + searchable (2026-06-23)

Status: **U1 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest 217 files / 1444 (+3: catalog-search rewrite 3‚Üí6; reason/priority retargeted to search)** ¬∑ `pnpm build` green. **No DB change** (form-layer behaviour over the spec-179 column) ‚Üí no migration, no db:test delta. Two operator follow-ups to spec 179: (1) "anything outside catalog must be **registered first**" ‚Üí the PR item is now **catalog-only** (the `ý∏ôý∏≠ý∏ÅýπÅý∏Ñý∏ïý∏ïý∏≤ý∏•ýπáý∏≠ý∏Å` free-text escape hatch is GONE); (2) "typing in item selection **searches** the catalog" ‚Üí the grouped `<select>` becomes a typeahead. **Operator AskUserQuestion on the register-flow ‚Üí chose "must register on setting"** (not inline add, not widen-to-SA): registration stays a Settings activity at `ý∏ïý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏Ñýπàý∏≤ ‚Üí ýπÅý∏Ñý∏ïý∏ïý∏≤ý∏•ýπáý∏≠ý∏Å` (`/catalog`, spec 175, BACK_OFFICE-gated). Of PR requesters only **site_admin** isn't a catalog curator (PM/super/**procurement** all are) ‚Äî a missing item means asking a curator to register it; preserves the curated anti-drift catalog (the point of spec 175). **Form reshape (`purchase-request-form.tsx`, the ONLY code surface ‚Äî no DB/RPC/route/nav):** removed the free-text `ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ý∏ßý∏±ý∏™ý∏îý∏∏` input, the `ý∏´ý∏ôýπàý∏ßý∏¢` dropdown + `ý∏≠ý∏∑ýπàý∏ô ýπÜ` unit-other, and the spec-179 grouped `<select>` (+ the `COMMON_UNITS`/`UNIT_OTHER_VALUE` machinery). New UX: a `ý∏Ñýπâý∏ôý∏´ý∏≤ý∏ßý∏±ý∏™ý∏îý∏∏ý∏àý∏≤ý∏ÅýπÅý∏Ñý∏ïý∏ïý∏≤ý∏•ýπáý∏≠ý∏Å` search input (dependency-free ‚Äî no combobox primitive exists; a normalised `contains` over `baseItem+specAttrs+unit`) ‚Üí a short clickable result list (item ¬∑ spec (unit) + category subtitle); picking sets `catalogItemId` and **derives** `item_description` (`baseItem`+specAttrs) + `unit` as an immutable read-only chip with a `ýπÄý∏õý∏•ý∏µýπàý∏¢ý∏ô` action; ý∏´ý∏°ý∏≤ý∏¢ýπÄý∏´ý∏ïý∏∏ still carries brand/model refinement. A **no-match** query shows `ýπÑý∏°ýπàý∏ûý∏öý∏ßý∏±ý∏™ý∏îý∏∏ý∏óý∏µýπàý∏Ñýπâý∏ôý∏´ý∏≤ ‚Äî ýπÄý∏ûý∏¥ýπàý∏°ý∏ßý∏±ý∏™ý∏îý∏∏ý∏óý∏µýπà ý∏ïý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏Ñýπàý∏≤ ‚Üí ýπÅý∏Ñý∏ïý∏ïý∏≤ý∏•ýπáý∏≠ý∏Å` linking `/catalog`. **`catalogItems` prop is now REQUIRED** (both callers already pass it). **Submit gated on a chosen item** (`catalogItemId !== ""`); the shared **validator stays lenient** (catalogItemId optional uuid-or-null) so the non-form create paths (AppSheet, `record_site_purchase`, legacy) ‚Äî `catalog_item_id` correctly NULL ‚Äî are unaffected; the catalog-only rule lives at the form (the needed_by/priority UX-only posture, ADR 0026). **Tests (RED-first):** `purchase-request-form-catalog.test.tsx` rewritten (no free-text item input; typing filters; select ‚Üí chip + ýπÄý∏õý∏•ý∏µýπàý∏¢ý∏ô + search hidden; submit gating; submit sends linked id + derived desc/unit; no-match ‚Üí /catalog link) + `-priority`/`-reason` retargeted to search+pick. **Two self-inflicted test fixes:** (a) a dynamic `ý∏àý∏≥ý∏ôý∏ßý∏ô (unit)` label broke `getByLabelText("ý∏àý∏≥ý∏ôý∏ßý∏ô")` ‚Üí reverted to plain (unit shows in the chip); (b) a typed-spread mock wrapper ‚Üí TS2556 ‚Üí `vi.mocked(createPurchaseRequest)`. **Browser pass = operator on-device.** **Out of scope (flagged):** catalog-only on `record_site_purchase` (ad-hoc on-site cash buy stays free-text unless extended); inline add-to-catalog (operator chose Settings-only); arrow-key result navigation (revisit if the catalog grows large); making `catalog_item_id` NOT NULL. **NEXT = the rest of procurement's "few requests".**

## Spec 180 ‚Äî PR item picker, U2: pro-max bottom-sheet (2026-06-23)

Status: **U2 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest 217 files / 1445 (+1: catalog picker now 8 cases ‚Äî adds the category-chip filter)** ¬∑ `pnpm build` green. **No DB change** (`catalog_items.image_path` already exists since spec 175 U4 ‚Äî the WP pages just select + sign it) ‚Üí no migration, no db:test delta. Operator on the U1 inline search: **"UX is weird, any better ones? Uxui pro max."** The U1 picker dumped all ~71 catalog items in an always-open inline `<ul>` under the field ‚Äî a wall that shoved the form down. AskUserQuestion ‚Üí **full pro-max** (bottom sheet + chips + thumbnails). **New component `CatalogItemPicker` (`src/components/features/purchasing/catalog-item-picker.tsx`):** the app's `BottomSheet` idiom (same primitive as ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å/stock/supply-plan) ‚Äî a trigger button `ýπÄý∏•ý∏∑ý∏≠ý∏Åý∏ßý∏±ý∏™ý∏îý∏∏ý∏àý∏≤ý∏ÅýπÅý∏Ñý∏ïý∏ïý∏≤ý∏•ýπáý∏≠ý∏Å` opens a sheet with a **pinned search** (`ý∏Ñýπâý∏ôý∏´ý∏≤ý∏ßý∏±ý∏™ý∏îý∏∏`, autofocus via rAF after the sheet's own focus-on-open), **category filter chips** (`RadioChip` ‚Äî the `/catalog` "select category first" pattern, spec 175 U6; ý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏´ý∏°ý∏î + each present category), and **thumbnail result rows** (40px image or placeholder, base+spec, unit, category subtitle) with the **matched substring highlighted** (`highlight()` wraps the case-insensitive match in `text-action font-semibold`). Picking closes the sheet ‚Üí a selected **chip** (thumbnail + name + `category ¬∑ unit` + `ýπÄý∏õý∏•ý∏µýπàý∏¢ý∏ô`); `ýπÄý∏õý∏•ý∏µýπàý∏¢ý∏ô` clears + reopens. No-match ‚Üí a dashed register-CTA card ‚Üí `/catalog`. **Thumbnails plumbed:** `PurchaseRequestCatalogItem` gains `thumbnailUrl: string | null`; both WP pages (`projects/[id]/work-packages/[wpId]` + `review/...`) now select `image_path` and `mintSignedUrls(CATALOG_IMAGES_BUCKET, ‚Ä¶)` (private bucket ‚Üí service-role signed URLs; rows already read under the user's RLS) ‚Äî same pattern as `/catalog`. **The form (`purchase-request-form.tsx`) shrank:** its inline search JSX + the `catalogQuery`/`catalogMatches` state + the `Link`/`ITEM_CATEGORY_LABEL` imports moved into the picker; the form keeps `catalogItemId` + derives `item_description`/`unit` and passes `selectCatalogItem`/`clearCatalogItem` to the picker. **Tests (RED-first):** `purchase-request-form-catalog.test.tsx` rewritten for the sheet flow (trigger ‚Üí search ‚Üí category chip ‚Üí result ‚Üí chip; submit gating; submit payload; no-match ‚Üí /catalog); `-reason` retargeted to open the sheet first. BottomSheet portals to `document.body` so testing-library `screen` still queries it. **Self-inflicted:** left a `setCatalogQuery("")` in the submit-reset after deleting that state (TS2304 + a test ReferenceError) ‚Üí removed. **Browser pass = operator on-device** (form behind LINE auth + project/WP context). **NEXT = the rest of procurement's "few requests".**

## Spec 180 ‚Äî PR item picker, U3: category tap no longer ghost-selects an item (2026-06-23)

Status: **U3 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest unchanged (217 / 1445)** ¬∑ `pnpm build` green. **No DB change, single-file fix.** Operator: "tapping item categories sometimes turns into item selection." Root cause: the category chips are `RadioChip`s (an sr-only native radio). Tapping one focuses the radio ‚Üí `BottomSheet`'s panel `onFocus={handleFocusCapture}` runs `scrollIntoView({ block: "center" })` (rAF) on the focused control ‚Äî intended to lift a text input above the keyboard, but on a chip it **scrolls the result list up under the finger**, so the synthesized touch `click` lands on a result row. The rAF timing is why it's intermittent ("sometimes"); it only misfires when the chip wasn't already centered. **Fix (`catalog-item-picker.tsx`):** `onFocusCapture={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}` on the category chip-row `<div>` ‚Äî a capture-phase stop halts the focus event before it bubbles to the panel's `onFocus`, so the sheet doesn't re-center on a chip (the radio still focuses; selection/a11y intact). The search input is untouched ‚Üí still centers for the keyboard. **Not unit-testable** (the bug is touch + scrollIntoView layout timing, absent in jsdom ‚Äî no real scroll/touch); existing category-chip filter test still green; verified on-device by the operator. **NEXT = the rest of procurement's "few requests".**

## Spec 181 ‚Äî Supply Plan bulk PR, U1: procurement plans in PM's stead (2026-06-23)

Status: **U1 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest 217 files / 1448 (+3: SUPPLY_PLAN_ROLES role-set pins)** ¬∑ **db:test 138 files / 2565 / 0** (+1 file 189, +11 asserts) ¬∑ `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809000900`; **no db:types delta** (RLS + RPC-gate only, no signature/column change). The "bulk PR (planned purchases)" arc opens. Operator's canonical flow: **PM plans ‚Üí procurement compares prices ‚Üí PD approves ‚Üí procurement purchases**; for the moment procurement ALSO does the planning (PM's stead), PD approval stays required. **Locked decisions (AskUserQuestion + free-text):** (1) plan must be **PD-approved** before procurement generates PRs; generated PRs are **born `approved`** (inherit the plan's PD approval, no per-PR re-approval) ‚Üí ready for the existing price-compare/PO flow [U3]; (2) procurement does create/add/remove/submit + generate; **approve/reject stay PD/super**; (3) entry = **inline grid** [U2], no paste-import v1; (4) **accuracy-correctness catch** ‚Äî a plan-generated PR is PLANNED not reactive, so it links to its plan line and is **excluded from `unplanned_miss`** [U3], else generating PRs would corrupt the very metric spec 176 exists to produce. **U1 = procurement ACCESS (this unit):** new `SUPPLY_PLAN_ROLES = [...PM_ROLES, "procurement"]` (role-home; members coincide with WORKER_ROSTER_ROLES, meaning differs = "who plans supply", kept separate per role-doctrine). **Migration `20260809000900`** (RLS + RPC widening, **`alter policy` + `CREATE OR REPLACE`, bodies sourced from LIVE = spec-176 U1/U3, edited only at role-list + membership lines**): the `supply_plans`/`supply_plan_lines` SELECT policies gain a `current_user_role()='procurement'` arm beside `can_see_project` (procurement is cross-project ‚Üí its arm carries NO membership gate, spec 171/173 pattern); `create_supply_plan`/`add_supply_plan_line`/`remove_supply_plan_line`/`submit_supply_plan` role-list += procurement + membership skipped for it (`role <> 'procurement' AND NOT can_see_project`); `approve_supply_plan`/`reject_supply_plan` **untouched** (PD/super). **Page** (`/projects/[id]/supply-plan`): `requireRole(SUPPLY_PLAN_ROLES)`; the `SupplyPlanAccuracy` card is rendered ONLY for the planner tier (procurement skips it ‚Äî `supply_plan_accuracy` stays PM-gated + the measure is the PM's, so the page would 42501/empty otherwise); `canApprove` stays PD/super ‚Üí procurement sees add/submit, no approve. **pgTAP `189`:** cross-project procurement (non-member) create+read+add+read-line+remove+submit + status=submitted; **cannot approve (42501)**; a non-member PM stays denied (procurement arm didn't widen PM); anon still can't EXECUTE (CREATE OR REPLACE preserved the revoke ‚Äî no DROP+CREATE anon re-grant). **Clean first push ‚Äî no traps** (CREATE OR REPLACE preserved grants; pgTAP 176 had no procurement pins to break; `alter policy` kept the policy names so any name pins hold). **NEXT = U2** (bulk-add RPC + the inline grid editor), then U3 (generation engine + accuracy exclusion), U4 (convert mode UI).

## Spec 181 ‚Äî Supply Plan bulk PR, U2: bulk-add + the inline grid editor (2026-06-23)

Status: **U2 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest 217 files / 1450 (+2: supply-plan-manager grid 6‚Üí8)** ¬∑ **db:test 139 files / 2574 / 0** (+1 file 190) ¬∑ `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809001000`; db:types regenerated (app + worker, +`add_supply_plan_lines`). The "table / multiple items at once" the operator asked for. **DB ‚Äî `add_supply_plan_lines(p_plan_id uuid, p_lines jsonb)`** (mig `20260809001000`): loops `jsonb_array_elements`, validates + inserts every line with the SAME per-line checks as the single `add_supply_plan_line` (qty>0, active catalog item, WP-in-project), **ATOMIC** ‚Äî any bad line `raise`s ‚Üí the whole batch rolls back (no partial saves). Same gate as the single add (PM/super/director/**procurement**, membership skipped for procurement); plan must be draft/rejected; returns the count inserted. The unique (item, WP) index still bites (dup vs the batch or vs existing ‚Üí 23505, rolls back). **Action `bulkAddPlanLines`** (`supply-plan/actions.ts`): validates each row client-side (uuid + qty>0), get-or-creates the plan (`create_supply_plan`), then one `add_supply_plan_lines` call; maps 23505/42501/22023. **UI ‚Äî `SupplyPlanManager` rewritten** to an **inline grid** replacing the spec-176 one-at-a-time BottomSheet: N draft rows (item grouped-`<select>` + WP `<select>` (ý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áýπÇý∏Ñý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ = null) + qty number + note + ýπÄý∏≠ý∏≤ýπÅý∏ñý∏ßý∏≠ý∏≠ý∏Å), `Ôºã ýπÄý∏ûý∏¥ýπàý∏°ýπÅý∏ñý∏ß`, and `ý∏öý∏±ý∏ôý∏óý∏∂ý∏Å N ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£` that bulk-saves only the COMPLETE rows (item + qty>0; blank/partial trailing rows ignored ‚Äî forgiving fast entry) ‚Üí resets to one blank row + refresh. The saved-lines list + the lifecycle buttons (ý∏™ýπàý∏áý∏≠ý∏ôý∏∏ý∏°ý∏±ý∏ïý∏¥ / ý∏≠ý∏ôý∏∏ý∏°ý∏±ý∏ïý∏¥ / ý∏ïý∏µý∏Åý∏•ý∏±ý∏ö) are unchanged. **Used the existing grouped-`<select>` per row (NOT the spec-180 searchable picker)** to keep U2 decoupled (no cross-feature coupling, no thumbnail plumbing, no page change) ‚Äî **swapping the grid's item cell to the searchable CatalogItemPicker is a flagged follow-up** if the 71-item select feels heavy. **Test rewrite (`supply-plan-manager.test.tsx`):** the two add cases ‚Üí grid (disable-save-until-item+qty, bulk-save shape `{projectId, lines:[{catalogItemId, workPackageId, qty, note}]}`, whole-project null-WP, ÔºãýπÄý∏ûý∏¥ýπàý∏°ýπÅý∏ñý∏ß adds a row); remove/submit/approve/reject unchanged; read-only now asserts the grid is absent. **Draft-row remove uses aria-label `ýπÄý∏≠ý∏≤ýπÅý∏ñý∏ßý∏≠ý∏≠ý∏Å`** (NOT `ý∏•ý∏ö`) so it doesn't collide with the saved-line `ý∏•ý∏ö` button in `getByRole`. **Self-inflicted:** wrote `plan(10)` for 9 assertions in pgTAP 190 ‚Üí the custom runner does NOT fail on plan-count mismatch (only counts not-ok), so it slipped past the first db:test green; caught by an explicit assert-count grep, fixed to `plan(9)`. **Lesson re-logged: the pgTAP runner is lenient on plan mismatches ‚Äî grep `plan(N)` vs the real assertion count, don't trust a green db:test alone.** (Spawned a chip to reconcile a few PRE-EXISTING plan mismatches in other files, incl. one "planned 16 ran 15" = a silently-skipped assertion.) **NEXT = U3** (the generation engine: `purchase_requests.supply_plan_line_id` + `generate_purchase_requests_from_plan` born-approved + accuracy exclusion), then U4 (convert UI).

## Spec 181 ‚Äî Supply Plan bulk PR, U3: the generation engine (2026-06-23)

Status: **U3 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **db:test 140 files / 2584 / 0** (+1 file 191, +10 asserts) ¬∑ **vitest unchanged** (DB + action only, no UI) ¬∑ `pnpm build` (pending the U3+U4 ship ‚Äî built green at U2) ; db:push applied `20260809001100`; db:types regenerated (+`supply_plan_line_id` column + `generate_purchase_requests_from_plan` RPC). The "bulk PR" engine: an approved plan's selected lines ‚Üí purchase_requests in one call. **Schema:** `purchase_requests.supply_plan_line_id` (nullable FK ‚Üí supply_plan_lines) + a **PARTIAL unique index** `where supply_plan_line_id is not null` (a line converts at most once = idempotency; NULL ‚Äî the vast majority ‚Äî unconstrained). No authenticated INSERT grant needed for the new column ‚Äî the definer RPC owns the insert. **RPC `generate_purchase_requests_from_plan(p_plan_id, p_line_ids uuid[])`** (mig `20260809001100`): gate PM/super/director/**procurement** (membership skipped for procurement); plan must be **`approved`** (else 22023); loops the selected lines, **born-`approved` PRs** (status='approved', `approved_by = the plan's approver`, decided_at=now, reason_code NULL, source 'app', requested_by=auth.uid(), supply_plan_line_id=line) ‚Äî **inherits the plan's PD approval, no per-PR re-approval**; **skips already-linked lines** (idempotent, returns the count); **whole-project lines (null WP) ‚Üí 22023** (a PR is raised against a WP). **Born-approved is SAFE ‚Äî verified the triggers:** the only AFTER-INSERT trigger `notify_pr_created` fires `when (new.status = 'requested')` so a born-approved PR sends NO false "needs approval" notification; every other purchase_requests trigger (audit_decision/cancellation/appsheet, derive, set_updated_at, notify_status_change) is AFTER/BEFORE **UPDATE** ‚Üí none fire on insert. **`auth.uid()` inside SECURITY DEFINER returns the CALLING user** (procurement), not the owner ‚Äî JWT claims are session context, role is what DEFINER swaps (same as approve_supply_plan). **Accuracy fix (the design catch):** `supply_plan_accuracy` (CREATE OR REPLACE, grants preserved) gains `and r.supply_plan_line_id is null` in the prs CTE ‚Äî **plan-generated PRs (reason NULL but PLANNED) are excluded from the reactive/untagged counts**, else every planned order would read as a planning miss. **Action `generatePlanPurchaseRequests`** relays the RPC (maps 42501/22023; revalidates /supply-plan + /requests). pgTAP `191`: procurement generates 2 ‚Üí born-approved + approved_by=PD; idempotent re-gen ‚Üí 0; null-WP ‚Üí 22023; non-approved plan ‚Üí 22023; **accuracy: the 2 generated PRs excluded from untagged (0), the 1 reactive PR still counts as unplanned_miss (1)**. Generated PRs flow into the existing create_purchase_order (operates on approved PRs) = procurement's price-compare/PO step. **NEXT = U4** ‚Äî the convert-mode UI (checkboxes on an approved plan's lines + ý∏™ý∏£ýπâý∏≤ý∏áý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠, per-row PR-ýπÅý∏•ýπâý∏ß badge), per the mockup.

## Spec 181 ‚Äî Supply Plan bulk PR, U4: the convert-mode UI (ARC COMPLETE) (2026-06-23)

Status: **U4 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23. SPEC 181 ARC COMPLETE (U1‚ÄìU4).** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest 217 files / 1453 (+3: SupplyPlanManager convert mode)** ¬∑ `pnpm build` green. **No DB change** (reuses the U3 `generate_purchase_requests_from_plan` RPC + a page-side converted-set read) ‚Üí no migration, no db:test delta. The user-facing payoff: on an **approved** plan, select lines and fire the PRs. **`SupplyPlanManager` convert mode** (`planStatus === 'approved'`): the saved-lines list gains a leading **checkbox** for each convertible line (WP-bound + not yet converted), a trailing **`ý∏™ý∏£ýπâý∏≤ý∏á PR ýπÅý∏•ýπâý∏ß`** badge for already-converted lines, and a **`ý∏ïýπâý∏≠ý∏áý∏£ý∏∞ý∏öý∏∏ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ôý∏Åýπàý∏≠ý∏ô`** note for whole-project (null-WP) lines; below, a **`ý∏™ý∏£ýπâý∏≤ý∏áý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ý∏àý∏≤ý∏ÅýπÅý∏úý∏ô`** bar with **ýπÄý∏•ý∏∑ý∏≠ý∏Åý∏óý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏´ý∏°ý∏î** + **`ý∏™ý∏£ýπâý∏≤ý∏áý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ (N)`** ‚Üí `generatePlanPurchaseRequests({projectId, planId, lineIds})` ‚Üí clears the selection + a `ý∏™ý∏£ýπâý∏≤ý∏áý∏Ñý∏≥ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ýπÅý∏•ýπâý∏ß N ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£` status + refresh. Everyone on the page can generate (the page gate = SUPPLY_PLAN_ROLES = exactly the RPC's generate gate). **Page** (`/projects/[id]/supply-plan`): `PlanLine` gains `converted: boolean` ‚Äî after loading the lines it reads `purchase_requests.supply_plan_line_id IN (lineIds)` (procurement/PM via the privileged PR SELECT) ‚Üí marks each line converted (idempotent badge + excluded from selection). **Tests:** the convert describe (badge on a converted line, no checkbox for converted/whole-project, select ‚Üí `generatePlanPurchaseRequests` shape `{projectId, planId, lineIds:["lc"]}`, select-all, no convert UI on a draft plan). **Browser pass = operator on-device.** **THE BULK-PR ARC IS DONE end-to-end:** procurement (PM's stead) plans in the inline grid (U2) ‚Üí PD approves ‚Üí procurement checks the approved lines and fires born-approved PRs (U3 engine, U4 UI) ‚Üí they flow into the existing price-compare/PO step; plan-linked PRs stay out of the PM-accuracy metric. **Flagged follow-ups:** swap the grid's grouped-`<select>` item cell to the spec-180 searchable picker; paste-from-spreadsheet import; whole-project (null-WP) line conversion; the convert UI as a proper desktop table (mockup) vs the current responsive list.

## Spec 181 ‚Äî Supply Plan bulk PR, U5: the missing nav door for procurement (2026-06-23)

Status: **U5 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ `pnpm build` green. **No DB change, single-file nav fix.** Operator: "procurement said they cannot find the menu." Root cause = the spec-171-U4 "access without a door" lesson, again: U1 admitted procurement to the `/projects/[id]/supply-plan` page + RPCs, but the **ýπÅý∏úý∏ôý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏´ý∏≤ chip on the project page was inside `{isManagerRole(ctx.role)}`** (PM/super/director) ‚Äî and **procurement is NOT a manager role**, so it never saw the link. The whole path otherwise already existed: procurement hub ‚Üí `ýπÇý∏Ñý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£` (`/projects`, PROJECT_VIEW_ROLES) ‚Üí a project page (PROJECT_VIEW_ROLES, spec 173) ‚Üí ‚Ä¶ but the supply-plan chip was the dead end. **Fix (`projects/[projectId]/page.tsx`):** `const canPlanSupply = SUPPLY_PLAN_ROLES.includes(ctx.role)` (mirrors `canOpenSchedule`) + the ýπÅý∏úý∏ôý∏àý∏±ý∏îý∏´ý∏≤ `<Link>` MOVED OUT of the manager-only block into its own `{canPlanSupply ? ‚Ä¶ : null}` door (placed right after the schedule chip). The reports/settings chips stay manager-only. No project-page test pins these chips (none exists), so no test delta. **LESSON re-logged (3rd time ‚Äî spec 171 U4, this): admitting a role to a PAGE + its RPCs is HALF the job ‚Äî also surface the NAV chip/link that reaches it, and check whether it's bundled inside an `isManagerRole`/PM-only block that excludes the new role.** **SPEC 181 NOW REACHABLE end-to-end for procurement.**

## Spec 182 ‚Äî Procurement price comparison, U1: quote capture + compare table (2026-06-23)

Status: **U1 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest 218 files / 1457 (+4: price-comparison)** ¬∑ **db:test 141 files / 2597 / 0** (+1 file 192, +13 asserts) ¬∑ `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809001200`; db:types regenerated (+`purchase_quotes` table + 2 RPCs). Procurement's price comparison was 100% offline (pick one supplier, type the amount into the PO; losing quotes vanish). U1 = structured quote capture + a ranked compare table. **DB ‚Äî `purchase_quotes`** (mig `20260809001200`): PR + supplier + `unit_price numeric(12,2) >= 0` + note; **UNIQUE (purchase_request_id, supplier_id)** (one quote per supplier; re-quote = remove+re-add). **Money posture (unit_price is sensitive ‚Äî more than PO existence):** RLS SELECT to **back-office only (PM/procurement/super_admin/project_director), NOT site_admin**; no write policy. RPCs `add_purchase_quote` (back-office gate; PR must be **`approved`** = the pre-PO sourcing window; dup supplier ‚Üí 23505; unknown PR/supplier or non-approved ‚Üí 22023) + `remove_purchase_quote`. **THE project_director RIDE-ALONG TRAP ‚Äî caught by db:test file 91 (3rd time this session): a policy/RPC naming `project_manager` MUST also name `project_director`.** My first cut listed `('project_manager','procurement','super_admin')` ‚Üí file 91 RED (2 fails). Fixed forward: added `project_director` to the policy + both RPCs, **re-applied to live via `supabase db query --linked --file`** (ALTER POLICY + CREATE OR REPLACE the 2 funcs) since the migration was already pushed, AND edited the migration file so a fresh setup is correct. **Action** `addPurchaseQuote`/`removePurchaseQuote` (maps 23505/42501/22023; trap: the RPC `p_note` param is typed `string` non-null ‚Üí pass `?? ""` not `?? null`). **UI ‚Äî `PriceComparison`** (`price-comparison.tsx`): ranks quotes cheapest-first, cheapest row highlighted + `ý∏ñý∏πý∏Åý∏™ý∏∏ý∏î`, total = unit_price √ó qty, `+N%` over the cheapest; an add form (supplier `<select>` of NOT-yet-quoted suppliers + ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏Ñý∏≤ý∏ïýπàý∏≠ý∏´ý∏ôýπàý∏ßý∏¢) + per-row remove. **Wired into `/requests/[requestId]`** in the `isBackOffice && status==='approved'` branch, ABOVE `CreatePoFromRequestButton` (compare ‚Üí then order); the page fetches the PR's quotes (supplier-name embed, ordered by price) only for that branch. **Tests:** pgTAP 192 (back-office add/read/remove, dup‚Üí23505, non-approved‚Üí22023, **site_admin can neither add (42501) nor READ (RLS count 0)**); `price-comparison.test.tsx` (rank+total+% , add offers only un-quoted suppliers, remove, empty state). **NEXT = U2** (pick a quote ‚Üí prefill `create_purchase_order` with that supplier + amount), then U3 (price-history benchmark via the spec-179 catalog link), U4 (per-quote doc attachment).

## Spec 182 ‚Äî Procurement price comparison, U2: pick winner ‚Üí PO (2026-06-23)

Status: **U2 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest 218 files / 1459 (+2: pick‚ÜíPO)** ¬∑ `pnpm build` green. **No DB change** (reuses `create_purchase_order` + the U1 quotes) ‚Üí no migration, no db:test delta. The winning quote now flows into the PO. **`CreatePurchaseOrderSheet`** gained `defaultSupplierId` + `defaultAmounts` props (seeded into the initial `supplierId`/`amounts` state; the caller **remounts via `key`** on a new pick ‚Äî avoids the setState-in-effect React-Compiler lint). **`PriceComparison`** now owns the create-PO action: each quote row gets a **pick radio** (defaults to the cheapest; the picked row gets `border-action bg-action-soft`), and a **`ý∏™ý∏£ýπâý∏≤ý∏áýπÉý∏öý∏™ý∏±ýπàý∏áý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ý∏àý∏≤ý∏Å {supplier}`** button opens the sheet **prefilled** with the picked supplier + `defaultAmounts = { [line.id]: net total }` (net = unit_price √ó qty; the sheet's default VAT mode is already `exclusive` = net+7%, matching Thai net quotes). With no quotes the button opens the sheet blank (same as before). **`/requests/[requestId]`** now renders `PriceComparison` with the PR-as-`line` and **drops the standalone `CreatePoFromRequestButton`** (PriceComparison owns it) ‚Äî the approved-state action is unified. **exactOptionalPropertyTypes trap:** passing `defaultSupplierId={picked?.supplierId}` (string|undefined) into a `?: string` prop ‚Üí TS2375; fixed by typing the prop `?: string | undefined`. **Test:** the PO sheet is mocked to a stub exposing the prefill props; default-pick = cheapest (s1, ý∏ø4,600), re-pick the dearer quote ‚Üí that supplier + total (s2, ý∏ø4,900). **NEXT = U3** (price-history benchmark via the spec-179 catalog link), then U4 (per-quote doc attachment).

## Spec 182 ‚Äî Procurement price comparison, U3: last-paid benchmark (2026-06-23)

Status: **U3 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest 218 files / 1461 (+2: price-history benchmark)** ¬∑ **db:test 142 files / 2607 / 0** (+1 file 193, +10 asserts) ¬∑ `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809001300`; db:types regenerated (+`item_price_history` RPC). The "what did we pay last time" reference above the quote table. **DB ‚Äî `item_price_history(p_catalog_item_id uuid)`** (mig `20260809001300`): SECURITY DEFINER table-returning read ‚Üí recent PURCHASED `purchase_requests` for a catalog item (the spec-179 link) ‚Üí `supplier_name` (coalesce suppliers.name, the text snapshot), **NET unit price** (`round(amount/(1+vat_rate/100)/nullif(qty,0), 2)` ‚Äî amount is the line GROSS per spec 119, so net = apples-to-apples with the net quotes), qty, purchased_at; `where amount is not null and quantity > 0`, **order by `coalesce(purchased_at, requested_at) desc` limit 5**. **Money posture (ADR 0038, same as purchase_quotes):** back-office role gate (PM/procurement/super/**project_director** ‚Äî the file-91 ride-along, named correctly first try this time) ‚Üí site_admin + anon get nothing; DEFINER also lets the cross-project price reference see purchases beyond the caller's membership (a price benchmark is org-wide). **UI ‚Äî `PriceComparison`** gains an optional `history: ItemPriceHistory[]` prop (default `[]` ‚Üí U1/U2 tests untouched); when non-empty it renders **`ýπÄý∏Ñý∏¢ý∏ãý∏∑ýπâý∏≠ý∏•ýπàý∏≤ý∏™ý∏∏ý∏î ý∏øX/{unit} ý∏àý∏≤ý∏Å Y ¬∑ N ý∏Ñý∏£ý∏±ýπâý∏á`** above the ýπÄý∏õý∏£ý∏µý∏¢ý∏öýπÄý∏óý∏µý∏¢ý∏öý∏£ý∏≤ý∏Ñý∏≤ heading (X/Y from `history[0]` = newest; N = `history.length`). **Page** (`/requests/[requestId]`): the select gains `catalog_item_id`; the history is fetched (one `rpc("item_price_history")`) only in the `isBackOffice && status==='approved' && catalog_item_id` case (off-catalog PRs have no axis ‚Üí no line) and passed to `PriceComparison`. **Tests:** pgTAP 193 (shape + SECURITY DEFINER + anon-no-exec; **site_admin ‚Üí 42501**; count excludes the amount-null PR + the other-item PR; net with VAT 0 = 98, with VAT 7% = 100, a null-supplier_id row coalesces to the text snapshot = 100; no null nets; `results_eq` on `purchased_at` proves newest-first); component test (benchmark line shows newest price/supplier/`N ý∏Ñý∏£ý∏±ýπâý∏á`; no line when history empty). **NET decision (no operator ask needed):** quotes are NET so last-paid is computed NET for a fair compare; the line uses the actual `unit` (matching the quote rows) not the literal "ý∏´ý∏ôýπàý∏ßý∏¢". **NEXT = U4** (per-quote doc attachment: `quote` attachment purpose + `quote_id` FK).

## Spec 182 ‚Äî Procurement price comparison, U4: per-quote source document (ARC COMPLETE) (2026-06-23)

Status: **U4 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23. SPEC 182 ARC COMPLETE (U1‚ÄìU4).** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest 218 files / 1463 (+2: quote-doc render)** ¬∑ **db:test 143 files / 2619 / 0** (+1 file 194, +12 asserts; +pin updates in file 20) ¬∑ `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809001400`+`20260809001500`; db:types regenerated (+`quote_id` column on attachments + the view). Each supplier quote (U1) now carries its source quotation doc (the üìé). **DB ‚Äî mig `‚Ä¶001400`** adds the `quote` value to `purchase_request_attachment_purpose` (own migration ‚Äî ADD VALUE can't be referenced same-txn, the spec-66 invoice precedent). **mig `‚Ä¶001500`:** `purchase_request_attachments.quote_id` (nullable FK ‚Üí `purchase_quotes`, **ON DELETE CASCADE** ‚Äî matches the sibling PR FK; both dormant outside the admin hard-delete) + `pra_quote_id_shape` CHECK (quote_id only on quote rows) + column-scoped `grant insert(quote_id)` (the spec-179 new-column lesson); the INSERT policy gains a **quote arm** (back-office only ‚Äî NOT site_admin; PR `approved`; quote on THIS PR); a **RESTRICTIVE select** gates quote-purpose rows to back-office (money ‚Äî a quotation shows prices); the storage upload policy widened (+procurement/+director, +`approved` status arm); the `_current` view appends `quote_id`. **Action `addQuoteAttachment`** mirrors `addInvoiceAttachment` (idempotent 23505 replay; purpose='quote'+quote_id; server rebuilds the path). **UI:** new `QuoteDocAttach` (per-row uploader, the InvoiceUploader flow) ‚Äî a doc-less quote row shows the attach control, a doc'd row shows a `ýπÄý∏≠ý∏Åý∏™ý∏≤ý∏£` link; **remove is disabled on a doc'd quote** (append-only + FK ‚Üí not hard-removable; kept for audit, aligned with "losing quotes are kept"). The page fetches quote docs (`purpose='quote'` from `_current`, signed via the pr-attachments bucket) ‚Üí `quoteId ‚Üí url` map passed to `PriceComparison`. **Per-row attach (not mid-add)** chosen for robustness + it matches the spec doc's "üìé per quote row"; the task's "in the add form" phrasing is an impl hint. **TRAPS caught by db:test (all forward-fixed; the corrected INSERT policy re-applied to live via `supabase db query --linked --file` since `‚Ä¶001500` was already pushed):** (1) **name-capture** ‚Äî `q.purchase_request_id = purchase_request_id` bound the unqualified RHS to `q` (purchase_quotes has that column!) ‚Üí always true ‚Üí a cross-PR quote could be linked; qualified to `purchase_request_attachments.purchase_request_id` (the hazard every attachments-policy header warns about). (2) **bare eval-once** ‚Äî wrote `current_user_role()`/`auth.uid()` bare ‚Üí pgTAP file 40 RED; wrapped as `(select ‚Ä¶)` (pg_get_expr drops the `public.` so `(select public.current_user_role())` still satisfies the lint ‚Äî file 40 stayed the model). (3) **two file-20 pins** my change legitimately invalidates ‚Äî the purpose-enum label set (+quote) + `policies_are` (the 4th, restrictive, policy); updated both. (4) **spec-143 membership** ‚Äî the pgTAP fixture's site_admin needed a `project_members` row to read the parent PR (procurement keeps its cross-project read). **Verified:** procurement attaches on an approved PR + reads; site_admin can neither attach (42501) nor read the quote doc (RESTRICTIVE, incl. via the view) but still sees the parent's reference row; a cross-PR quote_id is rejected. **THE PRICE-COMPARISON ARC IS DONE end-to-end:** capture N supplier quotes (U1) ‚Üí rank + pick ‚Üí prefill the PO (U2) ‚Üí last-paid benchmark from history (U3) ‚Üí each quote carries its source doc (U4). **Flagged follow-ups:** removing/replacing an attached quote doc (the doc-removal path is out of U4 scope ‚Äî a doc'd quote is currently immutable); a desktop table layout; quote expiry/validity dates.

## Spec 178 ‚Äî Store margin layer, U3: Store P&L read (2026-06-23)

Status: **U3 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **db:test 144 files / 2633 / 0** (+1 file 195, +14 asserts) ¬∑ `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809001600`; db:types regenerated (+`store_pnl`, app + worker). **Renumbered around the concurrent spec-179/181/182 session** (it took migs `‚Ä¶000800`‚Äì`‚Ä¶001500` + pgTAP 188‚Äì194, all merged to main while I was paused; U1+U2 stayed ancestors of HEAD `b20f3554`) ‚Üí my mig = `‚Ä¶001600`, pgTAP = 195. The margin layer becomes observable: `store_pnl(p_project_id)` returns **per-item** rows ‚Äî `qty_issued`, `cost_total` (Œ£ total_cost), `sell_total` (Œ£ **`coalesce(total_sell, total_cost)`** so unpriced/legacy issues count at cost), `margin` (sell‚àícost), `shrinkage_value` (Œ£ `stock_counts.variance_value`). **Reversed issues excluded** (anti-join `stock_reversals` on `issue_id` ‚Äî a reversed ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å never charged the WP). FULL OUTER JOIN of per-item issue aggregates + per-item count aggregates ‚Üí an item that was only counted (pure shrinkage) still appears. **Money gate super_admin / project_director** (mirrors `wp_profit` ‚Äî NOT procurement, NOT site_admin/PM); SECURITY DEFINER, called on the user session (admin client has no JWT ‚Üí 42501). Unknown project ‚Üí 22023. pgTAP 195 (14): structure + anon-deny + per-item cost/sell/margin/shrinkage + unpriced-at-cost + reversed-excluded + director-allowed + PM/procurement-denied + unknown-project. Clean first push. NEXT = U4 flip `wp_profit` (fold Œ£ total_sell of non-reversed issues into the materials line).

## Spec 178 ‚Äî Store margin layer, U4: flip wp_profit (the keystone) (2026-06-23)

Status: **U4 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest 1463** ¬∑ **db:test 145 files / 2637 / 0** (+1 file 196, +4 asserts) ¬∑ `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809001700`; **db:types ZERO drift** (same return signature). The transfer-pricing payoff lands: `wp_profit`'s `materials_cost` now = the GL acct-1400 purchase cost (unchanged) **PLUS** the per-WP `Œ£ coalesce(total_sell, total_cost)` of **non-reversed** `stock_issues` ‚Äî the store charges the WP its SELL price for material drawn from the store. **Disjoint sources** (store issues are never posted to the GL) ‚Üí additive, no double-count; a WP pays sell price for store stock AND purchase price for direct-to-WP buys. Reversed issues excluded (anti-join `stock_reversals` on `issue_id`). **Body sourced from the LIVE `pg_get_functiondef`** (confirmed unchanged by the concurrent session), not an old migration ‚Äî the recurring overload/dropped-gate trap; **SAME return signature ‚Üí CREATE OR REPLACE (body-only)**, so grants + the pgTAP **102** structure pins + the 90/91 gate invariants (no PM ref) all hold, and `settle_project` banks the new profit automatically (it reads `wp_profit.profit`). pgTAP 196 (4): WP-with-store-issues materials = store-sell 520 (priced 500 + unpriced-at-cost 20; reversed 5000 excluded) ¬∑ profit = budget 1000 ‚àí 520 ¬∑ a WP with no store draws unchanged (per-WP, no leak) ¬∑ labor_sell still 0 (only the materials line folds). **102/104/105 stayed green** (their fixtures have no `stock_issues` on the tested WPs ‚Üí the new term = 0). Clean first push. **wp_profit now reflects the store as a transfer-pricing BU. The cost-first dial is unlocked end-to-end (engine).** NEXT = U5 sell-rate setter UI (super-only) ¬∑ U6 store-margin UI on /store ¬∑ then Stream B mop-up.

## Spec 178 ‚Äî Store margin layer, U5: the sell-rate setter UI (super-only) (2026-06-23)

Status: **U5 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest 219 files / 1466 (+3: set-sell-rate)** ¬∑ `pnpm build` green. **Code-only ‚Äî no DB / migration / db:test delta** (reuses the U1 `set_item_sell_rate` RPC). The first margin-layer UI: the operator can finally set the per-item sell rate. **Home = `/catalog`** (the item registry ‚Äî sell rate is a global per-item attribute). **Super_admin ONLY, money-locked end to end:** the rate is read on the page **via the admin client** and **only when `ctx.role === "super_admin"`** (the nova money pattern ‚Äî `item_sell_rates` is zero authenticated grant, so the rate never reaches the client for procurement/PM/director); written via the new **`setItemSellRate`** action (`requireRole(["super_admin"])` + `set_item_sell_rate` on the RLS server client so the definer super gate resolves; maps 42501/22023). New **`SetSellRate`** client control (per row, gated on a `canSetSellRate` prop) ‚Äî shows `ý∏ø{rate}` when set or `ý∏ïý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏£ý∏≤ý∏Ñý∏≤ý∏Çý∏≤ý∏¢` when unset ‚Üí a BottomSheet number input ‚Üí `setItemSellRate` ‚Üí refresh. `CatalogList` += `canSetSellRate` prop + `sellRate?` on `CatalogItem` (omitted for non-super via conditional spread ‚Äî the rate doesn't leave the server). Labels `ITEM_SELL_RATE_LABEL`/`SET_ITEM_SELL_RATE_LABEL` in labels.ts. **2 traps:** (1) `getByLabelText(/ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏Ñý∏≤ý∏Çý∏≤ý∏¢/)` matched BOTH the input AND the BottomSheet dialog (its `aria-labelledby` ‚Üí title `ý∏ïý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏£ý∏≤ý∏Ñý∏≤ý∏Çý∏≤ý∏¢` contains `ý∏£ý∏≤ý∏Ñý∏≤ý∏Çý∏≤ý∏¢`) ‚Üí query the number input by `getByRole("spinbutton")`. (2) `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` rejected the explicit `undefined` on optional `sellRate` ‚Üí omit the key via conditional spread (the spec-177-U2 lesson). Browser pass = operator on-device (auth-gated). NEXT = U6 store-margin UI on /store ¬∑ then Stream B mop-up.

## Spec 178 ‚Äî Store margin layer, U6: the Store P&L view on /store (ARC COMPLETE) (2026-06-23)

Status: **U6 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23. SPEC 178 STREAM A (margin layer) ARC COMPLETE (U1‚ÄìU6).** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest 220 files / 1468 (+2: store-pnl-view)** ¬∑ `pnpm build` green. **Code-only ‚Äî no DB / migration / db:test delta** (reads the U3 `store_pnl` RPC). The margin layer is now visible: /store shows a **ý∏Åý∏≥ýπÑý∏£-ý∏Çý∏≤ý∏îý∏óý∏∏ý∏ôý∏™ýπÇý∏ïý∏£ýπå** section ‚Äî per item (ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å qty ¬∑ ý∏ïýπâý∏ôý∏óý∏∏ý∏ô ¬∑ ý∏¢ý∏≠ý∏îý∏Çý∏≤ý∏¢ ¬∑ margin, + ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏áý∏Çý∏≤ý∏î-ýπÄý∏Åý∏¥ý∏ô when a count varies) and the **project net = Œ£ margin + Œ£ shrinkage** (ý∏Åý∏≥ýπÑý∏£ý∏™ý∏∏ý∏óý∏òý∏¥). **super_admin / project_director ONLY** (`canSeePnl`, mirrors the `store_pnl` RPC gate ‚Äî procurement/PM/site_admin never see margins); rendered only when a project is selected. **Page** calls `store_pnl(project)` on the user session (definer gate resolves via JWT) and resolves item names separately (covers since-deactivated items). New presentational **`StorePnlView`** (pure server component, no `'use client'`) + `STORE_PNL_LABEL`. Browser pass = operator on-device (auth-gated). **STREAM A DONE: U1 sell-rate data ‚Üí U2 issue snapshots sell ‚Üí U3 store_pnl read ‚Üí U4 wp_profit fold ‚Üí U5 sell-rate setter UI ‚Üí U6 store P&L view. The store is a transfer-pricing BU end to end (engine + screens).** NEXT = **Stream B** cost-side mop-up (deferred items): WP-detail reversal controls, site_admin count surface, count-history view, /store-manager receiver picker, manager confirm-on-behalf, GL posting of stock movements.

## Spec 178 ‚Äî Stream B (cost-side mop-up), B1: WP-detail reversal controls (2026-06-23)

Status: **B1 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest 220 files / 1470 (+2: wp-issue-stock reversal)** ¬∑ `pnpm build` green. **Code-only ‚Äî no DB** (reuses the spec-177-U11 `reverse_stock_issue` RPC + the U12 `reverseStockIssue` action). Closes a real gap: a site_admin who drew stock from the WP detail (`WpIssueStock`) could not undo it there ‚Äî only managers could, and only from /store. Now each recent ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å in `WpIssueStock` carries a `ý∏Åý∏•ý∏±ý∏öý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£` control (`ConfirmActionButton` ‚Üí `reverseStockIssue({ issueId })`, mirrors /store U12 exactly: confirm ‚Üí action ‚Üí refresh; ý∏Çý∏≠ý∏áý∏àý∏∞ý∏ñý∏πý∏Åý∏Ñý∏∑ý∏ôýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ý∏™ýπÇý∏ïý∏£ýπå). **No gate prop** ‚Äî `WpIssueStock` only renders under the WP-detail `!readOnly` gate (= SITE_STAFF = the `reverse_stock_issue` gate), so every issue shown is reversible by that field staffer. Test extends `wp-issue-stock.test.tsx` (+`reverseStockIssue` mock; the control shows on a recent issue; confirm ‚Üí reverseStockIssue with the issue id). **Flagged (unchanged):** the custody implication of reversing a CONFIRMED issue (a receiver already attested) is still an open follow-up ‚Äî same as /store. NEXT Stream B = site_admin count surface ¬∑ count-history ¬∑ /store-manager receiver picker ¬∑ manager confirm-on-behalf ¬∑ GL posting.

## Spec 178 ‚Äî Stream B, B4: /store-manager receiver picker (2026-06-23)

Status: **B4 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest 220 files / 1471 (+1: store-manager receiver)** ¬∑ `pnpm build` green. **Code-only ‚Äî no DB** (reuses `issue_stock`'s `p_receiver_worker_id`, U6/U7). Closes the deferred custody gap on the /store surface: a manager issuing from /store could not name a receiver, so those ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å were never portal-confirmable (always ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏£ý∏±ý∏ö). Now the /store ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å sheet has an optional **ý∏úý∏πýπâý∏£ý∏±ý∏ö (ý∏ñýπâý∏≤ý∏°ý∏µ)** select of the project's active workers ‚Üí `issueStock` passes `receiverWorkerId` (omit-key conditional spread when unselected). `StoreManager` += `workers` prop + `issueReceiver` state; the **page fetches the project's active workers** (`workers` by `project_id`+`active`, RLS-scoped ‚Äî empty for a viewer who can't read them, harmless since only the manager tier opens the sheet). Mirrors `WpIssueStock` U7 exactly. The existing no-receiver issue test still passes (backward compatible). Remaining Stream B = site_admin count surface ¬∑ count-history ¬∑ manager confirm-on-behalf ¬∑ GL posting.

## Spec 178 ‚Äî Stream B, B3: count-history view (ý∏õý∏£ý∏∞ý∏ßý∏±ý∏ïý∏¥ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ý∏ôý∏±ý∏ö) (2026-06-23)

Status: **B3 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest 220 files / 1473 (+2: store-manager count history)** ¬∑ `pnpm build` green. **Code-only ‚Äî no DB** (reads existing `stock_counts`). /store gains a read-only **ý∏õý∏£ý∏∞ý∏ßý∏±ý∏ïý∏¥ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£ý∏ôý∏±ý∏ö** audit list (last 10 counts for the project): per count the item, ý∏ôý∏±ý∏öýπÑý∏îýπâ (counted qty), and the colored ý∏™ýπàý∏ßý∏ôý∏ïýπàý∏≤ý∏á (variance ¬±N ‚Äî danger when short, action when over, muted when matched), mirroring the U10 live-preview styling. The valued shrinkage already shows in the U6 P&L; this is the qty audit trail. `StoreManager` += `CountRow` type + `counts` prop + the section (after ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Åý∏•ýπàý∏≤ý∏™ý∏∏ý∏î); the page fetches recent `stock_counts` (id, counted_qty, variance, unit, catalog_items) ‚Äî readable by any project viewer (the table's existing grant + RLS). Test extends store-manager.test (counts fixture + renderManager `counts`; the list renders with ý∏™ýπàý∏ßý∏ôý∏ïýπàý∏≤ý∏á -2; hidden when no counts). Remaining Stream B = **site_admin count surface** (needs a project-scoped page ‚Äî site_admin has no /store access) ¬∑ **manager confirm-on-behalf** (needs a confirm_stock_issue widening + a gate decision ‚Üí AskUserQuestion) ¬∑ **GL posting of stock movements** (Dr Inv/Cr AP into the spec-149 outbox ‚Äî a DB unit).

## Spec 178 ‚Äî Stream B, B2: site_admin stock-count surface (2026-06-23)

Status: **B2 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** lint ¬∑ typecheck ¬∑ **vitest 221 files / 1478 (+4: store-count-manager)** ¬∑ `pnpm build` green. **Code-only ‚Äî no DB** (reuses `record_stock_count`, SITE_STAFF-gated). Closes the self-governance gap: site_admin physically keeps the store but couldn't reach /store (BACK_OFFICE-gated), so had no UI to reconcile it. New **`/stock-count`** route (gated `SITE_STAFF_ROLES` = site_admin + PM tier; a settings drill-down: DetailHeader back ‚Üí /settings, no HubNav, like /store) ‚Äî project picker (RLS-scoped) ‚Üí on-hand list ‚Üí per-row **ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏àý∏ôý∏±ý∏ö** ‚Üí counted-qty sheet with the **live variance preview** (ý∏™ýπàý∏ßý∏ôý∏ïýπàý∏≤ý∏á ¬±N ý∏Çý∏≤ý∏î/ýπÄý∏Åý∏¥ý∏ô/ý∏ïý∏£ý∏áý∏Åý∏±ý∏ô) ‚Üí `recordStockCount`. New focused **`StoreCountManager`** (count-ONLY ‚Äî no ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤/ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å/reversal, those stay back-office on /store) + `STOCK_COUNT_LABEL`. **Entry:** a ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏àý∏ôý∏±ý∏öý∏™ý∏ïýπäý∏≠ý∏Å door in the site_admin **ý∏´ý∏ôýπâý∏≤ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô** block of /settings (beside ý∏≠ý∏∏ý∏õý∏Åý∏£ý∏ìýπå); `SETTINGS_TAB.match += "/stock-count"` so the ý∏ïý∏±ýπâý∏áý∏Ñýπàý∏≤ tab stays lit (per-role [label,href] pins untouched ‚Äî safe). **Guards:** added `stock-count` to the nav-back-affordance `STATIC_DETAIL` list (it uses the back-chip DetailHeader); the new component lives in the already-allowed `features/store` domain. Test `store-count-manager.test.tsx` (4): picker ‚Üí push `/stock-count?project=`; on-hand + ý∏ïý∏£ý∏ßý∏àý∏ôý∏±ý∏ö; empty store; count ‚Üí recordStockCount + refresh with the live variance. **Remaining Stream B = manager confirm-on-behalf (needs a gate AskUserQuestion) ¬∑ GL posting (DB unit).**

## Spec 178 ‚Äî Stream B, B5a: confirm-on-behalf engine (2026-06-23)

Status: **B5a SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** typecheck ¬∑ lint ¬∑ **db:test 146 files / 2651 / 0** (+1 file 197, +14 asserts) ¬∑ vitest ¬∑ `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809001800`; db:types regenerated (+received_on_behalf/received_by + the RPC). The custody fallback for login-less workers: a named receiver with no portal login can't self-attest, so their ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å sits ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏£ý∏±ý∏ö forever. **Operator gate (AskUserQuestion): PM tier ONLY (project_manager/super_admin/project_director) AND never the issuer** (separation of duties ‚Äî no rubber-stamping your own handoff). `stock_issues` += `received_on_behalf boolean default false` + `received_by uuid` (the confirming manager) ‚Äî a worker self-confirm (`confirm_stock_issue`, untouched) leaves both at defaults, so the two paths are always distinguishable. New `confirm_stock_issue_on_behalf(p_issue_id)` definer: PM-tier gate (42501) ‚Üí unknown issue (22023) ‚Üí can_see_project (42501) ‚Üí receiver-named (22023) ‚Üí not-already-received (22023) ‚Üí not-the-issuer (`auth.uid()=issued_by` ‚Üí 42501) ‚Üí set received_at + received_on_behalf=true + received_by=auth.uid(). pgTAP 197 (14): structure + anon-deny + site_admin-denied + non-member-denied + no-receiver + already-received + unknown + issuer-self-denied + happy (received_at/on_behalf/by stamped). `confirm_stock_issue` (worker-self, pgTAP 183) unchanged ‚Üí stays green (additive columns). **NEXT = B5b the UI** (a ý∏¢ý∏∑ý∏ôý∏¢ý∏±ý∏ôý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÅý∏óý∏ô control on a manager's pending-ý∏£ý∏≠ý∏£ý∏±ý∏ö issues) ¬∑ then B6 GL posting.

## Spec 178 ‚Äî Stream B, B5b: confirm-on-behalf UI (2026-06-23)

Status: **B5b SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** typecheck ¬∑ lint ¬∑ **vitest 221 files / 1482 (+4: store-manager confirm-on-behalf)** ¬∑ `pnpm build` green. **Code-only ‚Äî no DB** (calls the B5a RPC). The /store ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Åý∏•ýπàý∏≤ý∏™ý∏∏ý∏î list gains a **ý∏¢ý∏∑ý∏ôý∏¢ý∏±ý∏ôý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÅý∏óý∏ô** control (`ConfirmActionButton` ‚Üí `confirmStockIssueOnBehalf`) on each **pending + named** issue (`canIssue && receiverWorkerId && !receivedAt`) ‚Äî a manager attests receipt for a login-less receiver. The RPC enforces not-the-issuer (the button shows regardless; a self-confirm maps to a clean 42501 message "ý∏úý∏πýπâýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Åý∏¢ý∏∑ý∏ôý∏¢ý∏±ý∏ôýπÄý∏≠ý∏áýπÑý∏°ýπàýπÑý∏îýπâ"). New `confirmStockIssueOnBehalf` action (maps 42501/22023, revalidates /store). Shown beside ý∏Åý∏•ý∏±ý∏öý∏£ý∏≤ý∏¢ý∏Åý∏≤ý∏£; hidden once received or for non-managers. **Surfaced on /store only** (where the viewer is the manager tier); the WP-detail confirm-on-behalf is deferred (site_admin there isn't PM-tier ‚Üí would be a dead button). **SPEC 178 COMPLETE except B6 (GL posting).** NEXT = B6 GL posting of stock movements (Dr Inv/Cr AP ‚Üí spec-149 outbox, a DB unit).

## Spec 178 ‚Äî Stream B, B6a: store GL posting (receive + issue) (2026-06-23)

Status: **B6a SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** typecheck ¬∑ lint ¬∑ **db:test 147 files / 2664 / 0** (+1 file 198, +13 asserts) ¬∑ vitest ¬∑ `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809001900`; db:types regenerated (+2 posters). Operator chose the **full standard perpetual-inventory scheme + a new Inventory account** (AskUserQuestion). Store movements now post to the GL **at COST** (the sell/margin stays in the management P&L ‚Äî wp_profit/store_pnl). **New account 1500** `ý∏ßý∏±ý∏™ý∏îý∏∏ý∏Ñý∏áý∏Ñý∏•ý∏±ý∏á (ý∏™ýπÇý∏ïý∏£ýπåý∏´ý∏ôýπâý∏≤ý∏áý∏≤ý∏ô)` / Inventory - site store (idempotent COA seed, under Assets 1000). **post_stock_receipt_to_gl:** Dr Inventory 1500 (project) / Cr AP 2100 (supplier; null party if unsourced) at total_cost. **post_stock_issue_to_gl:** Dr WIP 1400 (project+WP) / Cr Inventory 1500 at moving-avg total_cost. Both mirror `post_purchase_to_gl` (reverse-and-repost auto-correct, SECURITY DEFINER service_role-only, balanced via `post_journal_internal`). AFTER-INSERT enqueue triggers on `stock_receipts`/`stock_issues` (append-only ‚Üí INSERT-only) feed the spec-149 async outbox; `drain_gl_posting` CREATE OR REPLACE routes the two new source tables. **No double-count:** issues are tagged `source_table='stock_issues'` (‚âÝ purchase_requests), so wp_profit's 1400-purchase-filtered materials term excludes them ‚Äî the store-issue-SELL term it already adds is the management figure; the GL holds the cost. pgTAP 198 (13): account postable ¬∑ posters service_role-only ¬∑ receipt insert enqueues ¬∑ receive Dr1500/Cr2100 balanced ¬∑ issue Dr1400(WP)/Cr1500 balanced ¬∑ issue tagged stock_issues. **TRAP (caught by db:test, 4 files/18 failures): the drainer CREATE OR REPLACE copied the STALE 4-case body from the original migration `20260743000200` ‚Äî but the LIVE drainer had 7 cases (spec-149 U5/U5b/U6 added client_billings/retention_receivables/wht_certificates) ‚Üí my replace DROPPED those 3 ‚Üí tests 85/86/87/88 ("drain posts the ‚Ä¶") went red. The recurring lesson AGAIN: CREATE OR REPLACE a routing/dispatch fn from the LIVE `pg_get_functiondef`, never an old migration. Fixed the migration to the full 9-case body + applied the corrected drainer to LIVE via `db query --file` (migration already applied ‚Üí file==DB).** **FLAGGED ‚Äî B6b: count(shrinkage) + reversal GL legs** (a reverse_stock_receipt/issue currently drops on-hand but does NOT yet post a GL flip ‚Üí the GL overstates until B6b; beta has no real store data; will add post_stock_count_to_gl + post_stock_reversal_to_gl + triggers/routing). **SPEC 178 effectively COMPLETE ‚Äî U1‚ÄìU6 + B1/B2/B3/B4/B5/B6a; only B6b (shrinkage/reversal GL legs) remains.**

## Spec 178 ‚Äî Stream B, B6b: store GL adjustments (count + reversal) ‚Äî ARC COMPLETE (2026-06-23)

Status: **B6b SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23. SPEC 178 COMPLETE.** typecheck ¬∑ lint ¬∑ **db:test 148 files / 2680 / 0** (+1 file 199, +16 asserts) ¬∑ vitest ¬∑ `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809002000`; db:types regenerated (+2 posters). Completes the store GL at COST. **post_stock_count_to_gl:** shrinkage (short) Dr COGS-materials 5100 / Cr Inventory 1500; overage Dr 1500 / Cr 5100; **zero-variance ‚Üí returns null (no entry)**. **post_stock_reversal_to_gl:** flips the original ‚Äî reverse a ý∏£ý∏±ý∏öýπÄý∏Çýπâý∏≤ ‚Üí Dr AP 2100 / Cr Inventory 1500; reverse a ýπÄý∏öý∏¥ý∏Å ‚Üí Dr Inventory 1500 / Cr WIP 1400 (the WP). Reads the reversed receipt/issue for cost + dims. AFTER-INSERT enqueue triggers on `stock_counts`/`stock_reversals`; **drain_gl_posting CREATE OR REPLACE ‚Äî this time sourced from the LIVE 9-case body (the B6a lesson) + the 2 new cases = 11 total**. pgTAP 199 (16, owner-context like file 83): posters service_role-only ¬∑ count enqueues ¬∑ shrinkage Dr5100/Cr1500 balanced ¬∑ overage Dr1500 ¬∑ zero-variance no entry ¬∑ receipt-reversal Dr2100/Cr1500 balanced ¬∑ issue-reversal Cr1400(WP) balanced. **SPEC 178 DONE ‚Äî the store is a transfer-pricing BU end to end: catalog‚Üísupply-plan‚Üíreceive‚Üíhold‚Üíissue(2 surfaces)‚Üícustody(+on-behalf)‚Üícount‚Üíreversal, all at cost on the books, with the per-item sell-rate margin layer (rate‚Üíissue-snapshot‚Üístore P&L‚Üíwp_profit fold‚Üísetter+P&L screens) + site_admin count surface. Operator to set real per-item sell rates to earn margin.**

## Spec 178 ‚Äî Stream B, B7: inventory ‚Üî GL reconciliation (2026-06-23)

Status: **B7 SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** typecheck ¬∑ lint ¬∑ **db:test 149 files / 2686 / 0** (+1 file 200, +6 asserts) ¬∑ `pnpm build`/vitest unchanged (DB-only, zero types drift); db:push applied `20260809002100`. Closes the store's money-integrity loop: the B6 GL postings gave Inventory **1500 exactly one feeder** (store movements) ‚Üí unlike multi-feeder AP 2100 / WIP 1400 it reconciles to its subledger. Extended **`gl_reconciliation()`** (the no-arg one the app/`/accounting` call; sourced from the LIVE mig-749 body + one `sub` field + one UNION arm ‚Äî the source-from-LIVE discipline; same return shape ‚Üí CREATE OR REPLACE, grants preserved) with an **`inventory_1500`** check: GL 1500 balance (dr‚àícr) must equal Œ£ `stock_on_hand.total_value`. Holds when `posting_backlog`=0 (GL posts async vs synchronous on-hand ‚Äî the existing backlog check surfaces the lag). The `/accounting` reconciliation surface auto-renders the new row (it iterates rows). pgTAP 200 (6): the row exists ¬∑ ties at 500=500 (ok=true) ¬∑ flags drift (on-hand 600 vs GL 500 ‚Üí ok=false, drift ‚àí100). pgTAP 88 unaffected (it queries recon rows by check_name, no row-count pin). **Adversarial check confirmed: store GL touches only 2100/1400/1500/5100 ‚Äî none of the OTHER single-feeder recon invariants (1210/2210/1310/2200) ‚Äî so B6 never broke reconciliation; B7 adds the one tie that 1500 makes possible.** **SPEC 178 fully complete + money-integrity-checked.**

Status: **SHIPPED to prod ‚Äî 2026-06-23.** typecheck ¬∑ lint ¬∑ **db:test 149 files / 2687 / 0** (file 186 +1 assert) ¬∑ `pnpm build` green; db:push applied `20260809002200`; db:types zero drift.
